rules.rule: DOCTOR OPD RECEPTION QUEUE (load_pending_consultations_by_clinician_id): filters by clinician (FK, via clinicianId param) AND followUp==false AND status IN {PENDING}; then post-filters in Java to keep only rows whose patientBill.status is PAID or COVERED. So a doctor sees a pending normal consultation only after its consultation bill is settled.
DOCTOR FOLLOW-UP QUEUE (load_follow_up_list_by_clinician_id): filters by clinician AND followUp==true AND status IN {PENDING,IN-PROCESS}; NO patientBill settlement filter is applied (all rows shown).
DOCTOR IN-PROCESS QUEUE (load_in_process_consultations_by_clinician_id): filters by clinician AND followUp==false AND status IN {IN-PROCESS,TRANSFERED}; no bill filter.

NURSE OUTPATIENT QUEUE (get_nurse_outpatient_list): status IN {IN-PROCESS,PENDING}, then Java-filter keep patientBill.status IN {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED,NONE}. NOT keyed to any nurse username/role — returns all matching consultations system-wide.

LAB ORDER WORKLIST is patient-class-split into 3 endpoints. Outpatient: take consultations status==IN-PROCESS, their LabTests, keep patient.type==OUTPATIENT AND test.patientBill.status IN {PAID,COVERED}. Inpatient: admissions status==IN-PROCESS, keep type==INPATIENT AND bill IN {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}. Outsider: nonConsultations status==IN-PROCESS, keep type==OUTSIDER AND bill IN {PAID,COVERED}. Result deduped to a Patient set (HashSet). INPATIENT path additionally accepts VERIFIED bills; OUT* paths do not.
RADIOLOGY ORDER WORKLIST mirrors lab exactly (outpatient/inpatient/outsider), same context status==IN-PROCESS, same patient.type split, same bill-status gates (INPATIENT also allows VERIFIED).

PROCEDURE ORDER WORKLIST has only outpatient + outsider endpoints (no dedicated inpatient procedure list endpoint found). Same status==IN-PROCESS context + type split + bill IN {PAID,COVERED}. NOTE legacy bug: getProcedureOutpatientList builds its self URI with path '.../get_lab_outpatient_list' (copy-paste), and getProcedureOutsiderList uses '.../get_lab_outsider_list'.

PHARMACY/PRESCRIPTION WORKLIST split outpatient/inpatient/outsider. Outpatient: consultations status==IN-PROCESS -> prescriptions, keep type==OUTPATIENT AND bill IN {PAID,COVERED}. Inpatient: admissions IN-PROCESS, type==INPATIENT, bill IN {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}. Outsider: nonConsultations IN-PROCESS, type==OUTSIDER, bill IN {PAID,COVERED}. A code comment notes a prior bug findAllByStatusOrFollowUp was removed.
rules.citation: api/PatientResource.java:4338-4388 (outpatient 4343-4349 incl. bug comment 4342, inpatient 4377-4383, outsider 4360-4366)

rules.rule: PENDING-TRANSFER QUEUE (get_consultation_transfers): single predicate status=='PENDING' over ALL ConsultationTransfer rows — not scoped to a clinic, clinician, or user.

Order/result worklists are NOT scoped by performing-user identity or department role anywhere; they are scoped purely by (patient.type) + (context status IN-PROCESS) + (patientBill.status). There is no per-lab/per-radiology-room assignment filter.
DOCTOR INPATIENT LIST (get_doctor_inpatient_list): admission.status IN {IN-PROCESS,STOPPED}, no other filter. NURSE INPATIENT LIST: admission.status=='IN-PROCESS' only.

per-patient order view (get_lab_tests_by_patient_id) gathers the patient's IN-PROCESS consultation+nonConsultation+admission, their LabTests, and returns only those whose patientBill.status IN {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}.

NUMBERING: NO document number is generated for a consultation or for any clinical order (LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription). Each is identified solely by its IDENTITY surrogate id. No requestConsultationNo/requestLabTestNo/... service or setNo() call exists anywhere in com.orbix.api. The planning doc's CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} and ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} schemes DO NOT EXIST in the legacy.
rules.citation: domain/Consultation.java:48-50 (id only, no no); grep for requestConsultationNo|requestLabTestNo|requestRadiologyNo|requestProcedureNo|requestPrescriptionNo across com.orbix.api = No matches found

extractions.numbering: NONE for the OPD clinical objects. Consultation, ConsultationTransfer, LabTest, Radiology, Procedure and Prescription each carry only a database IDENTITY surrogate id (@GeneratedValue(strategy=GenerationType.IDENTITY)) and NO human-facing document number. Citations: domain/Consultation.java:48-50; domain/ConsultationTransfer.java:37-39; domain/LabTest.java:44-46; domain/Radiology.java:44; (Procedure/Prescription likewise IDENTITY-only). A repository-wide grep for requestConsultationNo|requestLabTestNo|requestRadiologyNo|requestProcedureNo|requestPrescriptionNo|consultationNoService and for setNo( on these entities returned NO matches. There is NO prefix, NO zero-padding, NO yyyyMMdd, NO sequence, NO fiscal-year reset. This DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS planning-doc 05-clinical-opd.md which invents CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} and ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}. (Note: PatientCreditNote DOES get a generated no via patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo() at api/PatientResource.java:648, but that is a billing artefact, NOT a consultation/order number.)
driftVsPlanningDoc: NUMBERING — planning doc invents CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} for consultations and ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} for orders. Legacy reality: NO document number exists for consultations or any order; all are IDENTITY-id only. Cite domain/Consultation.java:48-50, domain/LabTest.java:44-46; grep for requestConsultationNo/requestLabTestNo/... = No matches.

RBAC privilege NAMES — planning doc invents CONSULTATION_START, ORDER_RAISE, PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE, PRESCRIPTION_CREATE. These DO NOT EXIST. The only ACTIVE @PreAuthorize codes touching the consultation lifecycle are the coarse PATIENT-ALL / PATIENT-CREATE / PATIENT-UPDATE family on do_consultation/switch/cancel/free (api/PatientResource.java:509,548,606,681).

RBAC coverage — planning doc implies fine-grained method security on order/prescription/diagnosis endpoints. Legacy reality: save_lab_test, save_radiology, save_procedure, save_prescription, save_working_diagnosis, save_final_diagnosis, accept/reject/hold/verify_lab_test, accept/reject/hold/verify_radiology, accept_procedure, and EVERY worklist endpoint (get_*_list, load_*_consultations_by_clinician_id, get_consultation_transfers) have NO active @PreAuthorize — they are either absent or commented out (e.g. api/PatientResource.java:572,595; the only commented privilege strings are stale 'PATIENT-A'/'PATIENT-C'/'PATIENT-U' and 'PRODUCT-CREATE'). Effective access control on the clinical workflow is authentication-only (no method-level authority check).
RBAC '177 privilege codes' — false (matches the firm memory: '177' = count of @PreAuthorize SITES, not distinct codes; and most clinical sites are commented out anyway).

ORDER MODEL — planning doc invents a polymorphic ClinicalOrder(kind=LAB_TEST|RADIOLOGY|PROCEDURE). Legacy reality: three separate entities LabTest.java / Radiology.java / Procedure.java with independent (and slightly different) state machines — radiology has no COLLECTED step, procedure verifies via add_note. Cite domain/LabTest.java:43, domain/Radiology.java:44, domain/Procedure.java:45; api/PatientResource.java:4272-4295 vs 3960-3981.

DIAGNOSIS MODEL — planning doc invents one ConsultationDiagnosis(kind=WORKING|FINAL). Legacy reality: two separate endpoints/entities WorkingDiagnosis + FinalDiagnosis (save_working_diagnosis api/PatientResource.java:1650; save_final_diagnosis api/PatientResource.java:1770).

CONSULTATION STATUS NAMES — planning doc invents clean BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED. Legacy reality (free-form Strings): PENDING, IN-PROCESS (hyphen, not underscore), TRANSFERED (single-R legacy spelling), CANCELED, SIGNED-OUT. There is no BOOKED and no COMPLETED on Consultation. Cite api/PatientResource.java:494,884-887,2808,618,699.

DOCTOR QUEUE SCOPING — planning doc implies role-based queues. Legacy reality: the doctor reception queue is scoped by clinicianId request param + followUp flag + status, with a Java-side patientBill PAID/COVERED filter; nurse/lab/radiology/pharmacy worklists are NOT scoped by the performing user at all — only by patient.type + context status==IN-PROCESS + patientBill.status. Cite api/PatientResource.java:807-827, 3639-3649, 3668-3717.
openQuestions: The doctor reception queue (load_pending_consultations_by_clinician_id) takes clinicianId as a REQUEST PARAM, not from the JWT principal, and has no @PreAuthorize. Should the modern build derive the clinician from the authenticated user, and should these queues gain authority checks? (Legacy = no enforcement; HDE/engagement-lead must approve any tightening as a change request.)

Most clinical-workflow endpoints have NO active @PreAuthorize (commented or absent). Confirm with security-architect whether the modern target re-uses the coarse PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE codes on do_consultation as the de-facto clinical authority, or introduces real per-action privileges (which would be NEW behaviour, not legacy reproduction).

Procedure has no dedicated inpatient worklist endpoint (only outpatient + outsider). Is inpatient procedure ordering intentionally absent, or surfaced via a different (admission) screen? Needs domain confirmation.

Several worklist self-URIs are copy-paste wrong (procedure lists return '.../get_lab_outpatient_list' / 'get_lab_outsider_list' URIs — api/PatientResource.java:3783,3800). These are Location-header cosmetics only; confirm no client depends on them before silently 'fixing'.

Bill-status gating is inconsistent across patient classes: INPATIENT worklists also accept patientBill.status=='VERIFIED' while OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER accept only PAID/COVERED (and nurse outpatient also accepts NONE). Confirm this asymmetry is intended business behaviour (inpatient bill verified-but-unpaid still serviceable) and must be preserved exactly.
'TRANSFERED' / 'IN-PROCESS' / 'SIGNED-OUT' legacy spellings: confirm the modern build preserves the exact wire strings for any external integration, or maps them — mapping is a change request.

confirmedClaims: Consultation.status is a free-text String (NOT an enum), @NotBlank — confirmed Consultation.java:55-56; the @GeneratedValue(IDENTITY) id and all field/JoinColumn citations (paymentType 52-53, membershipNo 54, followUp 57, patient 62-65, patientBill @OneToOne updatable=false 70-73, clinic updatable=false 77-80, clinician updatable=TRUE 85-88, visit 93-97, insurancePlan 99-102, createdBy 104-105, createdOn 106-107, createdAt 108) are all exact.

Planning-doc status names BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED do NOT exist on Consultation. Real spellings confirmed: PENDING (PatientServiceImpl.java:494), IN-PROCESS with hyphen (PatientResource.java:886,915,995), TRANSFERED single-R (PatientServiceImpl.java:2808), CANCELED single-L (PatientResource.java:618), SIGNED-OUT (PatientResource.java:699,764; PatientServiceImpl.java:1956). 'COMPLETED' at PatientServiceImpl.java:436 is on ConsultationTransfer, not Consultation — confirmed.

Booking guard chain confirmed: clinician must be active else InvalidOperationException (PatientServiceImpl.java:427-429); no Consultation in {PENDING,TRANSFERED} (447-450) then {+IN-PROCESS} (451-455); resource-layer no active Admission in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} (PatientResource.java:528-534) and patient.type must == OUTPATIENT (PatientResource.java:535-537); PENDING ConsultationTransfer forces matching destination clinic (PatientServiceImpl.java:431-435).

follow_up param must be exactly 1 or 0 else InvalidEntryException 'Invalid argument supplied. Follow up should be equal to 1 or 0' — confirmed PatientResource.java:515-522 (the extraction's quoted message is an accurate substring).

Booking PatientBill: amount=clinic.getConsultationFee(), paid=0, balance=fee, qty=1, billItem/description='Consultation', status='UNPAID' overridden to 'NONE' when followUp==true — confirmed PatientServiceImpl.java:459-469. INSURANCE re-pricing via ConsultationInsurancePlan (clinic,plan,covered=true), else 'Plan not available for this clinic', then amount=paid=plan.consultationFee, balance=0, status='COVERED'/'NONE' — confirmed PatientServiceImpl.java:597-616.

PAY-BEFORE-SERVICE open gate (PENDING->IN-PROCESS): bill must be PAID or COVERED else 'Could not open. Payment not verified.' and a ClinicianPerformance is created — confirmed PatientResource.java:884-896 (ClinicianPerformance 889-893). Follow-up variant additionally accepts NONE (the CR-20 free-bill) — confirmed PatientResource.java:910-925, NONE at 914.
Worklist load_pending_consultations_by_clinician_id filters to bill status PAID or COVERED only, hiding unpaid PENDING from the doctor — confirmed PatientResource.java:817-826 (filter at 823).

createConsultationTransfer guards: source must be IN-PROCESS (PatientServiceImpl.java:2756-2758), no other PENDING transfer for patient (2764-2766), no PENDING LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription (2769-2800), destination clinic.id != current (2804-2806), then setStatus('TRANSFERED') (2808). cancel_consultation_transfer: requires status==TRANSFERED and a PENDING transfer, sets transfer CANCELED (993) and consultation back to IN-PROCESS (995) — confirmed PatientResource.java:989-996.

cancel_consultation: only a PENDING consultation (else 'Could not cancel...') sets CANCELED (618), bill CANCELED (627); if a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists it is set REFUNDED and a PENDING PatientCreditNote raised with no from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo() (636-654); matching PatientInvoiceDetail deleted (659-672) — all confirmed.
Admission force-close: patient Consultations in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} set SIGNED-OUT, admission IN-PROCESS, ward bed OCCUPIED — confirmed PatientServiceImpl.java:1951-1962 (SIGNED-OUT at 1956).

doConsultation returns null on success (PatientServiceImpl.java:678) while controller wraps as ResponseEntity<Patient> body (PatientResource.java:540) — confirmed. clinician/clinic resolved via .get() without isPresent guard (PatientResource.java:525-526,540) — confirmed.

RBAC confirmed exactly: do_consultation/switch_to_normal_consultation/cancel_consultation/free_consultation carry @PreAuthorize(hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE')) at PatientResource.java:509,548,606,681; create_consultation_transfer (572) and get_consultation_transfers (595) have the annotation COMMENTED OUT; open_consultation (879), open_follow_up_consultation (905), switch_to_consultation_by_consultation_id (933), cancel_consultation_transfer (983), and all loaders (771,806,832,859,957) have NO @PreAuthorize. Planning-doc codes CONSULTATION_START/PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE/PRESCRIPTION_CREATE do not exist.

Polymorphic 'ClinicalOrder' does not exist — three separate entities LabTest/Radiology/Procedure plus Prescription used throughout (PatientServiceImpl.java:2759-2762; PatientResource.java:701,715,728,742). Single 'ConsultationDiagnosis' invention and 'ProviderProfile' invention are correctly flagged as non-existent.

PatientInvoice numbering for INSURANCE consultations: no='String.valueOf(Math.random())' (PatientServiceImpl.java:627) then overwritten with id.toString() (PatientServiceImpl.java:638) — confirmed; same random-then-id pattern also appears in the admission invoice path (1974,1985). No prefix/zero-pad/fiscal-year reset for any consultation-area artefact — confirmed.

switchToConsultationById sets bill COVERED (if insurancePlan!=null) else UNPAID and followUp=false (PatientResource.java:940-947) — confirmed. switchToNormalConsultation service sets bill COVERED/UNPAID, leaves followUp untouched, returns patient unchanged (PatientServiceImpl.java:684-693) — confirmed.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: The extraction states the complete Consultation status set is {PENDING, IN-PROCESS, TRANSFERED, CANCELED, SIGNED-OUT} and asserts 'There is NO COMPLETED status on Consultation anywhere' framed as the full enumeration.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: INCOMPLETE STATUS SET. A sixth status 'HELD' is genuinely set on Consultation in the save_deceased_note flow: consultation.setStatus('HELD') at PatientResource.java:5753 (when an OUTPATIENT consultation patient dies), and HELD->SIGNED-OUT on deceased-note approval at PatientResource.java:5914-5915. 'HELD' is also queried as an active-consultation status in change_type at PatientResource.java:329 alongside a further value 'STOPPED' (PatientResource.java:328). The state machine MUST include HELD (entered from save_deceased_note, exited to SIGNED-OUT on approval). 'STOPPED' is referenced in the change_type guard query (PatientResource.java:328) but no Consultation.setStatus("STOPPED") was found in PatientServiceImpl/PatientResource — flag as possibly-dead/defensive but not invented.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: The state machine and the notes enumerate the SIGNED-OUT entry points as only: free_consultation (PatientResource.java:699,764) and admission force-close (PatientServiceImpl.java:1956).
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MISSED at least TWO additional SIGNED-OUT transitions in PatientResource.java. (1) Referral-plan creation (save_referral_plan) sets consultation SIGNED-OUT at PatientResource.java:5501 and resets patient to CASH/no-plan (5504-5507), guarded by no UNPAID lab/radiology/procedure/prescription bills (5483-5499). (2) A second close path sets con SIGNED-OUT at PatientResource.java:5670 and resets patient.type='OUTPATIENT' (5673-5675), again guarded by no-UNPAID-child-bills (5634-5668). Plus the deceased-note HELD->SIGNED-OUT at 5915. These are real lifecycle terminations the consultation state machine must cover.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Booking-flow INSURANCE rules listed only the re-pricing block (PatientServiceImpl.java:597-616) and invoice creation (621-674).
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MISSED two earlier INSURANCE guards inside doConsultation BEFORE re-pricing: (a) a two-plan / pending-invoice guard — if paymentType==INSURANCE and the resolved plan id differs from the patient's current plan id and a PENDING PatientInvoice exists, throws 'Use of two or more insurance plan...' (PatientServiceImpl.java:537-549), and (b) immediately before the cover block, ALL the patient's PENDING PatientInvoices are flipped to 'APPROVED' (PatientServiceImpl.java:586-590). Also the plan is re-resolved by name and must exist else NotFoundException 'Insurance plan not found in database' (PatientServiceImpl.java:530-533). These are material booking-time side effects/guards omitted from the rules list.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Field note for Consultation states paymentType 'doConsultation only ever sets INSURANCE (555) or CASH (571), else throws (582)'.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MINOR but worth correcting: paymentType is FIRST seeded from the patient at PatientServiceImpl.java:496 'consultation.setPaymentType(p.getPaymentType())' before the INSURANCE/CASH branch overwrites it. The net observable result (final value is INSURANCE/CASH or the branch throws InvalidOperationException 'Invalid Payment type selected' at 582) is as the extraction says, but the initial assignment at 496 was not cited.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: ConsultationTransfer entity field list (citing PatientServiceImpl behaviour lines) omits the entity's own fields.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MINOR completeness gap: ConsultationTransfer.java carries a 'reason' field (ConsultationTransfer.java:43) and 'status' is @Column(nullable=false) (ConsultationTransfer.java:40-41), clinic JoinColumn is updatable=true (ConsultationTransfer.java:56) while consultation and patient JoinColumns are updatable=false (51,46). The extraction cited only behaviour lines and did not open the entity; the entity-level facts above were not stated.

missedBehaviour: HELD is a real, undocumented Consultation status: set at PatientResource.java:5753 in save_deceased_note for an outpatient consultation, and resolved HELD->SIGNED-OUT on deceased-note approval at PatientResource.java:5914-5915. The state machine must add: IN-PROCESS/active -> HELD (deceased note) and HELD -> SIGNED-OUT (deceased note approval).

Additional Consultation -> SIGNED-OUT transition on referral: save_referral_plan sets consultation SIGNED-OUT and patient back to CASH/no insurance plan, guarded by no UNPAID child bills (PatientResource.java:5483-5507, SIGNED-OUT at 5501).

Additional Consultation -> SIGNED-OUT transition (patient.type reset to OUTPATIENT) at PatientResource.java:5670, guarded by no UNPAID lab/radiology/procedure/prescription child bills (PatientResource.java:5634-5675).

doConsultation INSURANCE two-plan guard: throws 'Use of two or more insurance plan. The patient should sign of the initial patientInvoice before proceeding with another plan' when the resolved plan differs from the patient's current plan and a PENDING PatientInvoice exists (PatientServiceImpl.java:537-549).
doConsultation bulk side effect: every PENDING PatientInvoice for the patient is set to APPROVED before the cover/invoice block runs (PatientServiceImpl.java:586-590).
doConsultation plan re-resolution guard: insurance plan re-looked-up by name; absent -> NotFoundException 'Insurance plan not found in database' (PatientServiceImpl.java:530-533).

change_type active-consultation guard queries Consultation statuses {PENDING, IN-PROCESS, STOPPED, HELD} (PatientResource.java:325-333) — reveals 'STOPPED' as a referenced (queried) Consultation status value not in the extraction's enumeration; no setStatus("STOPPED") found for Consultation in the consultation files, so it is either dead/defensive or set in a flow outside this scan — should be raised as an ambiguity rather than ignored.

save_deceased_note and save_referral_plan endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize (commented out at PatientResource.java:5694) yet they drive Consultation lifecycle transitions (HELD, SIGNED-OUT) — extends the extraction's open RBAC concern to these additional unauthorized doctor/clinical actions.

doConsultation seeds a NEW Visit (sequence='SUBSEQUENT', status='PENDING') for every consultation booking and attaches it (PatientServiceImpl.java:501-512) — a side effect not surfaced in the extraction's booking rule.

confirmedClaims: ClinicalNote entity (clinical_notes table) has 8 free-text clinical fields with NO bean-validation; mainComplain is @Column(length=500), the rest default varchar; only audit columns created_by_user_id/created_on_day_id are nullable=false; consultation @OneToOne (54-57) + nonConsultation @OneToOne (59-62) + admission @ManyToOne (64-67) all nullable — verified ClinicalNote.java:40-73.

GeneralExamination (general_examinations) stores ALL vitals incl. pressure/temperature/pulseRate/weight/height/bodyMassIndex/bodySurfaceArea/saturationOxygen/respiratoryRate as plain String columns with no numeric typing or range validation; description is @Column(length=1000); same three encounter FKs (consultation @OneToOne 55-58, nonConsultation @OneToOne 60-63, admission @ManyToOne 65-68) — verified GeneralExamination.java:42-74.

Diagnosis is modelled as TWO physically separate entities/tables: WorkingDiagnosis (working_diagnosises) and FinalDiagnosis (final_diagnosises), structural twins, each with mandatory updatable=false diagnosisType FK and mandatory updatable=false patient FK, consultation OR admission link, NO nonConsultation FK, NO severity/onset/laterality/ICD field — verified WorkingDiagnosis.java:32-64 and FinalDiagnosis.java:35-67. The planning-doc single polymorphic ConsultationDiagnosis with kind=WORKING|FINAL does not exist.

DiagnosisType (diagnosis_types) has @NotBlank @Column(unique=true) on both code (41-43) and name (44-46); active defaults false on entity (48) but save() forces active=true only when id==null; no ICD validation/format mask — verified DiagnosisType.java:41-48 and DiagnosisTypeServiceImpl.java:43-48.

DiagnosisType can never be deleted via the service: deleteDiagnosisType throws InvalidOperationException because private allowDeleteDiagnosisType() unconditionally returns false — verified DiagnosisTypeServiceImpl.java:72-90 (return false at line 89).

Consultation diagnosis save paths enforce a duplicate guard via existsByConsultationAndDiagnosisType throwing 'Duplicate Diagnosis Types is not allowed' (saveWorkingDiagnosis PatientResource.java:1662-1664; saveFinalDiagnosis PatientResource.java:1782-1784), but admission diagnosis paths (saveAdmissionWorkingDiagnosis 1685-1707; saveAdmissionFinalDiagnosis 1804-1825) have NO duplicate guard — asymmetry confirmed. Repository methods confirmed in WorkingDiagnosisRepository.java:33 and FinalDiagnosisRepository.java:27.

saveCG (PatientResource.java:1456-1606) enforces exactly-one-encounter via pairwise/triple InvalidOperationException 'Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously' (1469-1481) and NotFoundException 'No Admission or Consultation found' (1519-1521); persistence is UPSERT (overwrite last row via findAllBy...) for consultation/non-consultation and append-capable for admission — verified 1484-1598.

load_clinical_note/general_examination loaders AUTO-CREATE and persist an empty row as a side effect of a GET, returning HTTP 201 (ResponseEntity.created): clinical note by consultation 1238-1246, general exam by consultation 1268-1276, clinical note by admission 1373-1381, general exam by non-consultation 1403-1411, general exam by admission 1440-1448 — all cited ranges verified.

PatientVital staging entity (patient_vitals) has status default 'EMPTY' (PatientVital.java:45); load_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id auto-creates with status='EMPTY' (PatientResource.java:1298-1307); request_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id requires status=='SUBMITTED' else throws 'Vitals already requested or not submitted', copies fields into a transient (non-persisted) GeneralExamination, and flips PatientVital to 'ARCHIVED' then saves it — verified PatientResource.java:1321-1340.

delete_working_diagnosis (1917-1922) and delete_final_diagnosis (1924-1929) are GET endpoints performing unguarded deleteById(id) with no existence check, no @PreAuthorize, no soft-delete, no audit — verified.

send_clinical_note_and_general_examination_to_history (1608-1648) is ADMISSION-ONLY: throws 'Not Allowed for consultations' if consultation resolves (1616-1618), requires admission, and unconditionally inserts a NEW blank ClinicalNote and NEW blank GeneralExamination via saveAndFlush — verified.

RBAC: the ONLY active @PreAuthorize touching this area is diagnosis_types/save = hasAnyAuthority('ADMIN-ACCESS') at DiagnosisTypeResource.java:74 (mapping at 73). All clinical-note / general-examination / working & final diagnosis save/load/delete endpoints in PatientResource (1224-1929) have NO active @PreAuthorize; the only nearby annotations are commented-out (//@PreAuthorize PRODUCT-CREATE at 1932, PATIENT-A/C/U at 1975). Planning-doc privilege names CONSULTATION_START/PRESCRIPTION_CREATE/PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE do not exist.

EARLY-DISCOVERY confirmed independently: grep @Audited over com.orbix.api returns ZERO files (no Envers audit baseline); grep fingerprint|deviceId|device_id|deviceBinding|X-Device returns ZERO files (no device-binding); grep \bicd\b returns ZERO files (no ICD coding). The createdBy/createdOn/createdAt columns are hand-rolled stamps, not Envers revisions.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Extraction rule for auto-create loaders states 'Same auto-create for non-consultation and admission loaders' for ClinicalNote.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Overstated for ClinicalNote. Only TWO clinical-note loaders exist: load_clinical_note_by_consultation_id (PatientResource.java:1224) and load_clinical_note_by_admission_id (PatientResource.java:1352). There is NO load_clinical_note_by_non_consultation_id endpoint (grep load_clinical_note_by returns only consultation and admission). The non-consultation loader exists ONLY for GeneralExamination (load_general_examination_by_non_consultation_id, 1389). The cited line ranges themselves are valid, but the prose generalizes a non-consultation note loader that does not exist.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Extraction's PatientVital note cites the request flow as PatientResource.java:1317-1346 / 1321-1346.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Range is broadly correct but the entry point at line 1320-1321 calls consultationRepository.findById(id) and immediately dereferences c.get() WITHOUT a presence check, so a non-existent consultation id yields a raw NoSuchElementException (HTTP 500), not the clean NotFoundException used elsewhere. The 'Vitals already requested or not submitted' throw is precisely at 1323-1324; the field-copy block 1327-1338; ARCHIVED set 1339; save 1340; 'Vital Signs not found' NotFoundException at 1344 fires only when a consultation exists but has no PatientVital. The extraction did not surface the unchecked c.get() defect.

missedBehaviour: MISSED: requestPatientVitalByConsultationId dereferences consultationRepository.findById(id).get() with no isPresent() guard, throwing a raw NoSuchElementException (HTTP 500) for a missing consultation id — a behavioural divergence from the clean NotFoundException pattern used by sibling loaders. PatientResource.java:1320-1321.

MISSED: A THIRD final-diagnosis-by-consultation loader exists, load_consultation_final_diagnosis (PatientResource.java:1858-1885), duplicating load_final_diagnosis (1828-1856) but OMITTING model.setDoctor(...) — so this endpoint returns FinalDiagnosisModel rows with a null doctor field while the twin endpoint populates it. This duplicate-endpoint asymmetry was not documented. PatientResource.java:1858-1885 vs 1828-1856.

MISSED: saveCG reads cg.getClinicalNote().getConsultation().getId(), getNonConsultation().getId(), and getAdmission().getId() at PatientResource.java:1461-1463; the mutual-exclusion logic therefore REQUIRES the client to always send all three nested encounter objects each carrying a non-null id (a sentinel like 0 for the absent ones). A payload missing any of the three nested objects produces a NullPointerException before any guard runs. This payload-shape precondition for the exactly-one-encounter rule was not stated. PatientResource.java:1461-1463.

MISSED: load_clinical_note_by_admission_id (1360-1364) and load_general_examination_by_admission_id (1426-1430) select the 'current' row by iterating findAllByAdmission with NO ORDER BY and taking the last element of the iteration — non-deterministic w.r.t. DB return order. The extraction's open question notes this but the rule body asserting admission append-behaviour does not flag that the returned 'current' note/exam is order-dependent. PatientResource.java:1360-1364, 1426-1430.

confirmedClaims: THREE separate entities (LabTest, Radiology, Procedure) with separate tables lab_tests/radiologies/procedures, NOT a polymorphic ClinicalOrder — confirmed at LabTest.java:42, Radiology.java:41, Procedure.java:39. Each is an @Entity with IDENTITY PK (LabTest.java:44-46, Radiology.java:43-45, Procedure.java:41-43).

'status' is a free-text String column on all three (LabTest.java:55, Radiology.java:51, Procedure.java:54), NOT a JPA enum. No BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED value exists anywhere (planning-doc invention).

Field-casing divergence confirmed exactly: LabTest uses camelCase setters acceptedBy/rejectedBy/etc (LabTest.java:118,130,124,137,143) while Radiology and Procedure use lowercase acceptedby/rejectedby/orderedby/etc (Radiology.java:108,115,121,128,134,140; Procedure.java:117,123,129,135,142). The DB column names (ordered_by_user_id etc.) are identical across all three via @Column.

Radiology has a legacy Byte[] attachment field (Radiology.java:50) in addition to the RadiologyAttachment child entity; LabTest does not. Procedure result narrative lives in the 'note' field @Column(length=10000) (Procedure.java:44-45).

LabTest state machine confirmed: create->PENDING (PatientServiceImpl.java:819); accept PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED clearing reject fields (PatientResource.java:3896-3911); reject PENDING|ACCEPTED->REJECTED clearing accept fields (3922-3936); collect ACCEPTED->COLLECTED (3947-3957); verify COLLECTED->VERIFIED writing result/level/range/unit (3968-3982); hold ACCEPTED->PENDING setting held* fields (4013-4023); delete PENDING-only (2917-2918).

Radiology state machine confirmed: create->PENDING (PatientServiceImpl.java:1062); accept (4208-4223); reject (4234-4248); hold ACCEPTED->PENDING (4259-4269); verify from ACCEPTED->VERIFIED writing result+attachment (4280-4294); collectRadiology mapped to suspicious URL '/patients/collect_radiology111' moving ACCEPTED->COLLECTED (4317-4335). The active verify path ignores COLLECTED, so lab and radiology pipelines genuinely diverge.

Procedure state machine confirmed: create->PENDING (PatientServiceImpl.java:1313); accept PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED (4034-4049); update_procedure edits note only when ACCEPTED, no status change (4060-4066); add_note finalizes to VERIFIED with non-empty-note check AND bill-status gate (3405-3415); delete PENDING-only (3478-3480). NO APPROVED state, NO approve endpoint, NO reject_procedure endpoint, NO hold_procedure endpoint exist (grep confirmed zero matches) — planning-doc M14 'approve' step is fabricated. ProcedureModel.java:28-57 has no approved/collected field.

Procedure settlement gate IS explicit in addProcedureNote: requires patientBill.status in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED}, else throws 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified' (PatientResource.java:3408-3414).

Lab/radiology accept/verify endpoints do NOT check the bill — they only check the order's own status. Settlement is enforced upstream at worklist queries: lab outpatient/outsider {PAID,COVERED} (3676,3710), lab inpatient adds VERIFIED (3693), and at by_patient_id queries {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} (3820 lab, 4134 radiology, 4607 procedure). add_report gated {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} (3389 lab).

Duplicate-order prevention via existsByConsultationAnd<Type>Type / existsByNonConsultationAnd<Type>Type (PatientResource.java:1948-1957 lab, 1991-2000 radiology, 2067-2076 procedure). Patient-type gate: consultation requires OUTPATIENT, nonConsultation requires OUTSIDER (1945,1952 / 1988,1995 / 2064,2071).

Order-creation parent rules confirmed: exactly one of consultation/nonConsultation/admission (PatientServiceImpl.java:767-781); nonConsultation PENDING->IN-PROCESS or already IN-PROCESS else rejected (790-797); admission PENDING rejected as 'not verified', must be IN-PROCESS (804-810). Each creates a PatientBill qty1 status UNPAID amount=Type.getPrice() (821-828 lab); INSURANCE/admission with covered <Type>InsurancePlan -> COVERED + PENDING PatientInvoice/Detail (837-911); admission without covered plan -> VERIFIED (912-918).

Attachment limits confirmed: max 5 per LabTest and per Radiology (PatientServiceImpl.java:2828, 2927); lab attach only when status==COLLECTED (2832), radiology attach only when status==ACCEPTED (2931) — genuinely different gate states.

saveLabTestResult requires COLLECTED (3993), saveRadiologyResult requires ACCEPTED (4305) — edit-without-status-change paths confirmed. Reject-comment endpoints persist only when status==REJECTED (PatientResource.java:2026 radiology, 2042 lab).

PatientInvoice.no seeded with String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with id.toString() (PatientServiceImpl.java:857,878-879) — confirmed; cancellation PatientCreditNote.no from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo() (PatientResource.java:2932,3438) — belongs to billing context. Clinical orders themselves have no human-facing number (IDENTITY PK only).

No @Audited / Hibernate Envers annotation on any of the three entities — confirmed by full reads of LabTest.java (1-154), Radiology.java (1-150), Procedure.java (1-147); auditing is ad-hoc *By/*On/*At columns. Downstream agents must not assume an Envers baseline. No device-fingerprint/device-binding logic — actor identified solely via userService.getUserId(request)/getUser(request) at every transition (e.g. 3900,3926,3951,3976,4017,4212,4288,4329).

Clinical-order endpoints carry NO active @PreAuthorize: save_lab_test (1932) and save_radiology (1975) annotations are commented out; accept/reject/collect/verify/hold (3888,3914,3939,3960,4005,4026,4200,4226,4251,4272) have none; attachment endpoints commented (5942,5961,6011,6075,6094,6144). Planning-doc privileges CONSULTATION_START/PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE/PRESCRIPTION_CREATE do not appear anywhere in this resource. (Note: other NON-clinical-order endpoints in PatientResource DO have active @PreAuthorize at 289,379,399,509,548,606,681 — patient CRUD, correctly out of clinical-orders scope.)

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Rule: 'Lab/radiology attachment viewing (download) is gated on status==VERIFIED.' citing PatientResource.java:6021 (lab) and 6154 (radiology).
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MATERIALLY WRONG — both mischaracterized and polarity-inverted. Lines 6021 and 6154 are inside deleteLabTestAttachment (6010-6025) and deleteRadiologyAttachment (6143-6158), and the check BLOCKS DELETION when status==VERIFIED ('Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' / '...Radiology already verified'). It is a delete-guard, not a view/download gate, and VERIFIED disables the action rather than enabling it. The actual download endpoints download_lab_test_attachment (PatientResource.java:5960-6007) and download_radiology_attachment (6093-6140) have NO status gate at all — they read the file unconditionally. Correct rule: an attachment may NOT be deleted once its parent order is VERIFIED; downloads are ungated.
refutedOrCorrected.citation: PatientResource.java:6021 (lab delete-guard), 6154 (radiology delete-guard), 5960-6007 (lab download, no gate), 6093-6140 (radiology download, no gate)

refutedOrCorrected.claim: State-machine entry deleteProcedure: 'guard: status must be PENDING' with no credit-note effect noted (PatientResource.java:3478-3480), inconsistent with the deleteLabTest/deleteRadiology entries which DO note the credit note.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Incomplete, not wrong. The PENDING-only guard is correct (3478-3479), but the extraction OMITTED that deleteProcedure ALSO issues a PatientCreditNote when a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists (PatientResource.java:3486-3512), identical to delete_lab_test (2925-2938) and delete_radiology (3431-3444). All three delete paths share the same credit-note-on-RECEIVED-payment behaviour; the extraction documented it for lab/radiology but silently dropped it for procedure. Also deletes any PatientInvoiceDetail and (if it empties the invoice) the PatientInvoice, and the orphaned PatientBill/PatientPaymentDetail (3513-end).

refutedOrCorrected.claim: deleteLabTest cited at 'PatientResource.java:2917-2961'.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Off-by-a-few but non-material. The @PostMapping('/patients/delete_lab_test') is at line 2912 and the method signature at 2913; the PENDING guard is at 2917-2918 and the method ends at 2965. The cited 2917-2961 range lands inside the method body and the substantive claims (PENDING-only, credit note on RECEIVED) are correct — citation start should be 2912.

missedBehaviour: Procedure worklist/by-patient query divergence: get_procedures_by_patient_id queries only consultations and nonConsultations (procedureRepository.findAllByConsultationInOrNonConsultationIn, PatientResource.java:4603), OMITTING admissions — whereas get_lab_tests_by_patient_id (3815) and get_radiologies_by_patient_id (4130) use findAllByConsultationInOrNonConsultationInOrAdmissionIn (include admissions). Consequence: an admission-scoped Procedure is created (PatientServiceImpl.java:1305-1313 supports admission) but never surfaces in the by-patient procedure list. Flag as ambiguity (intentional vs bug).

ProcedureModel carries a 'held' field (ProcedureModel.java:53) and the Procedure entity has held_by/held_on/held_at columns (Procedure.java:128-132), yet NO hold-procedure endpoint exists — reinforces the extraction's vestigial-columns open question; the held column is also exposed through the read model, not merely dead at the entity level.

On reject, LabTest clears accepted* fields (PatientResource.java:3930-3932) and on accept clears rejected* fields incl. rejectComment='' (3904-3907); Radiology accept clears reject fields (4216-4219) and reject clears accept fields (4242-4244) — but Radiology reject does NOT clear/blank rejectComment (no setRejectComment in rejectRadiology), unlike LabTest/Procedure accept which set rejectComment(''). Minor field-handling asymmetry worth noting for data-migration.

deleteRadiology has no Optional.isPresent() guard before r.get() at PatientResource.java:3422-3423 (and deleteLabTest at 2916-2917, deleteProcedure at 3477-3478) — a non-existent id triggers a raw NoSuchElementException (HTTP 500) rather than the project's NotFoundException(404). Behavioural edge case for qa-test-engineer; the extraction did not note the missing not-found handling on the delete endpoints.

issued=0 / balance=qty at create verified at PatientServiceImpl.java:1693-1694.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: issueMedicine all-or-nothing rule cited as 'issued==qty (PatientResource.java:3224-3226)'.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: The issued!=qty throw is at lines 3224-3225 ('You can only issue the prescribed qty'); line 3226 is blank. More importantly there is a SECOND, EARLIER issue-value guard the extraction OMITTED entirely (see missedBehaviour): line 3221 throws 'Invalid issue value' when balance>issued OR issued<=0. The all-or-nothing characterization is correct but the citation range and the full guard set are incomplete.

missedBehaviour: issueMedicine has a SECOND issue-value guard the extraction omitted entirely: PatientResource.java:3221 throws InvalidOperationException 'Invalid issue value in '+medicine.name when (balance > issued) OR (issued <= 0). This runs BEFORE the issued==qty check (3224-3225). The rebuild must reproduce BOTH guards: 3221 (reject zero/negative or below-balance issue) and 3224 (reject any value != full qty). Net effect with both: only issued==qty (==balance, since balance==qty pre-dispense) is accepted.

save_prescription CONTROLLER patient-type guards omitted: consultation branch requires patient.getType()=='OUTPATIENT' else 'Patient is not an outpatient...' (PatientResource.java:2106-2107); nonConsultation branch requires 'OUTSIDER' else 'Patient is not an outsider...' (2113-2114). These run before the duplicate-drug check and gate which prescribing context is legal per patient type; the extraction documented the duplicate guard but not these type guards.

The chart is only persisted for admissions in IN-PROCESS status with a nurse.

Latent legacy quirk in deletePrescription empty-invoice cleanup: the loop counter uses 'j = j++' (PatientResource.java:3589), a no-op self-assignment, so j stays 0 regardless of how many PatientInvoiceDetails remain; consequently the parent PatientInvoice is ALWAYS deleted whenever a detail was removed (3591-3592), even if other details still reference it. Tangential to prescriptions-alerts but a real data-integrity bug the rebuild must decide to preserve or fix via change request.

savePrescription has redundant/overlapping one-of guards (PatientServiceImpl.java:1489-1503): the c&&nc&&a and c&&nc&&!a branches both throw the same 'more than two properties' message; behaviourally correct but the extraction's '1489-1503' range is right while not noting the c.isPresent() admission-clinician override (1504-1508 sets clinician from consultation, while admission branch 1523-1526 overrides clinician from the request user) - a clinician-attribution difference between OPD-consultation and admission prescribing worth flagging.

confirmedClaims: ConsultationTransfer entity has NO @NotBlank on status (the import is absent); status is only DB-NOT-NULL via @Column(nullable=false) at domain/ConsultationTransfer.java:40-41. Verified.

ConsultationTransfer has NO clinician field, NO @Version, NO source-clinic/source-clinician/acceptedBy/acceptedOn/completedAt fields. The full entity (domain/ConsultationTransfer.java:35-65) carries only id, status, reason, patient, consultation, clinic (destination, updatable=true), createdBy, createdOn, createdAt. Verified.
clinic relation on ConsultationTransfer is updatable=true (the only updatable relation); patient and consultation are updatable=false (domain/ConsultationTransfer.java:46,51,56). Verified.

Consultation.status carries @NotBlank (domain/Consultation.java:52->55-56 actually @NotBlank at line 55 above status at 56); clinic is updatable=false (line 78), clinician is updatable=true (line 86); followUp default false (line 57). Verified. NOTE: insurancePlan and paymentType also present but not transfer-relevant.
RAISE precondition: source consultation status MUST equal 'IN-PROCESS' else InvalidOperationException 'Can not transfer. Not an active consultation' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2756-2757). Verified.
One-pending-transfer-per-patient guard via findAllByPatientAndStatus(patient,'PENDING') non-empty -> reject (PatientServiceImpl.java:2764-2767). Verified.

Four independent no-open-work guards, each null-safe and tripping only on literal 'PENDING': LabTest (2769-2776), Radiology (2777-2784), Procedure (2785-2792), Prescription (2793-2800). Orders in any non-PENDING status do not block. Verified. Fetches at 2759-2762.

Same-clinic guard uses '==' on Long ids: if(transfer.getClinic().getId() == con.getClinic().getId()) (PatientServiceImpl.java:2804-2806). Reference-equality latent bug for ids outside Integer cache (-128..127) is real. Verified.

On RAISE source consultation set to 'TRANSFERED' and saved (2808-2809) BEFORE transfer set 'PENDING' and saved (2811-2819), all in one createConsultationTransfer @Transactional method. Verified. setStatus('TRANSFERED') occurs ONLY at line 2808 in the whole service.

Implicit ACCEPT/completion inside doConsultation: if a PENDING transfer exists for the patient it must point at the same clinic being sent to, else reject naming the required clinic; if match, transfer set COMPLETED (PatientServiceImpl.java:431-438). Verified.

Destination clinician chosen at ACCEPT time (cn param to doConsultation, stored on NEW consultation at line 490); only validation is cn.isActive() (427-429). NO clinic-clinician affiliation check exists. Verified as NOT FOUND.
CANCEL reverts source consultation to IN-PROCESS (not PENDING) and sets transfer CANCELED, without deleting the row (PatientResource.java:993,995). Verified.

Transfer status set is PENDING / COMPLETED / CANCELED (American single-L 'CANCELED', single-R 'TRANSFERED'). Verified: PENDING PatientServiceImpl.java:2811; COMPLETED 436; CANCELED PatientResource.java:993; TRANSFERED 2808.
No human-facing number/sequence for ConsultationTransfer; only DB IDENTITY id (domain/ConsultationTransfer.java:37-39). No prefix/padding/fiscal reset. Verified.

create_consultation_transfer @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:572); get_consultation_transfers @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (595); cancel_consultation_transfer has NO @PreAuthorize at all (983-984). All three method-level UNGUARDED. Verified. do_consultation ACCEPT path IS guarded hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE') (509).

Planning-doc privilege codes CONSULTATION_START / PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE / PRESCRIPTION_CREATE do not exist; legacy uses PATIENT-ALL/PATIENT-CREATE/PATIENT-UPDATE (and commented PATIENT-A/-C/-U). Verified.
Legacy uses THREE separate order entities LabTest/Radiology/Procedure plus Prescription, not a polymorphic ClinicalOrder (PatientServiceImpl.java:2759-2762). Verified.

CONFLICT is real: doConsultation marks transfer COMPLETED at 436-437, then the guard at 444-449 checks for any consultation in {PENDING,TRANSFERED}; the source consultation is still TRANSFERED (never moved out in the completion branch), so this guard throws 'Patient has pending or held consultation...'. The happy-path accept is unreachable unless the TRANSFERED consultation is signed out first. Verified.
open_consultation guard: status=='PENDING' AND bill status PAID or COVERED -> IN-PROCESS (PatientResource.java:884-886); follow-up variant also allows bill status NONE (914). Verified.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: free_consultation no-ops on a TRANSFERED consultation: 'the method only frees IN-PROCESS and no-ops on TRANSFERED'; 'the outer if at 687 is true so the IN-PROCESS branch is not entered and nothing happens'; 'the error text claims TRANSFERED is freeable but the code does not free it.'
refutedOrCorrected.correction: WRONG and materially so. The outer guard is if(!status.equals('TRANSFERED')) at line 687. When status IS 'TRANSFERED', the negated condition is FALSE, so the ENTIRE inner block (688-758) is SKIPPED, and control falls through to lines 763-765 which run UNCONDITIONALLY: consultation.setStatus('SIGNED-OUT'); save. Therefore a TRANSFERED consultation IS freed (signed out), it just skips the order-bill-cancellation loops. The extraction inverted the control flow. This also RESOLVES the lifecycle question: free_consultation is the operational step that signs out the TRANSFERED source consultation, so that the subsequent doConsultation re-send no longer trips the {PENDING,TRANSFERED} guard at 444-449. The error message at 757 only fires for statuses that are NEITHER TRANSFERED NOR IN-PROCESS (e.g. PENDING/SIGNED-OUT/CANCELED). Message and behaviour are actually consistent.
refutedOrCorrected.citation: PatientResource.java:687 (outer guard), 688-758 (skipped-when-TRANSFERED inner block), 756-758 (else throw, reachable only for non-TRANSFERED non-IN-PROCESS), 763-765 (unconditional setStatus('SIGNED-OUT'))

refutedOrCorrected.claim: CANCEL is guarded so that 'if the found transfer is not PENDING it throws Could not cancel transfer'.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Partially misleading. The transfer is fetched via findByConsultationAndStatus(c.get(), 'PENDING') at line 990, so conTra is ALWAYS PENDING when present. The inner re-check at 992 and its else-throw 'Could not cancel transfer' at 998 are therefore DEAD CODE / unreachable. The accurate statement is: cancel only fires when consultation.status=='TRANSFERED' (989) AND a PENDING transfer exists for that consultation (990-991); otherwise it silently no-ops and returns the consultation unchanged. The throw at 998 can never execute given the query at 990.
refutedOrCorrected.citation: PatientResource.java:989-1001 (esp. 990 query is status-scoped to PENDING; 992 redundant check; 998 unreachable throw)

refutedOrCorrected.claim: stateMachine entry: 'Consultation = PENDING -> Consultation = IN-PROCESS' precondition citation says 'follow-up variant also allows bill status NONE (PatientResource.java:914)' and treats open_follow_up_consultation as equivalent.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Citation is correct but the extraction omits a behavioural divergence: open_consultation THROWS 'Could not open. Not a pending consultation.' when status != PENDING (PatientResource.java:899), whereas open_follow_up_consultation does NOT throw on a non-PENDING status -- if status != PENDING it simply falls through and returns true (no setStatus) without error (PatientResource.java:909-928, note the missing else branch after 913 block). Minor but a real asymmetry worth recording.
refutedOrCorrected.citation: PatientResource.java:898-900 (open_consultation else-throw) vs PatientResource.java:913-928 (open_follow_up_consultation has no else-throw on non-PENDING; method returns true at 928 regardless)

missedBehaviour: The do_consultation resource endpoint (the ACCEPT/send path) carries TWO additional pre-guards the extraction did not capture, applied BEFORE doConsultation runs: (a) the patient must have NO Admission in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} else 'Could not process consultation, the patient has an active admission' (PatientResource.java:531-534); (b) the patient.type must equal 'OUTPATIENT' else 'Please change patient type to OUTPATIENT to continue with operation' (PatientResource.java:535-537). These gate every transfer-accept.

get_consultation_transfers returns ALL pending transfers system-wide via findAllByStatus('PENDING') -- it is NOT scoped by patient, clinic, or clinician (PatientResource.java:599). The extraction did not document the query scope, which matters for the destination-clinic worklist UI.

do_consultation derives the destination clinic and clinician by NAME lookup, not id: clinicRepository.findByName(clinic_name) and clinicianRepository.findByNickname(clinician_name) (PatientResource.java:525-526). The transfer-clinic match at PatientServiceImpl.java:433 then compares c.getId() (resolved-by-name clinic) to conTrans.getClinic().getId() using '==' on Long -- same reference-equality latent-bug pattern as the raise-time check at 2804, and the extraction flagged it only for raise, not for the accept-side comparison at 433.

createConsultationTransfer reassigns transfer.setConsultation(con) at PatientServiceImpl.java:2812 after saving the consultation (the inline '///??' authoring comment), and transfer.setPatient(con.getPatient()) at 2813 -- so the patient and consultation on the persisted transfer are taken from the freshly-saved source consultation, ignoring whatever patient the client may have supplied in the request body. The resource layer at PatientResource.java:587-588 also overwrites transfer.consultation and transfer.clinic from DB lookups before the service runs. The extraction noted patient is set from consultation but did not note the consultation re-set or the resource-layer overwrite of client-supplied fields.

After a transfer is COMPLETED (PatientServiceImpl.java:436), the source consultation is left in TRANSFERED status permanently within doConsultation; there is no setStatus moving it to a terminal state in the completion branch. Combined with the corrected free_consultation finding, the only paths OUT of TRANSFERED are: free_consultation -> SIGNED-OUT (PatientResource.java:764) and cancel_consultation_transfer -> IN-PROCESS (PatientResource.java:995). The extraction listed these as open questions but did not connect free_consultation (correctly) as the resolution.

findByPatientAndStatus on ConsultationTransfer returns Optional (ConsultationTransferRepository.java:39); if two PENDING transfers ever coexisted for one patient this would throw NonUniqueResultException at doConsultation:431. The raise guard at 2764-2767 prevents this in practice, but the Optional-vs-List asymmetry between raise (findAllByPatientAndStatus) and accept (findByPatientAndStatus) is an implicit single-pending-transfer invariant the extraction did not surface.

confirmedClaims: DeceasedNote entity is faithfully described: @Id IDENTITY (DeceasedNote.java:37-39), patientSummary/causeOfDeath plain String with no JPA constraint (DeceasedNote.java:40-41), date/time @Column(date_/time_) (DeceasedNote.java:43-46), status default PENDING (DeceasedNote.java:48), admission/consultation @OneToOne optional updatable=false OnDelete NO_ACTION (DeceasedNote.java:50-58), patient @OneToOne optional=false (DeceasedNote.java:60-63), createdBy/createdOn nullable=false updatable=false, approvedBy/On nullable=true updatable=true (DeceasedNote.java:65-75). VERIFIED.

ReferralPlan entity faithfully described: externalMedicalProvider is @ManyToOne optional=false (ReferralPlan.java:49-52); admission/consultation @ManyToOne optional=true updatable=false (ReferralPlan.java:54-62); status default PENDING (ReferralPlan.java:47); the seven clinical String narrative fields exist (ReferralPlan.java:39-45). VERIFIED.

ExternalMedicalProvider: @NotBlank unique code and name (ExternalMedicalProvider.java:33-38), active boolean default false (ExternalMedicalProvider.java:44); save_referral_plan looks it up by id and 404s if absent but never checks active (PatientResource.java:5398-5401). VERIFIED.

Patient.type is String @NotBlank (Patient.java:63-64); there is NO Patient.deceased boolean field anywhere in Patient.java (grep for deceased/boolean death returned no matches). Death is recorded only by setType("DECEASED") at PatientResource.java:5901 and 5920, the ONLY two DECEASED literals in the whole tree. VERIFIED.

No closure() method, no kind discriminator, and no PATIENT_IS_DECEASED constant exist anywhere in com.orbix.api (grep for closure|kind|PATIENT_IS_DECEASED returned zero files). The planning-doc inventions are confirmed as inventions. VERIFIED.

Death save state transitions: OPD consultation set to HELD unconditionally with no status guard (PatientResource.java:5753); inpatient admission null->IN-PROCESS->HELD with ward bed freed to EMPTY (PatientResource.java:5720-5731); new note always saved PENDING (PatientResource.java:5766); existing note reused without re-applying HELD (PatientResource.java:5715-5717, 5748-5750). VERIFIED.

Death approval (get_deceased_summary) only advances state when linked admission/consultation status == HELD (PatientResource.java:5891, 5914); on success consultation/admission -> SIGNED-OUT, Patient.type -> DECEASED, note -> APPROVED, admission ward bed -> EMPTY (PatientResource.java:5892-5907, 5915-5926). VERIFIED.

Bill-gate inconsistency is REAL: death/inpatient gates reject UNPAID OR VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:5857, 5876, 5598), whereas OPD-referral save/summary gates reject ONLY UNPAID across LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription (PatientResource.java:5468, 5477, 5486, 5495, 5637, 5646, 5655, 5664). VERIFIED.

OPD referral save clears insurance: patient.setPaymentType("CASH") and setInsurancePlan(null), and sets consultation SIGNED-OUT at save time before approval (PatientResource.java:5501-5507). Inpatient referral save sets admission STOPPED (PatientResource.java:5451). VERIFIED.

Referral approval admission branch guards on status==STOPPED, stamps dischargedBy/On/At from the plan creator's createdBy/createdOn/createdAt, frees ward bed, sets Patient.type=OUTPATIENT (PatientResource.java:5611-5631); Admission has real fields dischargedBy/dischargedOn/dischargedAt (Admission.java:80-83). VERIFIED.

approvedBy/approvedOn on both DeceasedNote and ReferralPlan are populated from createdBy/createdOn (the creator), not the approver; only approvedAt uses dayService.getTimeStamp() (PatientResource.java:5905-5907, 5924-5926, 5628-5630, 5679-5681). VERIFIED.
The 'Could not get discharge summary' message inside the referral endpoint admission branch is a real copy-paste artifact (PatientResource.java:5599). VERIFIED.

48-hour ARCHIVED sweep for DeceasedNote (UpdatePatient.java:572-584) and ReferralPlan (UpdatePatient.java:586-598), archiving immediately when approvedAt is null, using findAllByStatus("APPROVED"). VERIFIED.
load_deceased_list and load_referral_list return only PENDING+APPROVED via findAllByStatusIn, hiding ARCHIVED (PatientResource.java:5826-5830, 5567-5571). VERIFIED.

RBAC: do_consultation @PreAuthorize hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE') (PatientResource.java:509) and switch_to_normal_consultation (PatientResource.java:548); ALL death/referral endpoints have their @PreAuthorize commented out (PatientResource.java:5393, 5537, 5563, 5579, 5694, 5778, 5822, 5838). The booking gate rejects non-OUTPATIENT with the generic 'Please change patient type to OUTPATIENT to continue with operation' (PatientResource.java:535-536), never a PATIENT_IS_DECEASED error. VERIFIED.
doConsultation never inspects patient.type; its guards are clinician-active, consultation-transfer, and pending/active consultation checks only (PatientServiceImpl.java:425-455). VERIFIED.

get_referral_summary consultation branch re-sets con.status=SIGNED-OUT unconditionally with no status guard, unlike the admission branch which guards status==STOPPED (PatientResource.java:5670 vs 5611). The open question flagging this asymmetry is valid. VERIFIED.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Patient.type observed value set across the codebase: OUTPATIENT, INPATIENT, OUTSIDER, DECEASED (Patient.type field note).
refutedOrCorrected.correction: Incomplete enumeration. A tree-wide grep for setType( shows the value set also includes at least NONE (PatientServiceImpl.java:3282 generalPatient.setType("NONE")). So the writable type domain is OUTPATIENT/INPATIENT/OUTSIDER/DECEASED/NONE, not the four listed. This is a minor omission, not a behavioural error, but the extraction claimed to enumerate 'the observed value set across the codebase' and missed NONE.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: ARCHIVED sweep for both DeceasedNote and ReferralPlan runs in UpdatePatient.java at >=48h after approval, or immediately if approvedAt is null (UpdatePatient.java:572-594).
refutedOrCorrected.correction: The line range and 48h/immediate logic are exactly correct, BUT the extraction omits a behaviourally critical fact: the entire sweep block is wrapped in try{...}catch(Exception e){} with an empty catch (UpdatePatient.java:600). Any exception during the archival loop is SILENTLY swallowed, so a single bad record can abort the remainder of the sweep with no log and no error. For 'exact process' fidelity this swallow must be reproduced/flagged.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Death approval is blocked if ANY related PatientInvoiceDetail bill is UNPAID/VERIFIED; on success all related PatientInvoices set APPROVED (citing 5856-5868 admission, 5875-5887 consultation).
refutedOrCorrected.correction: The gate logic and APPROVED writes are correct, but the extraction MISSED a null-ordering subtlety in the actual control flow. patientInvoiceRepository.findAllByAdmission(adm) is invoked at line 5851 BEFORE the adm!=null check at 5852, and the APPROVED-write loop at 5865-5868 runs OUTSIDE the adm!=null guard; symmetrically findAllByConsultation(con) at 5870 and the APPROVED loop at 5884-5887 run for whichever side is null. Thus for a consultation-only note findAllByAdmission(null) executes (and vice versa). This is part of exact process and should be documented; the extraction's state-machine summary glossed over it.

missedBehaviour: The day-rollover archival sweep is wrapped in an empty catch-all: try{...}catch(Exception e){} at UpdatePatient.java:600 silently swallows any exception thrown while archiving DischargePlan/DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan, so one malformed record can halt the rest of the sweep with no error surfaced (UpdatePatient.java:600).

refutedOrCorrected.claim: Extraction states Prescription is 'Created with status PENDING' (entity note) and the state-machine entry says 'from (create) to PENDING, trigger save_prescription -> savePrescription, status setStatus PENDING in service'.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MATERIALLY WRONG. The legacy savePrescription sets the initial prescription status to 'NOT-GIVEN', NOT 'PENDING'. There is no prescription.setStatus('PENDING') anywhere in PatientServiceImpl.java (a setStatus('PENDING') grep returns consultation:494, labTest:819, radiology:1062, procedure:1313, transfer:2811 — prescription is absent). Downstream consequence the extraction missed: the createConsultationTransfer guard blocks transfer only when a prescription.status equals 'PENDING' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2795), so a freshly-created NOT-GIVEN prescription does NOT block a transfer — the guard is effectively inert for newly created prescriptions.

refutedOrCorrected.claim: State-machine entry for Prescription create lists guard only as 'no duplicate medicine for same consultation; OUTPATIENT for consultation context' citing PatientResource.java:2109-2111.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: INCOMPLETE. savePrescription additionally enforces exactly-one-of {consultation, nonConsultation, admission} (throws if more than one or none) and, for the admission context, requires admission.status=='IN-PROCESS' (rejects PENDING as 'Admission not verified' and any other status as 'already signed off'). These context guards live in the SERVICE, not the resource.
refutedOrCorrected.citation: service/PatientServiceImpl.java:1489-1503 (one-context rule) and :1514-1522 (admission must be IN-PROCESS)

refutedOrCorrected.claim: open_follow_up_consultation state-machine entry lists guard as 'status==PENDING AND bill status in {PAID,COVERED}; for follow-up also allows NONE' citing PatientResource.java:913-916.
refutedOrCorrected.correction: MINOR OMISSION. open_follow_up_consultation also has a precondition that the consultation.isFollowUp()==true, else it throws 'This is not a follow up consultation' BEFORE the status/bill checks. The bill-status set {PAID,COVERED,NONE} and status==PENDING are correct.

MISSED: savePrescription context-exclusivity guards (exactly one of consultation/nonConsultation/admission) and the admission-must-be-IN-PROCESS guard at service/PatientServiceImpl.java:1489-1503 and :1514-1522 were not captured in the state-machine 'guard' field.

The extraction noted the duplicate guard at :2109-2111 (consultation branch) but not this defective outsider-branch copy at :2116.

Encounter: consultation_id real FK NULL, non_consultation_id real FK NULL, admission_uid loose NULL. patient_id NOT NULL real FK. patient_bill_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL loose @OneToOne. clinician_user_uid/insurance_plan_uid loose NULL. issue_pharmacy_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL loose→masterdata pharmacies (set on dispense, :97-100). Lifecycle: ordered_* (written), accepted_*/held_*/rejected_*/verified_* (DECLARED but NEVER written — boilerplate, include cols for fidelity), approved_*+approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ (THE dispense audit, only group populated, :139-143). pk_/uq_uid/fk_consultation/fk_non_consultation/fk_patient, ck_prescriptions_status CHECK status IN ('NOT-GIVEN','GIVEN'), ck_prescriptions_qty_nonneg CHECK (qty>=0 AND issued>=0 AND balance>=0), ck_prescriptions_one_encounter (num_nonnulls=1).

prescription_batches: id BIGINT IDENTITY + uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL UNIQUE (PrescriptionBatch.java:34-48; near-inert linkage, no consuming logic). no VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL (@NotBlank batch number, free-text no generator); manufactured_date DATE NULL; expiry_date DATE NULL; qty NUMERIC(19,6) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (legacy double→NUMERIC qty); prescription_id BIGINT NOT NULL real FK→prescriptions (@ManyToOne, :44-47). pk_/uq_uid/fk_prescription. idx on prescription_id.

patient_prescription_charts: id BIGINT IDENTITY + uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL UNIQUE (PatientPrescriptionChart.java:34-82; inpatient drug-administration record, NO status field). dosage VARCHAR(200), output VARCHAR(200), remark TEXT (all free-text). Encounter: consultation_id real FK NULL, non_consultation_id real FK NULL, admission_uid loose NULL. prescription_id BIGINT NOT NULL real FK→prescriptions (MANDATORY, :57-60). patient_id NOT NULL real FK. clinician_user_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL loose; nurse_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL loose→iam.nurses (:72-75). Audit. pk_/uq_uid/fk_prescription/fk_patient/fk_consultation. App rule: linked prescription must be GIVEN (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544), admission-only IN-PROCESS + nurse required (:2564-2577) — enforced in app, not DB. ck_patient_prescription_charts_one_encounter (num_nonnulls(consultation_id,non_consultation_id,admission_uid)=1).

deceased_notes: id BIGINT IDENTITY + uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL UNIQUE (DeceasedNote.java:36-76). patient_summary TEXT (REST enforces non-empty, no JPA constraint), cause_of_death TEXT, death_date DATE (legacy date_), death_time TIME (legacy time_) — client-supplied verbatim. status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING' (PENDING→APPROVED→ARCHIVED, :48). Mutually-exclusive encounter: admission_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL loose (@OneToOne, :50-53), consultation_id BIGINT NULL real FK→consultations (@OneToOne, :55-58). patient_id BIGINT NOT NULL real FK (@OneToOne optional=false, :60-63). created_by/business_day_uid/created_at; approved_by_user_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL (copied from createdBy NOT approver — legacy quirk :71-72), approved_on_day_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL. pk_/uq_uid/fk_consultation/fk_patient, uq_deceased_notes_consultation UNIQUE(consultation_id) partial, uq_deceased_notes_admission UNIQUE(admission_uid) partial (one note per encounter), ck_deceased_notes_status CHECK status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED','ARCHIVED'), ck_deceased_notes_one_encounter (num_nonnulls(consultation_id,admission_uid)=1).

referral_plans: id BIGINT IDENTITY + uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL UNIQUE (ReferralPlan.java:35-80; clinical referral to ExternalMedicalProvider, SEPARATE from death). Seven narrative cols (all TEXT, copied from request body): referring_diagnosis, history, investigation, management, operation_note, icu_admission_note, general_recommendation. status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING' (PENDING→APPROVED→ARCHIVED, :47). external_medical_provider_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL loose→referral.external_medical_providers (NOT built yet — @ManyToOne optional=false, :49-52). Encounter: admission_uid loose NULL, consultation_id real FK NULL (mutually exclusive, :54-62). patient_id NOT NULL real FK. created_by/business_day_uid/created_at; approved_by_user_uid/approved_on_day_uid VARCHAR(26) NULL (copied from creator, legacy quirk), approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL. pk_/uq_uid/fk_consultation/fk_patient, ck_referral_plans_status CHECK status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED','ARCHIVED'), ck_referral_plans_one_encounter (num_nonnulls(consultation_id,admission_uid)=1).

clinician_performances (if not already owned by iam): id BIGINT IDENTITY + uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL UNIQUE. Created on open_consultation/open_follow_up (clinicianPerformanceService.check, PatientResource.java:889-893). Cols: clinician_user_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL loose, consultation_id BIGINT NULL real FK→consultations, patient_id BIGINT NULL real FK, business_day_uid, audit. NOTE: confirm ownership with solution-architect — may belong to iam/reporting; if so, EXCLUDE from clinical migration. Listed as a candidate only; default = defer to its owning module.
sequences: NONE required for the clinical/encounter module. VERIFIED FACT: Consultation, ConsultationTransfer, NonConsultation, LabTest, Radiology, Procedure, Prescription, ClinicalNote, GeneralExamination, WorkingDiagnosis, FinalDiagnosis, DeceasedNote, ReferralPlan, PatientVital, PatientPrescriptionChart, PrescriptionBatch ALL use bare DB IDENTITY surrogate ids with NO human-facing document number, NO prefix, NO zero-padding, NO fiscal reset (Consultation.java:48-50; grep requestConsultationNo|requestLabTestNo|requestRadiologyNo|requestProcedureNo|requestPrescriptionNo across com.orbix.api = NO matches). The planning-doc CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} / ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} schemes DO NOT EXIST — do NOT create seq_consultation_no or seq_order_no.

DiagnosisType.code, ExternalMedicalProvider.code, PrescriptionBatch.no are user-supplied free-text strings — NO sequence (DiagnosisType.java:41-43; ExternalMedicalProvider.java:33-35; PrescriptionBatch.java:38-39).
Side-artefact numbers (PatientInvoice random-then-id, PatientCreditNote.requestPatientCreditNoteNo) belong to the BILLING context and already have seq_pcn_no (V13). No new sequence in inc-05.
uid VARCHAR(26) values are ULIDs generated in the application layer (not a DB sequence), consistent with V19/V15 convention.
indexes: consultations (additive on existing V19 table): existing idx_consultations_patient_id, idx_consultations_visit_id, idx_consultations_patient_status retained. ADD idx_consultations_clinician_status_followup ON consultations (clinician_user_uid, status, follow_up) — the DOCTOR WORKLIST queries: load_pending_consultations_by_clinician_id (clinician + followUp=false + status PENDING, PatientResource.java:817), load_follow_up_list (clinician + followUp=true + status IN PENDING,IN-PROCESS, :844), load_in_process (clinician + followUp=false + status IN IN-PROCESS,TRANSFERED, :871). This composite covers all three clinician worklists.
consultations: ADD idx_consultations_insurance_plan_uid ON consultations (insurance_plan_uid) WHERE insurance_plan_uid IS NOT NULL (insurance re-pricing/claim joins).

consultation_transfers: idx_consultation_transfers_consultation ON (consultation_id); idx_consultation_transfers_patient ON (patient_id). PARTIAL UNIQUE uq_consultation_transfers_one_pending_per_patient ON (patient_id) WHERE status='PENDING' — enforces the legacy invariant 'at most one PENDING transfer per patient' (findByPatientAndStatus Optional, PatientServiceImpl.java:2764, doConsultation:431). idx_consultation_transfers_pending ON (status) WHERE status='PENDING' — the system-wide pending-transfer queue (get_consultation_transfers findAllByStatus('PENDING'), unscoped, PatientResource.java:599).

lab_tests: idx_lab_tests_consultation ON (consultation_id), idx_lab_tests_non_consultation ON (non_consultation_id), idx_lab_tests_admission_uid ON (admission_uid), idx_lab_tests_patient ON (patient_id), idx_lab_tests_status ON (status), idx_lab_tests_patient_bill_uid ON (patient_bill_uid). PARTIAL idx_lab_tests_worklist ON (status) WHERE status IN ('PENDING','ACCEPTED','COLLECTED') — the lab worklist surfaces IN-PROCESS-context orders pending action (PatientResource.java:3668-3717). UNIQUE PARTIAL uq_lab_tests_consultation_type — NO: duplicate-type guard is app-level existsByConsultationAndLabTestType; do NOT add DB unique (admission path allows dupes).

radiologies: idx_radiologies_consultation, idx_radiologies_non_consultation, idx_radiologies_admission_uid, idx_radiologies_patient, idx_radiologies_status, idx_radiologies_patient_bill_uid. PARTIAL idx_radiologies_worklist ON (status) WHERE status IN ('PENDING','ACCEPTED').

procedures: idx_procedures_consultation, idx_procedures_non_consultation, idx_procedures_admission_uid, idx_procedures_patient, idx_procedures_status, idx_procedures_patient_bill_uid, idx_procedures_theatre_uid ON (theatre_uid) WHERE theatre_uid IS NOT NULL. PARTIAL idx_procedures_worklist ON (status) WHERE status IN ('PENDING','ACCEPTED').

prescriptions: idx_prescriptions_consultation, idx_prescriptions_non_consultation, idx_prescriptions_admission_uid, idx_prescriptions_patient, idx_prescriptions_status, idx_prescriptions_medicine_uid ON (medicine_uid), idx_prescriptions_patient_bill_uid. COMPOSITE idx_prescriptions_patient_medicine_status ON (patient_id, medicine_uid, status) — drives BOTH alert queries: same-medicine-30-day (findAllByPatientAndMedicineAndStatus(...,'GIVEN') + approvedAt, PatientResource.java:4496) and unfinished-course (same fetch + days parse, :4556). PARTIAL idx_prescriptions_pharmacy_worklist ON (status) WHERE status='NOT-GIVEN' (pharmacy dispense queue).
clinical_notes: idx_clinical_notes_consultation ON (consultation_id), idx_clinical_notes_non_consultation ON (non_consultation_id), idx_clinical_notes_admission_uid ON (admission_uid).

patient_vitals: idx_patient_vitals_consultation, idx_patient_vitals_non_consultation, idx_patient_vitals_admission_uid. PARTIAL idx_patient_vitals_submitted ON (status) WHERE status='SUBMITTED' (nurse→doctor handoff request, PatientResource.java:1321).

working_diagnoses: idx_working_diagnoses_consultation ON (consultation_id), idx_working_diagnoses_admission_uid ON (admission_uid), idx_working_diagnoses_patient ON (patient_id), idx_working_diagnoses_diagnosis_type_uid ON (diagnosis_type_uid).

deceased_notes: idx_deceased_notes_consultation, idx_deceased_notes_admission_uid, idx_deceased_notes_patient. PARTIAL idx_deceased_notes_list ON (status) WHERE status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED') (load_deceased_list hides ARCHIVED, PatientResource.java:5826).

referral_plans: idx_referral_plans_consultation, idx_referral_plans_admission_uid, idx_referral_plans_patient, idx_referral_plans_provider ON (external_medical_provider_uid). PARTIAL idx_referral_plans_list ON (status) WHERE status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED').

lab_test_attachments: idx_lab_test_attachments_lab_test ON (lab_test_id); radiology_attachments: idx_radiology_attachments_radiology ON (radiology_id); prescription_batches: idx_prescription_batches_prescription ON (prescription_id); patient_prescription_charts: idx_patient_prescription_charts_prescription ON (prescription_id), idx_patient_prescription_charts_admission_uid ON (admission_uid), idx_patient_prescription_charts_patient ON (patient_id).
non_consultations: idx_non_consultations_patient ON (patient_id), idx_non_consultations_visit ON (visit_id), idx_non_consultations_status ON (status).
