Default assumption: same schema, real FK, matching V19 stub which already FKs consultations→patients/visits.]
flywayPlan: V20__clinical_consultation_extend.sql — ALTER the EXISTING V19 consultations table additively (ADR-0008-R2, do NOT recreate): DROP CONSTRAINT ck_consultations_status; ADD CONSTRAINT ck_consultations_status CHECK (status IN ('PENDING','IN-PROCESS','TRANSFERED','CANCELED','SIGNED-OUT','HELD')); ADD COLUMN membership_no VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT ''; ADD COLUMN insurance_plan_uid VARCHAR(26); CREATE INDEX idx_consultations_clinician_status_followup; CREATE INDEX idx_consultations_insurance_plan_uid. Header cites Consultation.java:55-56, verification HELD@5753, CR-21 ownership transfer.

V21__clinical_non_consultations.sql — CREATE TABLE non_consultations (walk-in track) + indexes + FKs to patients/visits. Must precede V23-V27 because lab_tests/radiologies/procedures/prescriptions/clinical_notes FK non_consultation_id.

V22__clinical_consultation_transfers.sql — CREATE TABLE consultation_transfers + partial-unique one-pending-per-patient index + pending-queue index. FK→consultations (V19/V20). Header cites ConsultationTransfer.java:35-65, the CONFLICT open-question (source consultation left TRANSFERED, resolved by free_consultation→SIGNED-OUT PatientResource.java:764).

V23__clinical_notes_exam_diagnosis.sql — CREATE TABLE clinical_notes, general_examinations, patient_vitals, working_diagnoses, final_diagnoses (+ all one-encounter CHECKs, partial-unique upsert constraints on consultation/non_consultation, indexes). Header documents the consultation-vs-admission upsert/append asymmetry and the working_diagnoses/final_diagnoses two-separate-tables fidelity (WorkingDiagnosis.java:32 working_diagnosises → normalised working_diagnoses; FinalDiagnosis.java:35).

V24__clinical_lab_tests.sql — CREATE TABLE lab_tests + lab_test_attachments (FK CASCADE) + status CHECK + worklist partial index + one-encounter CHECK. Header cites LabTest.java:43-154, the rrange reserved-word column, camelCase audit setters.

V25__clinical_radiologies.sql — CREATE TABLE radiologies (incl. attachment BYTEA) + radiology_attachments (FK CASCADE) + status CHECK + indexes. Header cites Radiology.java:42-150, the COLLECTED dead-state note, ACCEPTED-gated attachments.

V26__clinical_procedures.sql — CREATE TABLE procedures (NO collected/approved cols; hours/minutes NUMERIC(19,6) not double; proc_time/proc_date) + status CHECK (4 values, no APPROVED) + theatre_uid index. Header cites Procedure.java:40-147, planning-doc M14-approve is fabricated.

V27__clinical_prescriptions.sql — CREATE TABLE prescriptions (status default NOT-GIVEN, only NOT-GIVEN/GIVEN; qty/issued/balance NUMERIC(19,6); approved_* dispense audit) + prescription_batches + patient_prescription_charts + alert composite index (patient_id,medicine_uid,status) + qty-nonneg CHECK. Header cites Prescription.java:38-144, the two-status-only finding, the PharmacySaleOrderDetail disambiguation (the 6+3-state lifecycle is NOT here).

V28__clinical_closure_deceased_referral.sql — CREATE TABLE deceased_notes + referral_plans (status CHECK PENDING/APPROVED/ARCHIVED, one-encounter CHECK, partial-unique per encounter for deceased, list partial indexes). Header cites DeceasedNote.java:36-76, ReferralPlan.java:35-80, Patient.type='DECEASED' is a String not a boolean, approved_by-copies-creator legacy quirk, external_medical_provider_uid loose (table not built).

V29__clinical_audit.sql — DEFERRED / NOT AUTHORED NOW. Append-only audit_log + per-PHI-entity audit tables (changed_by, changed_at, operation, old_values JSONB, new_values JSONB, client_ip, session_id) MUST wait for security-architect's PHI field classification and audit-coverage requirements. The clinical module is PHI-dense (clinical_notes, general_examinations, working/final_diagnoses, deceased_notes, lab/radiology results) so audit will be substantial — but I will not self-select the covered set. Placeholder version reserved; do NOT author until security-architect delivers the classification (traceability note per audited entity required at authoring time). NOTE: legacy has NO Envers/@Audited — audit is greenfield, nothing to migrate.

CONVENTION for all V20-V28: each is a single transactional DDL file; tables before their dependent FKs; all constraints named pk_/uq_/fk_/ck_/idx_; header block with bounded-context, ADR/CR refs, and legacy file:line citations matching the V15/V19 house style. Migrations are additive-only after baseline (new tables/columns/indexes); the only ALTER is the V20 consultation extend which DROPs+re-ADDs the status CHECK (a constraint-widening, additive in effect — no data loss, existing PENDING rows remain valid).
notes: AUDIT DDL IS DEFERRED — NOT IN THIS DELIVERABLE. Per role boundary, I will not author audit_log/per-entity audit tables until security-architect delivers the PHI field classification and audit-coverage requirements. The clinical module is the most PHI-dense in the system (SOAP notes, diagnoses, lab/radiology results, cause-of-death) so the eventual audit scope will be large; V29 is reserved. Do not infer audit coverage from legacy — legacy has ZERO @Audited entities and no Envers revision tables (confirmed by grep across com.orbix.api); audit is a greenfield security/compliance requirement.

OWNERSHIP / ADR-0008-R2: The consultations table physically exists from V19 (registration stub, CR-21). inc-05 takes ownership and EXTENDS it additively in V20 — it is NEVER recreated. The status CHECK is widened from {PENDING} to the full 6-value legacy set; existing PENDING rows stay valid (no backfill needed). This is the only ALTER; all else is CREATE TABLE.

EXACT LEGACY STATUS STRINGS (do NOT 'clean up'): Consultation = PENDING, IN-PROCESS (hyphen), TRANSFERED (single-R), CANCELED (single-L), SIGNED-OUT, HELD. There is NO BOOKED, NO IN_PROGRESS, NO COMPLETED on Consultation (planning-doc inventions). STOPPED is a query-only ghost (PatientResource.java:328) with no setStatus — EXCLUDED from the CHECK; flag to backend as a potential dead branch. ConsultationTransfer = PENDING/COMPLETED/CANCELED. LabTest/Radiology = PENDING/ACCEPTED/REJECTED/COLLECTED/VERIFIED. Procedure = PENDING/ACCEPTED/REJECTED/VERIFIED (NO COLLECTED, NO APPROVED). Prescription = NOT-GIVEN/GIVEN (only two). DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan/PatientVital lifecycle PENDING|EMPTY→...→ARCHIVED.

THREE SEPARATE ORDER TABLES, not one polymorphic ClinicalOrder: lab_tests, radiologies, procedures (+ prescriptions as a fourth) — planning-doc ClinicalOrder(kind=...) is fabricated. TWO SEPARATE DIAGNOSIS TABLES, not one ConsultationDiagnosis(kind=WORKING|FINAL): working_diagnoses + final_diagnoses are byte-for-byte structural twins. Faithfully reproduced.

NO DOCUMENT NUMBERS anywhere in the clinical module — every entity is IDENTITY-id + uid only. No CONS-/ORD- prefix scheme exists in legacy (grep negative). No sequences created. Side-artefact numbers (invoice/credit-note) belong to billing.

FLOAT→NUMERIC migrations (pre-approved data-type change, money/qty guardrail): Procedure.hours/minutes (double→NUMERIC(19,6)), Prescription.qty/issued/balance (double→NUMERIC(19,6)), PrescriptionBatch.qty (double→NUMERIC(19,6)). NO float/double in the new schema. Prices/money do NOT appear on these clinical rows — they flow through billing.patient_bills via patient_bill_uid.

VITALS PRESERVED AS FREE-TEXT STRINGS (exact-process fidelity): GeneralExamination/PatientVital store pressure/temperature/pulse_rate/weight/height/BMI/BSA/SpO2/respiratory_rate as VARCHAR with no numeric typing/validation/computation (GeneralExamination.java:42-51). Converting to numeric is an OPEN QUESTION escalated to engagement-lead — NOT done by default. If approved, it is a CR with backfill, not a silent change.

ADMISSION linkage is a LOOSE admission_uid (no FK) on 12 clinical tables. The admissions table is NOT built in this codebase yet (Inpatient is a future increment) — loose uid is the only correct choice now and stays loose under ADR-0008 (admissions is a different bounded context). Same for referral_plans.external_medical_provider_uid (referral module not built).

PARENT-TABLE ORDERING for Flyway: non_consultations (V21) and the V20 consultations-extend must precede the order/note/diagnosis tables (V23-V28) that carry consultation_id/non_consultation_id real FKs. Within each file, parent before child (lab_tests before lab_test_attachments, prescriptions before batches/charts).

APP-LAYER (not DB) RULES to hand to backend-engineer — do NOT encode as DB constraints because legacy enforces them in service/resource code and several are asymmetric: (a) exactly-one-encounter is the ONLY rule I encoded as a CHECK (num_nonnulls=1) since it is a clean invariant; (b) duplicate-diagnosis-type guard exists for CONSULTATION paths only, NOT admission (PatientResource.java:1662 vs 1685) — so NO DB unique on (admission_uid, diagnosis_type_uid); (c) max-5-attachments and status-gated attach (lab=COLLECTED, radiology=ACCEPTED); (d) pay-before-service gates (bill PAID/COVERED, +NONE for follow-up, +VERIFIED for inpatient) are app/worklist filters; (e) one-PENDING-transfer-per-patient IS encoded as a partial unique index since it is a clean DB-enforceable invariant.

OPEN QUESTIONS escalated (for engagement-lead/solution-architect/security-architect, not resolved here): (1) consultation patient_id/visit_id real-FK-ability depends on whether registration+clinical share one physical schema — default real FK matching V19; if separate schemas, switch to loose uids. (2) clinician_performances ownership (iam vs reporting vs clinical) — excluded from clinical migration pending solution-architect. (3) vitals numeric-vs-string. (4) HELD/STOPPED status set completeness. (5) ALL clinical endpoints have NO @PreAuthorize (open in legacy) — security-architect must decide whether the modern build adds privilege gating (behavioural change, CR) before audit DDL is scoped.

RECONCILIATION hooks for data-migration-engineer (preview, full strategy doc to follow): row-count parity per table (lab_tests/radiologies/procedures/prescriptions/working_diagnoses/final_diagnoses/clinical_notes/general_examinations/consultations/consultation_transfers/deceased_notes/referral_plans); Prescription quantity integrity check SUM(issued) and balance=qty-issued per GIVEN row; orphan check (every order's patient_bill_uid resolves in billing.patient_bills); status-domain check (no order/consultation row outside its CHECK set after ETL); one-encounter invariant spot-check (num_nonnulls=1 holds for every migrated clinical row). NO ETL script approved without these.

Stale-but-seeded codes present in legacy for fidelity only: PATIENT-A, PATIENT-C, PATIENT-U (V2:58-60) appear ONLY in COMMENTED-OUT legacy gates on create/get consultation transfer (PatientResource.java:572,595) and PRODUCT-CREATE (V2:61) only commented on save_lab_test (PatientResource.java:1932). They gate nothing in legacy; treat as dead, do not promote to active gates without a CR.
endpointGuards: POST send-to-doctor / do_consultation (booking → Consultation PENDING): hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE') — REAL, PatientResource.java:509. Already wired in new build PatientController.java:189.
POST cancel_consultation (→ CANCELED + bill CANCELED + credit-note/refund): hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE') — REAL, PatientResource.java:606.
POST free_consultation (→ SIGNED-OUT + child-bill cancel): hasAnyAuthority('PATIENT-ALL','PATIENT-CREATE','PATIENT-UPDATE') — REAL, PatientResource.java:681.
POST diagnosis_types/save (masterdata DiagnosisType create/update): hasAnyAuthority('ADMIN-ACCESS') — REAL, DiagnosisTypeResource.java:74; already enforced DiagnosisTypeController.java:47,55.

GET open_consultation (PENDING→IN-PROCESS, the pay-before-service open gate + ClinicianPerformance write): LEGACY HAS NO @PreAuthorize (PatientResource.java:879). FINDING: a state-mutating doctor action is method-unguarded in legacy. Recommend authenticated-only parity (no privilege gate) to faithfully reproduce; tightening to PATIENT-* is a CHANGE REQUEST requiring engagement-lead approval, not silent introduction.
GET open_follow_up_consultation (PENDING→IN-PROCESS, accepts bill NONE — CR-20): LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize (PatientResource.java:905). Same finding/recommendation as open_consultation.
POST create_consultation_transfer (IN-PROCESS→TRANSFERED): LEGACY @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:572). Authenticated-only parity; do not invent a gate.
GET cancel_consultation_transfer (TRANSFERED→IN-PROCESS): LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize (PatientResource.java:983). Authenticated-only parity.

GET get_consultation_transfers (system-wide PENDING transfer queue, unscoped): LEGACY @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:595). Authenticated-only parity — and see PHI concern: unscoped queue leaks patient identifiers.

POST save_lab_test / save_radiology / save_procedure / save_prescription (order create): LEGACY NO active @PreAuthorize (commented //PRODUCT-CREATE at save_lab_test:1932, //PATIENT-A/C/U at save_radiology:1975; save_procedure:2050 and save_prescription:2093 bare). Authenticated-only parity.

POST accept/reject/collect/verify/hold _lab_test | _radiology | accept_procedure | procedures/add_note (order lifecycle transitions): LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize anywhere (PatientResource.java:3888,3914,3939,3960,4005,4026,4200,4226,4251,4272,3405). Authenticated-only parity.
POST issue medicine / dispense (Prescription NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN): LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize. Authenticated-only parity; do NOT map PHARMACY_ORDER-* (that is procurement, not clinical dispense).

GET/POST save_working_diagnosis | save_final_diagnosis | admission variants | delete_working_diagnosis | delete_final_diagnosis: LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize (PatientResource.java:1650,1770,1685,1804,1917,1924). Authenticated-only parity. NOTE delete_* are GET endpoints doing unguarded hard deleteById — flag separately (defect, see notes).

GET/POST clinical-note / general-examination save & loaders (saveCG, load_*_by_consultation_id auto-create-on-GET): LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize (PatientResource.java:1224-1929). Authenticated-only parity.

ALL worklist GETs: load_pending_consultations_by_clinician_id, load_follow_up_list_by_clinician_id, load_in_process_consultations_by_clinician_id, get_nurse_outpatient_list, get_lab/radiology/procedure/pharmacy _outpatient/inpatient/outsider_list, get_*_by_patient_id (PatientResource.java:806,832,859,3639,3668-3801,4338-4388,3812): LEGACY NO @PreAuthorize. Authenticated-only parity (CR-04 reads-authenticated-only-no-privilege precedent, PatientController.java:65-66).

POST save_deceased_note, GET get_deceased_summary (→ type=DECEASED), POST save_referral_plan, GET get_referral_summary: LEGACY @PreAuthorize COMMENTED OUT (PatientResource.java:5393,5537,5563,5579,5694,5778,5822,5838). Authenticated-only parity; these mutate Patient.type and consultation/admission lifecycle while ungated — flag as the highest-severity legacy authz gap for an explicit CR decision.
security.settlementGate: PAY-BEFORE-SERVICE is wired exactly as the inc-04 seam dictates: the clinical (inc-05) module evaluates billing.api.SettlementPolicy.requireSettled(settled, paymentType, inpatient, emergency, billUid) (SettlementPolicy.java:60-65) at its OWN start()/accept() against its OWN LOCAL settled flag — it NEVER calls back into billing to read bill status. The local flag is written ONLY in the billing→encounter direction: PaymentService.create() calls settlementDispatcher.onBillPaid(bill, ctx) in the SAME transaction as bill.markPaid()/markSettled, before billRepository.save (PaymentService.java:97-100); SettlementDispatcher.onBillPaid (SettlementDispatcher.java:31-35) sets bill.markSettled(ctx.timestamp()) and is the documented seam that will ALSO set settled=true on the inc-05 Consultation/LabTest/Radiology/Procedure local projection in that same tx — no @Async, no REQUIRES_NEW, no reverse edge (ADR-0008 §6). PARITY MAPPING of the legacy open gate onto requireSettled: legacy open_consultation requires bill PAID|COVERED (PatientResource.java:884-896) and open_follow_up additionally accepts NONE (CR-20, PatientResource.java:914); these map to SettlementPolicy outcomes — CASH outpatient/outsider → prepayment REQUIRED (the only blocked case; legacy's PAID requirement), INSURANCE→COVERED auto-pass, inpatient→pass (legacy worklists also accept VERIFIED for inpatient, PatientResource.java:3693), follow-up NONE→pass via the local settled flag being set by the followUp NONE-bill path (CR-20). Inc-05 must drive the local flag for COVERED/NONE/inpatient at booking/charge time (not only on cash PAID), since onBillPaid fires only on the cash PAID transition. The no-reverse-edge invariant is enforced by ApplicationModules.verify() (ModularityTest.java:17, MODULES.verify()) PLUS an ArchUnit rule asserting no com.otapp.hmis.clinical.* type depends on com.otapp.hmis.billing.* except the published billing.api package (SettlementPolicy/PayBeforeServiceException), and that SettlementDispatcher writes flow billing→encounter only. Acceptance: an ArchUnit test must fail if any clinical accept()/start() injects a billing repository or service to re-check bill status. ENFORCEMENT-LEVEL NOTE: legacy enforced pay-before-service only at the open gate and as a worklist UI filter — accept/verify transition endpoints themselves do NOT re-check the bill (PatientResource.java:3896-3909 etc.). Inc-05 must place requireSettled at start()/accept() (the open transition), preserving that legacy enforcement point; adding it to every transition is hardening that needs a CR.
phiConcerns: PHI in query strings / path params: legacy free_consultation validates by patient registration 'no' passed in the request, and worklists/loaders key off patient ids; the same-medicine alert and per-patient order views take patient/medicine ids on the GET URL (PatientResource.java:4480-4521,3812-3820). Specify: clinical identifiers (patient uid, MRN) MUST travel as opaque ULIDs in path segments, NEVER patient name/membershipNo/DOB in query strings (which land in access logs / browser history / referer headers). No PHI in URLs — minimisation mandate.

Ungated PHI reads (parity precedent CR-04): every clinical worklist and loader is authenticated-only with NO privilege gate in legacy, matching inc-03 CR-04 (PatientController.java:65-66, PatientQueryService.java:22). This is acceptable PARITY but it means any authenticated user can read any patient's clinical notes, diagnosis, vitals, lab/radiology results. Specify a PHI access-LOG requirement (who/when/which patient record) on these authenticated-only reads as the compensating control, since there is no per-record privilege boundary. Hand the read-access-log field set to data-architect.

Unscoped system-wide queues leak cross-patient PHI: get_consultation_transfers returns ALL PENDING transfers system-wide (findAllByStatus('PENDING'), PatientResource.java:599) and the lab/radiology/pharmacy worklists return all matching patients regardless of performing user/department (PatientResource.java:3668-3801,4338-4388). Reproducing this verbatim broadens PHI exposure; flag that any scoping to clinic/department/clinician is a CHANGE REQUEST (it is net-new), but the PHI access-log must capture the breadth of records each query returns.

PHI in free-text clinical columns drives encryption-at-rest scope: ClinicalNote (8 SOAP free-text fields incl. mainComplain length=500, ClinicalNote.java:44-52), GeneralExamination/PatientVital (all vitals as free-text Strings), WorkingDiagnosis/FinalDiagnosis.description, Procedure.note (length=10000), Prescription.instructions/reference, DeceasedNote.patientSummary/causeOfDeath, ReferralPlan clinical narratives. These are PHI clinical content — specify encryption-at-rest and exclusion from any non-audit query projection/log. Coordinate the authoritative field list with data-architect's PHI inventory.

PHI MUST NOT leak into audit before/after diffs in plaintext beyond what compliance requires: audit of clinical mutations (notes, diagnosis, prescription dispense, deceased/referral) must capture who/what/when and before/after, but the before/after payloads contain PHI — specify that the tamper-evident audit store is itself encrypted-at-rest and access-controlled, and that approvedBy/approvedOn copy-from-createdBy legacy quirk (DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan, PatientResource.java:5905-5906) means the audit actor must be captured from the real authenticated principal, NOT the copied creator id, or the audit trail misattributes the approver.

No PHI in JWT claims beyond the minimal subject/user-uid: the legacy actor is resolved via userService.getUserId(request)/getUser(request) from the JWT (PatientResource.java:3900 etc.). Specify the JWT carries only the user uid and authorities — never patient identifiers, membershipNo, or names — even though clinical endpoints operate on PHI.

Error responses must not echo PHI: legacy has multiple raw .get() dereferences (NoSuchElementException → HTTP 500) and friendly-message paths that interpolate patient/medicine names (e.g. 'Invalid issue value in '+medicine.name, PatientResource.java:3221; clinician/clinic name lookups PatientResource.java:525-526). Specify error bodies omit PHI and use opaque uids; map missing-entity to 404 without echoing the supplied PHI value.
notes: RBAC verdict for inc-05: the clinical-OPD authorization surface is, in legacy, almost entirely authenticated-only. Only the consultation booking-lifecycle mutations (do_consultation/switch/cancel/free) carry the REAL PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE gates, and only diagnosis_types/save carries ADMIN-ACCESS. EVERYTHING else (open/transfer/order-lifecycle/diagnosis/notes/worklists/deceased/referral) is ungated at method level. The RBAC document must state this as the verified baseline and treat any per-action privilege as net-new requiring a CR — NOT reproduce the planning doc's CONSULTATION_START/PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE/PRESCRIPTION_CREATE inventions.

The complete legacy privilege inventory is 35 codes (V2__seed_iam.sql:26-61). The '177 privilege codes' in 05-clinical-opd.md is the count of @PreAuthorize annotation SITES, not distinct codes — same drift the firm memory records. Do not seed any new V20+ privilege for inc-05 unless a CR is approved.

Settlement seam integrity is enforceable today: ModularityTest.MODULES.verify() (ModularityTest.java:17) already runs Spring Modulith verification; add an ArchUnit rule that (a) clinical.* may depend on billing.api only, and (b) SettlementDispatcher is the sole writer of the downstream local settled flag. PaymentService.applyPaymentSideEffects (PaymentService.java:174-178) is the documented but empty inc-05/06 seam for consultation→SIGNED-OUT, admission→IN-PROCESS, pharmacy-SO→APPROVED on payment — security review must verify inc-05 wires those via the dispatcher (billing→encounter), never by clinical code reaching into billing.

Exact-process caveat on the gate placement: legacy enforces pay-before-service at the open transition + worklist UI filter only; accept/verify endpoints do NOT re-check the bill (a deliberately reproduced gap, see worklists-rbac extraction openQuestions). Put SettlementPolicy.requireSettled at start()/accept() (the open transition) for parity. Per-transition enforcement is hardening — flag as CR.

Audit-scope reminder (guardrail): do NOT derive audit coverage from Hibernate Envers — legacy annotates ZERO entities (confirmed by repeated @Audited grep returning zero across com.orbix.api). Audit coverage for inc-05 must be compliance-driven + confirmed-write-path-driven, with rigour on clinical mutations: Prescription dispense (NOT-GIVEN→GIVEN, the approvedBy/approvedOn/approvedAt triplet), diagnosis add/delete (incl. the unguarded hard delete_working/final_diagnosis GETs — PatientResource.java:1917-1929), and the deceased/referral type-change transitions. Hand finalised audit requirements to data-architect for physical schema.

Device-binding: confirmed ABSENT in legacy (fingerprint/deviceId/X-Device grep = zero files across com.orbix.api, corroborated in two independent extractions). Document that no device-binding requirement exists for inc-05; any proposal to add one is a CR, not exact-process.

Defects to route to backend-engineer/code-reviewer as security-relevant findings (not to silently fix): delete_working_diagnosis/delete_final_diagnosis are GET endpoints performing unguarded hard deleteById with no auth, no existence check, no audit (PatientResource.java:1917-1929) — destructive clinical mutation via GET violates safe-method semantics and erases the who-removed-a-diagnosis trail; and loadClinicalNote/loadGeneralExamination GETs persist rows as a side effect (HTTP 201 from GET, PatientResource.java:1230-1277). Both are CR candidates with security/audit impact.

Coordinate PHI field identification with data-architect (owns the authoritative PHI inventory + physical audit schema); security-architect owns the compliance requirements and controls (encryption-at-rest, masking, access-log) that drive them. Do not finalise the inc-05 RBAC model without legacy-analyst confirmation that the PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE + ADMIN-ACCESS mapping and the ungated-elsewhere baseline match the extracted legacy data — which the verified facts above already corroborate.

clinicalSoundness: CONSULTATION LIFECYCLE is clinically realistic for a Tanzanian OPD pay-before-service facility. The flow PENDING (booked at registration) -> IN-PROCESS (doctor opens after payment) -> SIGNED-OUT (doctor releases) mirrors how a TZ clinic actually runs: cashier/registration collects the consultation fee or confirms NHIF cover, then the doctor sees the patient. The two pay-before-service gates (open requires bill PAID|COVERED at PatientResource.java:885; worklist hides unpaid PENDING at PatientResource.java:817-826) faithfully reproduce the legacy revenue-protection model and are clinically acceptable for routine OPD. PRESERVE the hyphenated free-text status strings PENDING/IN-PROCESS/TRANSFERED/CANCELED/SIGNED-OUT/HELD exactly (Consultation.java:55-56); the planning-doc BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED are inventions and must not be substituted.

ACCEPT-BEFORE-COMPLETE on diagnostic orders is clinically sound and must be preserved. Lab: ACCEPTED -> COLLECTED -> VERIFIED (PatientResource.java:3947,3968) correctly models specimen acceptance, specimen collection, then result verification. Radiology: ACCEPTED -> VERIFIED directly with no COLLECTED step (PatientResource.java:4280-4292) is also clinically correct because imaging has no specimen to collect; the divergence from lab is intentional, not a defect, and must be preserved. The collect_radiology111 endpoint (PatientResource.java:4317-4333) is a dead/renamed artefact and the live radiology pipeline correctly ignores COLLECTED.

PROCEDURE flow PENDING -> ACCEPTED -> VERIFIED-via-add_note (PatientResource.java:3405-3415) is clinically adequate: the procedure result narrative is captured in the 'note' field and finalisation requires a non-empty note. CONFIRMED there is NO separate APPROVED state and NO approve step for procedures (Procedure.java has no approved* columns; ProcedureModel.java:28-57 has no approved field). The planning-doc M14 'procedure approve' step and PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE privilege are fabrications and must NOT be introduced; doing so would add a clinical gate that never existed in the source-of-truth process.

TRANSFER HAND-OFF (clinic-to-clinic) is clinically realistic for a multi-clinic facility. The raise guard requiring source consultation == IN-PROCESS and NO pending child orders/prescriptions (PatientServiceImpl.java:2756-2800) is good clinical hygiene: a patient should not be moved between clinics with un-actioned lab/radiology/procedure/prescription orders left open. Destination must differ from source (PatientServiceImpl.java:2804). The destination clinician is correctly chosen at ACCEPT time, not raise time (the transfer targets a CLINIC, which matches real triage/registration re-routing). PRESERVE the corrected free_consultation behaviour (verification finding): a TRANSFERED consultation IS signed out (PatientResource.java:763-765 unconditional SIGNED-OUT) which is the operational step that releases the source consultation so the destination re-send succeeds.

PRESCRIBING flow NOT-GIVEN (ordered by clinician) -> GIVEN (dispensed by pharmacy) with the qty/issued/balance model and all-or-nothing dispense (issued must equal qty, PatientResource.java:3221,3224) is clinically acceptable for an OPD/inpatient dispensing context. The PatientPrescriptionChart guard requiring the linked Prescription to be GIVEN before a nurse can chart administration (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544-2546 'Prescription not picked from pharmacy') is correct medication-safety logic: you cannot chart administration of a drug that was never dispensed. PRESERVE both prescription status values exactly (NOT-GIVEN/GIVEN); the planning-doc PENDING/ACCEPTED/HELD/VERIFIED/APPROVED/SOLD lifecycle belongs to PharmacySaleOrderDetail (walk-in sales), NOT to clinician Prescription.

DEATH and REFERRAL CLOSURE are clinically and regulatorily appropriate as SEPARATE workflows (DeceasedNote vs ReferralPlan), not a single polymorphic closure(). The deceased-note workflow correctly captures patientSummary + causeOfDeath as mandatory (PatientResource.java:5699), correctly frees the ward bed on inpatient death (PatientResource.java:5729), and routes through a HELD -> APPROVED -> ARCHIVED review state. Capturing a documented cause of death and a clinical summary before the body/record is released is consistent with MOH death-documentation expectations. PRESERVE the dual consultation/admission paths exactly.
safetyHazards: PAY-BEFORE-SERVICE GATE ON EMERGENCY/INPATIENT CARE — SAFETY-CRITICAL, but verify-then-preserve. The OPD consultation open gate hard-blocks IN-PROCESS unless bill is PAID|COVERED (PatientResource.java:885). For genuine emergencies this is a clinical hazard: a collapsing patient must be seen before a cashier settles a consultation fee. HOWEVER the legacy already provides relief valves that MUST be carried forward unchanged: (a) the follow-up NONE-bill path opens on bill status NONE (PatientResource.java:914, CR-20), and (b) INPATIENT/admission order worklists accept patientBill.status==VERIFIED (deferred billing) in addition to PAID|COVERED (PatientResource.java:3693,4377), i.e. an admitted patient is serviceable on a not-yet-settled VERIFIED bill. SettlementPolicy in the new build already scopes the strict gate to CASH-outpatient-only — that scoping is the correct safety boundary and the inc-05 open gate must NOT be widened to block inpatient/emergency. RECOMMENDATION: route any true emergency-without-payment scenario through the existing VERIFIED/NONE deferred-billing mechanics rather than the cash open gate; do not invent a new blocking gate.

DECEASED RE-BOOKING IS NOT BLOCKED — clinically this is the SAFE legacy behaviour and must be preserved, NOT 'fixed'. After death, Patient.type is set to DECEASED (PatientResource.java:5901,5920) but NO code reads type==DECEASED to block booking; a deceased patient simply fails the generic OUTPATIENT requirement on do_consultation with the message 'Please change patient type to OUTPATIENT...' (PatientResource.java:535-536). The planning-doc PATIENT_IS_DECEASED hard block DOES NOT EXIST and must NOT be introduced silently. CLINICAL RATIONALE: a hard deceased-lock is hazardous in a real facility because deaths can be mis-recorded (wrong patient selected, data-entry error, MRN mix-up); an irreversible deceased flag with no documented un-set path (no code reverts type away from DECEASED) would permanently lock a living patient out of care. If a deceased-booking guard is ever wanted it is a CHANGE REQUEST requiring an approved, audited reversal path — flag to engagement-lead, do not auto-add.

PRESCRIBING DUPLICATE/UNFINISHED-COURSE ALERTS ARE ADVISORY AND MUST STAY ADVISORY (non-blocking). The same-medicine-within-30-days alert (PatientResource.java:4480-4521) and the unfinished-course alert (PatientResource.java:4528-4580) both return free-text advisory strings and NEVER block prescribing; the unfinished-course alert is even wrapped in a swallow-all try/catch. This is clinically correct: these are informational decision-support prompts, and a clinician must retain authority to re-prescribe (e.g. lost medication, dose change, legitimate early refill). DO NOT convert these soft alerts into hard blocks. SEPARATELY, the HARD duplicate-drug-per-consultation block (PatientResource.java:2109-2111 'Duplicate drug is not allowed. Consider editing qty') IS a real legacy block and must be preserved as-is — it prevents accidental double-prescription of the same medicine on one encounter, which is a genuine medication-safety control.

MISSING ALLERGY-CHECK GATE — clinical-safety gap inherited from legacy, flag but do not invent. ClinicalNote carries a free-text drugsAndAllergyHistory field (ClinicalNote.java:49) but NOTHING in the prescribing path reads it; savePrescription performs no allergy cross-check against the ordered Medicine. There is no drug-allergy interaction check anywhere. This is a recognised patient-safety weakness, but adding an allergy-contraindication block is NEW behaviour beyond exact-process and must be raised as a CHANGE REQUEST to engagement-lead/clinical governance, not silently implemented. Document it in the edge-case register as a known safety gap so it is a conscious decision, not an oversight.

CLINICAL ENDPOINTS HAVE NO METHOD-LEVEL AUTHORIZATION — a real safety/privacy hazard. open_consultation, open_follow_up_consultation, create/cancel_consultation_transfer, save/accept/reject/verify of lab/radiology/procedure, save_working/final_diagnosis, save_prescription, save_deceased_note, save_referral_plan and ALL worklists carry NO active @PreAuthorize (annotations commented out or absent; verified across PatientResource). This means any authenticated user can record a diagnosis, dispense-trigger, transfer, or DECLARE A DEATH. Reproducing this verbatim is clinically and medico-legally unsafe (unauthenticated death recording, unauthorised diagnosis entry). RECOMMENDATION: this is the single most important item to escalate to security-architect — adding authority gating is a behavioural change that needs an approved CR, but preserving fully-open clinical/death endpoints should be treated as a BLOCKER pending that decision, not a default.

NON-DETERMINISTIC 'CURRENT' CLINICAL RECORD ON ADMISSIONS — patient-safety relevant. load_clinical_note_by_admission_id and load_general_examination_by_admission_id return the LAST row from an UNORDERED findAllByAdmission iteration (PatientResource.java:1360-1364,1426-1430). With multiple appended admission notes the 'current' note/exam shown to a clinician is order-dependent and may display a stale or wrong note. For inpatient clinical documentation this is a real hazard (clinician acting on the wrong note). Flag to solution-architect/data-architect: the rebuild should define an explicit deterministic ordering (e.g. by createdAt/id desc) while preserving the append model — confirm as an exact-process clarification, not a silent behaviour change.
nhifConcerns: INSURANCE CONSULTATION RE-PRICING is correctly scoped per (clinic, plan, covered=true) via ConsultationInsurancePlan (PatientServiceImpl.java:597-616) and per-service plans exist for lab (LabTestInsurancePlan), radiology, procedure and medicine (MedicineInsurancePlan, PatientServiceImpl.java:1549). This matches NHIF per-service tariff structure. CONCERN: if a covered plan row is absent for a clinic the booking throws 'Plan not available for this clinic' (PatientServiceImpl.java:597-616) — confirm the masterdata ServicePrice/PriceLookup matrix in inc-05 fully covers every (plan x service x clinic) combination the facility submits to NHIF, otherwise insured patients silently fall through to CASH or are blocked. This is a claim-completeness risk.

TWO-PLAN GUARD must be preserved: doConsultation throws 'Use of two or more insurance plan...' when the resolved plan differs from the patient's current plan and a PENDING PatientInvoice exists (PatientServiceImpl.java:537-549), and flips all PENDING invoices to APPROVED before re-covering (PatientServiceImpl.java:586-590). This protects NHIF claim integrity (one open invoice per plan). The verification flagged this as MISSED in the original extraction — it MUST be in the inc-05 spec or insured billing will be wrong.

INSURANCE INVOICE NUMBERING IS BROKEN AT SOURCE and is an NHIF-submission risk. The PatientInvoice 'no' is seeded with String.valueOf(Math.random()) then overwritten with the DB id as a string (PatientServiceImpl.java:627,638; same pattern at 857/878, 1567/1588, 1636/1657). NHIF claim submission requires a stable, traceable invoice/claim reference. The inc-05 billing seam must replace this with the canonical billing invoice-number generator (the same context that owns patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo()), and ensure the transient random value can NEVER persist. Flag to data-architect/billing.

CLOSURE BILL-GATE INCONSISTENCY affects insured patients at end-of-encounter. Death/inpatient closure blocks on bills in {UNPAID, VERIFIED} (PatientResource.java:5857,5876) while OPD-referral closure blocks only on {UNPAID} (PatientResource.java:5468). For an NHIF patient, VERIFIED (deferred/insurer-pending) bills are legitimately unsettled at the cashier but covered by the insurer; blocking deceased-summary on VERIFIED but allowing referral on VERIFIED is asymmetric. Confirm with billing/NHIF compliance which rule is canonical so insured-patient closure does not stall on insurer-pending bills. Preserve exactly until the CR decision.

INSURANCE-TO-CASH WIPE ON OPD REFERRAL has revenue/claims implications. save_referral_plan sets patient.setPaymentType('CASH') and patient.setInsurancePlan(null) (PatientResource.java:5505-5507). For an NHIF patient referred to an external provider this strips their insurance profile; confirm this is intended (so a subsequent visit re-enrols the plan) and that any in-flight NHIF claim for the referred encounter is already closed before the wipe. Document in NHIF checklist.

DIAGNOSIS CODING IS NOT ICD AND CANNOT SUPPORT NHIF/MOH CODED CLAIMS AS-IS. DiagnosisType.code is a free-text user-supplied unique string with NO ICD-10 validation, no external code system, no format mask (DiagnosisType.java:41-43); there is ZERO ICD in the legacy tree. WorkingDiagnosis/FinalDiagnosis carry no ICD field. NHIF and MOH (DHIS2/HMIS) reporting increasingly expect ICD-10 coded diagnoses. Preserving the free-text DiagnosisType exactly is correct for exact-process, but flag to data-architect that an ICD-10 mapping/lookup is a likely future MOH/NHIF requirement and the DiagnosisType model should be designed to ACCOMMODATE an ICD code dimension (CR) without breaking the legacy free-text behaviour.
mustPreserve: Consultation free-text status set EXACTLY: PENDING, IN-PROCESS (hyphen), TRANSFERED (single-R), CANCELED (single-L), SIGNED-OUT, HELD (deceased path), plus the queried-but-unwritten STOPPED reference. No enum substitution, no spelling normalisation (Consultation.java:55-56; HELD at PatientResource.java:5753; STOPPED queried at PatientResource.java:328).

PAY-BEFORE-SERVICE gates exactly: normal open requires bill PAID|COVERED (PatientResource.java:885); follow-up open additionally accepts NONE (PatientResource.java:914, CR-20 free-bill); doctor worklist hides unpaid PENDING by filtering to PAID|COVERED (PatientResource.java:817-826).

Follow-up NONE-bill path (CR-20): booking with followUp==true overrides bill status to NONE (PatientServiceImpl.java:467-469,606-608); the open gate uniquely accepts NONE only for follow-ups. This is a distinct revenue rule and MUST be reproduced.

Three SEPARATE order entities LabTest/Radiology/Procedure plus Prescription — NOT a polymorphic ClinicalOrder; and TWO separate diagnosis entities WorkingDiagnosis/FinalDiagnosis — NOT a single ConsultationDiagnosis with a kind discriminator.

Order pipelines diverge and must stay divergent: LabTest ACCEPTED->COLLECTED->VERIFIED with attachments at COLLECTED (PatientServiceImpl.java:2832); Radiology ACCEPTED->VERIFIED with no live COLLECTED step and attachments at ACCEPTED (PatientServiceImpl.java:2931); Procedure PENDING->ACCEPTED->VERIFIED-via-add_note with NO approve step.

Procedure settlement gate exactly: addProcedureNote requires bill status in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED} else 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified' (PatientResource.java:3408-3414); lab/radiology accept/verify endpoints do NOT check the bill — settlement is enforced at worklist query level and report/note endpoints only.

Inpatient worklists accept patientBill.status==VERIFIED (deferred billing) in addition to PAID|COVERED; outpatient/outsider accept only PAID|COVERED; nurse-outpatient additionally accepts NONE (PatientResource.java:3693,3639-3649,4377). This per-patient-class asymmetry is the deferred-billing safety valve and must be preserved.

Prescription lifecycle EXACTLY two values NOT-GIVEN -> GIVEN (PatientServiceImpl.java:1532; PatientResource.java:3230); all-or-nothing dispense issued==qty with the earlier 'Invalid issue value' guard at PatientResource.java:3221; Prescription has NO payStatus field (payment carried on its PatientBill); dosage/frequency/route/days are free-text Strings, NOT FKs.

HARD duplicate-drug-per-consultation block (PatientResource.java:2109-2111) AND the SOFT advisory same-medicine-30-day / unfinished-course alerts (PatientResource.java:4480-4521, 4528-4580) are TWO distinct mechanisms — preserve both; alerts stay non-blocking free-text.

Consultation duplicate-diagnosis-type guard exists ONLY on consultation paths (existsByConsultationAndDiagnosisType, PatientResource.java:1662,1782); admission diagnosis paths have NO such guard (PatientResource.java:1685-1707,1804-1825). Do NOT silently unify — the asymmetry is real legacy behaviour.

Deceased workflow exactly: mandatory patientSummary + causeOfDeath (PatientResource.java:5699); inpatient frees ward bed on HELD (PatientResource.java:5729) and on approval SIGNED-OUT (PatientResource.java:5897); state HELD -> APPROVED -> ARCHIVED with 48h archival sweep (UpdatePatient.java:572-584); Patient.type set DECEASED (PatientResource.java:5901,5920); approvedBy/approvedOn copied from createdBy/createdOn (creator, not approver).

Referral workflow exactly as a SEPARATE entity: OPD referral sets consultation SIGNED-OUT at save, wipes patient to CASH/no-plan (PatientResource.java:5501-5507), patient.type stays/becomes OUTPATIENT (NOT deceased); inpatient referral sets admission STOPPED then SIGNED-OUT with ward-bed free and discharge-field stamping from plan creator (PatientResource.java:5611-5631).

Admission force-close: on admission, all of the patient's PENDING/IN-PROCESS consultations are force SIGNED-OUT (PatientServiceImpl.java:1956) and any active consultations cannot coexist with an active admission (do_consultation rejects active admission, PatientResource.java:531-534).
notes: TRANSFER ACCEPT 'CONFLICT' IS RESOLVED by the corrected free_consultation finding: a TRANSFERED source consultation IS signed out (PatientResource.java:763-765 unconditional SIGNED-OUT, skipping only the child-bill-cancellation loop). So the operational sequence is: doctor frees the TRANSFERED consultation -> registration re-sends patient to destination clinic -> the PENDING transfer is marked COMPLETED and a NEW PENDING consultation is created. The original 'guard appears to block re-send' open question is therefore a non-issue PROVIDED free_consultation is invoked first. The inc-05 spec must document this two-step operator workflow explicitly so the destination pickup is reproducible.

CLINICIAN-CLINIC AFFILIATION IS NOT CHECKED AT TRANSFER ACCEPT — only cn.isActive() is validated (PatientServiceImpl.java:427-429); there is NO check the destination clinician belongs to the destination clinic. The planning-doc affiliation check does not exist (NOT FOUND). Clinically, routing a patient to a clinic and then any active clinician is acceptable in a small TZ facility, but flag to solution-architect that clinic-clinician affiliation is OWNED BY iam (loose clinicUids @ElementCollection) and any affiliation enforcement would be NEW behaviour requiring a CR.

VITALS ARE FREE-TEXT STRINGS with no numeric typing, range validation, unit enforcement, or server-side BMI/BSA computation (GeneralExamination.java:42-51; PatientResource.java:1563-1588). Clinically this is weak (no auto-flagging of dangerous vitals, e.g. SpO2 or BP), but it is the exact legacy process. The pre-approved double->BigDecimal data-type change does NOT extend to silently adding range validation; if vitals validation/auto-flagging is wanted it is a separate CR. The PatientVital nurse-capture staging flow (EMPTY->SUBMITTED->ARCHIVED, PatientResource.java:1298-1346) feeds GeneralExamination and must be preserved including the unchecked .get() defect being fixed only as an error-contract clarification, not a process change.

DAY-ROLLOVER ARCHIVAL SWEEP for DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan is wrapped in an empty catch-all (UpdatePatient.java:600) that silently swallows exceptions — one bad record halts the sweep with no log. This touches the day-update batch (possibly a different bounded context than inc-05) but is clinically relevant because it governs how long approved death/referral records stay visible before archival. Flag to the owning context; for exact-process the swallow must be reproduced/flagged, not silently hardened.

GET-WITH-SIDE-EFFECT loaders persist empty clinical rows (HTTP 201 from a GET) for clinical notes / general exams (PatientResource.java:1230-1247 etc.). This is a defect-shaped behaviour but is exact-process; reproduce-vs-correct is a CR decision for engagement-lead. Not a clinical safety issue, but it pollutes the clinical record with blank notes and should be noted for QA test scenarios.

PHI/PII AUDIT GAP: there is NO Hibernate Envers audit trail (no @Audited anywhere) — auditing is hand-rolled createdBy/createdOn/createdAt only, and DECEASED-note/diagnosis/prescription endpoints are unauthorised and unaudited beyond the create stamp. For PHI governance the inc-05 spec should require, at minimum, audit events on: death recording/approval, diagnosis entry/hard-delete (delete_working/final_diagnosis do unguarded hard deleteById with no audit, PatientResource.java:1917-1929), prescription dispense, and consultation transfer. Hand to security-architect as PHI access-control + audit requirements; this is a requirement addition, not a code change.

SIGN-OFF STATUS: I can give a CONDITIONAL clinical sign-off on the inc-05 OPD consultation/order/transfer/prescribing/closure flow AS EXTRACTED, subject to: (1) security-architect ruling on the unauthorised clinical/death endpoints (treated as a blocker pending decision), (2) billing/data-architect replacing the Math.random() invoice numbering before any NHIF submission path, and (3) engagement-lead CR decisions on the deceased-rebooking guard, allergy-check gate, and closure bill-gate inconsistency. None of these are licence to deviate from exact-process; they are explicit conflicts surfaced for resolution.

edgeCases: Follow-up free-bill (CR-20 NONE path): a follow-up consultation books a PatientBill with status 'NONE' (PatientServiceImpl.java:467-469); open_follow_up_consultation must accept bill status PAID OR COVERED OR NONE, while the normal open_consultation must reject NONE and accept only PAID/COVERED (PatientResource.java:885 vs 914). Test both gates with all four bill statuses (PAID/COVERED/NONE/UNPAID) crossed against followUp true/false.

open_consultation vs open_follow_up_consultation status-guard asymmetry: open_consultation THROWS 'Could not open. Not a pending consultation.' when status != PENDING (PatientResource.java:898-900), but open_follow_up_consultation has NO else-throw on non-PENDING and silently returns true without setStatus (PatientResource.java:913-928). Edge: opening a follow-up that is already IN-PROCESS is a silent no-op returning success.

Transfer-accept reachability: at the destination clinic, doConsultation marks the PENDING ConsultationTransfer COMPLETED (PatientServiceImpl.java:436) but the SOURCE consultation remains TRANSFERED; the immediately-following guard rejects any patient with a consultation in {PENDING,TRANSFERED} (PatientServiceImpl.java:444-450). The happy path is only reachable if free_consultation was first called to SIGN-OUT the TRANSFERED source (which it does unconditionally for TRANSFERED, PatientResource.java:763-764). Test the full transfer cycle end-to-end and assert the source is SIGNED-OUT before destination re-send succeeds.

free_consultation TRANSFERED branch: a TRANSFERED consultation skips the entire IN-PROCESS inner block (no registration-number check, no child-bill cancellation) and falls through to unconditional SIGNED-OUT (PatientResource.java:687,763-764). IN-PROCESS path requires a matching registration number and cancels UNPAID/null child LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription bills (701-754). Test that TRANSFERED frees silently while IN-PROCESS enforces both.

free_consultation registration-number identity: IN-PROCESS path requires request 'no' non-empty (PatientResource.java:689), patient with that no must exist (692-695), and that patient.id must equal consultation.patient.id (696-698). Note 696 uses '!=' on Long objects (reference equality) — edge case for patient ids >127 outside the Integer cache. Test mismatched/blank/foreign registration numbers.

Same-clinic and clinic-match guards use '==' on Long ids (PatientServiceImpl.java:2804 raise-time; :433 accept-time). For clinic ids outside the Integer cache (-128..127) this is reference comparison, not value comparison — latent defect. Test transfers between clinics whose ids exceed 127.

Consultation booking blocked when patient has any active Admission in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} (PatientResource.java:531-534) AND requires patient.type=='OUTPATIENT' (535-537). A DECEASED patient is blocked only incidentally by the OUTPATIENT requirement, returning the generic 'Please change patient type to OUTPATIENT' message — there is NO 'PATIENT_IS_DECEASED' guard (PatientServiceImpl.java:425-455 never reads type). Test booking attempts for DECEASED/INPATIENT/OUTSIDER/NONE types.
follow_up request param accepts ONLY '1' or '0'; any other value throws InvalidEntryException 'Follow up should be equal to 1 or 0' (PatientResource.java:515-522). Test '2', 'true', empty, null.

INSURANCE two-plan guard: if paymentType==INSURANCE, the resolved plan id differs from the patient's current plan id, and a PENDING PatientInvoice exists, doConsultation throws 'Use of two or more insurance plan...' (PatientServiceImpl.java:537-549). Also: ALL the patient's PENDING PatientInvoices are flipped to APPROVED before the cover block runs (586-590). Plan re-resolved by name; absent -> 'Insurance plan not found in database' (530-533). Test multi-plan switching and pre-existing pending invoices.

INSURANCE consultation requires a ConsultationInsurancePlan for (clinic, plan, covered=true); absent -> 'Plan not available for this clinic' (PatientServiceImpl.java:597-616). Bill becomes amount=paid=plan.consultationFee, balance=0, status COVERED (or NONE if followUp). Test a covered plan missing for the target clinic.

cancel_consultation only from PENDING (PatientResource.java:611-612); cascades patientBill->CANCELED (627), refunds a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail as REFUNDED + raises a PENDING PatientCreditNote with reference 'Canceled consultation' (636-654), and DELETES the matching PatientInvoiceDetail (659-672). Test cancellation with and without a RECEIVED payment, and with/without an existing invoice detail.

switchToConsultationById flips followUp->false and resets a previously-free (NONE) bill to UNPAID (or COVERED if bill has an insurancePlan) — re-introducing a payment requirement for a previously-free follow-up (PatientResource.java:938-948). Revenue implication: a free follow-up becomes payable. Test that a NONE bill becomes UNPAID/COVERED and the consultation is no longer a follow-up.

Admission force-close: admitting a patient force-sets ALL their Consultations in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} to SIGNED-OUT (PatientServiceImpl.java:1951-1962). The open ClinicianPerformance record created at open-time is NOT explicitly closed here. Test admitting a patient with an active consultation.

HELD status (undocumented in extraction, found in verification): save_deceased_note sets an OUTPATIENT consultation to HELD (PatientResource.java:5753); get_deceased_summary advances HELD->SIGNED-OUT only when status==HELD AND no related PatientInvoiceDetail bill is UNPAID or VERIFIED, then sets Patient.type=DECEASED and note APPROVED (5912-5928). For any non-HELD status get_deceased_summary silently no-ops returning 200 and re-saves the unchanged note. Test the death approval against non-HELD statuses and uncleared bills.

STOPPED status: referenced as an active-consultation status in the change_type guard query {PENDING,IN-PROCESS,STOPPED,HELD} (PatientResource.java:325-333) but NO Consultation.setStatus('STOPPED') was found — STOPPED is set on Admission by inpatient referral (PatientResource.java:5451). Confirm STOPPED is not a Consultation status before modelling it; treat as possibly-dead/defensive for Consultation.

LabTest accept/reject toggle clears the opposite audit group: accept (PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED) clears rejected* incl. rejectComment=''; reject (PENDING|ACCEPTED->REJECTED) clears accepted* (PatientResource.java:3896-3935). Radiology reject does NOT blank rejectComment (no setRejectComment in rejectRadiology), unlike LabTest/Procedure — field-handling asymmetry for migration.

LabTest vs Radiology pipeline divergence: LabTest is ACCEPTED->COLLECTED->VERIFIED (collect requires ACCEPTED; verify requires COLLECTED); Radiology verifies straight ACCEPTED->VERIFIED with no collected step in the active path. A separate collectRadiology endpoint is mapped to the suspicious URL '/patients/collect_radiology111' (PatientResource.java:4317-4333) moving ACCEPTED->COLLECTED but the active verify ignores COLLECTED — treat collect_radiology111 as dead/renamed pending confirmation. Test both pipelines independently; do NOT unify.

Attachment gates differ by order type: LabTest attachment allowed only when status==COLLECTED; Radiology attachment allowed only when status==ACCEPTED; max 5 each (PatientServiceImpl.java:2828-2834, 2927-2933). Attachment DELETE is blocked once parent order is VERIFIED ('already verified', PatientResource.java:6021/6154); downloads are UNGATED. Test attach at wrong state, 6th attachment, delete-after-verify, and unauthenticated download.

Procedure has NO APPROVED state, NO approve/reject/hold endpoint despite held*/rejected* columns existing on the entity (Procedure.java:128-145). Only transitions: save->PENDING, accept (PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED), add_note->VERIFIED, delete (PENDING only). REJECTED is reachable in the accept guard but no reject_procedure endpoint exists — confirm how a Procedure reaches REJECTED. Do NOT build an approve step (planning-doc M14 is fabricated).

Procedure settlement gate is the ONLY explicit in-method bill check: add_note requires patientBill.status in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} else 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified' (PatientResource.java:3408-3414). Lab/Radiology accept/verify endpoints do NOT check the bill — settlement is enforced only at worklist queries and report/note endpoints. A direct API call to accept/verify an UNPAID lab/radiology order bypassing the worklist would succeed. Test direct settlement bypass.

get_procedures_by_patient_id OMITS admissions (queries only consultation+nonConsultation, PatientResource.java:4603), whereas lab/radiology by-patient queries include admissions. An admission-scoped Procedure is created but never surfaces in the by-patient procedure list. Flag as intentional-vs-bug.

Duplicate-order guard exists for LabTest/Radiology/Procedure per consultation/nonConsultation (existsByConsultationAnd<Type>Type) but NOT for admissions (PatientResource.java:1948-1957 etc.). Same asymmetry on diagnosis: consultation WorkingDiagnosis/FinalDiagnosis forbid duplicate (consultation,diagnosisType) pairs ('Duplicate Diagnosis Types is not allowed') but admission diagnosis paths have NO duplicate guard (PatientResource.java:1662-1664,1782-1784 vs 1685-1707,1804-1825). Test duplicates on both consultation and admission contexts.

Prescription initial status is 'NOT-GIVEN' (NOT 'PENDING') (PatientServiceImpl.java:1532). Complete Prescription status set is exactly {NOT-GIVEN, GIVEN}. Because the transfer guard checks prescription.status=='PENDING' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2793-2799), a freshly-created NOT-GIVEN prescription does NOT block a transfer — the prescription branch of that guard is effectively inert. Do NOT reproduce the PharmacySaleOrderDetail PENDING->...->SOLD lifecycle on Prescription.

Prescription dispense (issueMedicine) is all-or-nothing with TWO guards: status must be NOT-GIVEN (PatientResource.java:3217-3219); issued must be > 0 and not below balance ('Invalid issue value', :3221); issued must equal full qty ('You can only issue the prescribed qty', :3224-3225). On success status->GIVEN, issued=qty, balance=0, approvedBy/On/At stamped, issuePharmacy set. Stock must cover qty (:3243-3244). Test partial issue, zero/negative issue, insufficient stock.

HARD duplicate-drug guard on save_prescription (consultation branch) blocks an already-prescribed medicine on the same consultation ('Duplicate drug is not allowed. Consider editing qty', PatientResource.java:2109-2111). The OUTSIDER/nonConsultation branch (:2116) calls existsByConsultationAndMedicine(consultation_.get(), ...) on an EMPTY Optional — latent NPE for outsiders (no existsByNonConsultationAndMedicine method exists). Test outsider prescriptions.

Two SOFT prescribing alerts (advisory free-text, never block): same-medicine-within-30-days reads the LAST GIVEN prescription's approvedAt from an UNORDERED list and appends ' Has Drugs this month.' when days<=30 (PatientResource.java:4480-4521); unfinished-course parses prescription.days (String->int, swallowed on non-numeric) and fires when elapsed days < prescribed days (4528-4580). Both are wrapped in exception-swallowing try/catch. Neither is a 'remaining qty>0' rule. Test boundary days (0, 30, 31), non-numeric days, and the unordered-list non-determinism.

PatientPrescriptionChart (inpatient drug-administration) requires the linked Prescription.status=='GIVEN' else 'Prescription not picked from pharmacy' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544-2546); only for admissions in IN-PROCESS with a nurse present (2536-2538,2564-2577). Chart has NO status field. Test charting against a NOT-GIVEN prescription and for outpatients/outsiders.

ClinicalNote/GeneralExamination are UPSERT-per-encounter for consultation/nonConsultation (@OneToOne, overwrite the existing row) but APPEND for admission (@ManyToOne, multiple rows). saveCG enforces exactly-one-of {consultation,nonConsultation,admission} ('Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously') and requires the payload to carry all three nested encounter objects each with a non-null id sentinel, else NPE before guards run (PatientResource.java:1461-1481). admission loaders return the LAST row of an UNORDERED list (non-deterministic 'current' note). Test multi-encounter payloads, missing nested objects, and multi-row admission notes.

load_clinical_note/general_examination loaders AUTO-CREATE and PERSIST an empty row as a side-effect of a GET, returning HTTP 201 (PatientResource.java:1230-1247 etc.). request_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id dereferences findById(id).get() with no presence check (raw NoSuchElementException/500 for a missing id) and requires PatientVital.status=='SUBMITTED' else 'Vitals already requested or not submitted', copying into a transient GeneralExamination and flipping the vital to ARCHIVED (1320-1346). Test side-effecting GETs, missing ids, and the EMPTY/SUBMITTED/ARCHIVED vital lifecycle.

All vitals (pressure/temperature/pulseRate/weight/height/BMI/BSA/SpO2/respiratoryRate) are persisted as free-text Strings with NO numeric typing, NO range validation, NO server-side BMI/BSA computation (GeneralExamination.java:42-51; PatientResource.java:1563-1588). Test that arbitrary strings (e.g. 'high', '120/80', units) round-trip verbatim.

DiagnosisType can NEVER be deleted via the service (allowDeleteDiagnosisType always returns false, DiagnosisTypeServiceImpl.java:72-90); code and name uniqueness is DB-constraint-only (DataIntegrityViolation surfaces, no friendly error); the 'active' flag defaults false but create forces true and update never touches it. delete_working/final_diagnosis are UNGUARDED GET endpoints doing hard deleteById with no existence check (PatientResource.java:1917-1929). Test duplicate code/name, delete attempts, and hard-delete of a non-existent diagnosis id.

OPD referral (save_referral_plan) is blocked if the consultation has ANY UNPAID LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription bill (UNPAID only); on save it sets consultation->SIGNED-OUT, patient.paymentType->CASH and insurancePlan->null, and the patient remains a bookable OUTPATIENT (PatientResource.java:5460-5528). This is a DIFFERENT bill gate from the death/inpatient gates which reject UNPAID OR VERIFIED. Test the UNPAID-only vs UNPAID-or-VERIFIED asymmetry.

approvedBy/approvedOn on DeceasedNote and ReferralPlan are copied from createdBy/createdOn (the note creator), NOT the approving user/day; only approvedAt reflects the real approval timestamp (PatientResource.java:5905-5907,5628-5630). The 48-hour ARCHIVED sweep (UpdatePatient.java:572-594) is wrapped in an empty catch-all (line 600) that silently swallows any exception, halting the rest of the sweep. Test approver attribution and a malformed record aborting the sweep.

get_deceased_summary invoice handling runs the opposite-branch query against a null entity (findAllByAdmission(adm) before the adm!=null check, PatientResource.java:5851-5852) and the APPROVED-write loops run outside the null guards (5865-5868, 5884-5887) — for a consultation-only note findAllByAdmission(null) executes. Test consultation-only and admission-only death approvals.

doConsultation returns null on success (PatientServiceImpl.java:678) but the controller wraps it as ResponseEntity<Patient> with a null body (PatientResource.java:540); clinic/clinician are resolved by name via .get() with no isPresent guard (525-526) -> NoSuchElementException/500 for a bad name. doConsultation also seeds a NEW Visit (sequence='SUBSEQUENT', status='PENDING') for every booking (501-512). Confirm the new endpoint's response contract and error handling.

get_consultation_transfers returns ALL PENDING transfers system-wide via findByStatus('PENDING') — NOT scoped by patient, clinic, clinician, or user (PatientResource.java:599). Worklists generally are NOT scoped by the performing user: lab/radiology/pharmacy lists are scoped only by patient.type + context status==IN-PROCESS + patientBill.status (INPATIENT also accepts VERIFIED; nurse outpatient also accepts NONE). Test that the destination-clinic worklist is unfiltered.

Empty-invoice cleanup bug in deletePrescription uses 'j = j++' (no-op self-assignment, PatientResource.java:3589) so the parent PatientInvoice is ALWAYS deleted whenever any detail is removed, even if other details still reference it — data-integrity hazard. Test deleting one prescription line from a multi-line invoice.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-BOOK-1 (safety-critical, PHI; RBAC PATIENT-ALL/PATIENT-CREATE/PATIENT-UPDATE per PatientResource.java:509): GIVEN a patient with type=OUTPATIENT, no active Admission in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS}, and no Consultation in {PENDING,TRANSFERED,IN-PROCESS}, WHEN booking a consultation with an active clinician, THEN a Consultation is created with status='PENDING' (PatientServiceImpl.java:494) and a PatientBill amount=clinic.consultationFee, paid=0, balance=fee, qty=1, billItem/description='Consultation', status='UNPAID' (459-469); AND a new Visit sequence='SUBSEQUENT' status='PENDING' is attached (501-512). Verify exact field values and a non-active clinician is rejected with 'Clinician/Doctor not active' (427-429).

AC-BOOK-2 (safety-critical): GIVEN follow_up='1', THEN Consultation.followUp=true AND the consultation PatientBill.status is overridden to 'NONE' (the CR-20 free follow-up bill, PatientServiceImpl.java:467-469). GIVEN follow_up is any value other than '1' or '0', THEN InvalidEntryException 'Follow up should be equal to 1 or 0' (PatientResource.java:515-522). Verify with inputs 0,1,2,'true',empty.

AC-BOOK-3 (safety-critical, financial): GIVEN paymentType='INSURANCE' and a ConsultationInsurancePlan(clinic,plan,covered=true) exists, THEN the bill is repriced to amount=paid=plan.consultationFee, balance=0, status='COVERED' (or 'NONE' if followUp), and a PENDING PatientInvoice + PatientInvoiceDetail (description='Consultation', qty=1) are created/attached (PatientServiceImpl.java:597-674). GIVEN no covered plan for the clinic, THEN 'Plan not available for this clinic'. GIVEN a different plan than the patient's current plan AND a PENDING PatientInvoice exists, THEN 'Use of two or more insurance plan...' (537-549). All the patient's PENDING PatientInvoices are set APPROVED before covering (586-590).

AC-BOOK-4: GIVEN a patient with an active Admission in {PENDING,IN-PROCESS}, WHEN booking, THEN rejected with 'the patient has an active admission' (PatientResource.java:531-534). GIVEN patient.type != 'OUTPATIENT' (including DECEASED), THEN rejected with 'Please change patient type to OUTPATIENT to continue with operation' (535-537) — there is NO PATIENT_IS_DECEASED error. Verify with types INPATIENT/OUTSIDER/DECEASED/NONE.

AC-OPEN-1 (safety-critical, financial — pay-before-service): GIVEN a normal (followUp=false) Consultation with status='PENDING', WHEN open_consultation is called, THEN it succeeds (status->'IN-PROCESS', PatientResource.java:886) ONLY if patientBill.status is 'PAID' or 'COVERED'; otherwise InvalidOperationException 'Could not open. Payment not verified.' (885-896). A ClinicianPerformance record is created on success (889-893). GIVEN status != 'PENDING', THEN 'Could not open. Not a pending consultation.' (898-900). Verify the gate with bill statuses PAID/COVERED/NONE/UNPAID.

AC-OPEN-2 (safety-critical): GIVEN a follow-up Consultation (followUp=true) with status='PENDING', WHEN open_follow_up_consultation is called, THEN it succeeds (status->'IN-PROCESS') if patientBill.status is 'PAID' OR 'COVERED' OR 'NONE' — the NONE free-bill is accepted only here (PatientResource.java:914). GIVEN followUp=false, THEN 'This is not a follow up consultation' (910-912). GIVEN status != 'PENDING', THEN it returns success WITHOUT changing status (no else-throw) — reproduce this silent no-op exactly. A ClinicianPerformance record is created (918-922).

AC-WORKLIST-1: load_pending_consultations_by_clinician_id returns rows filtered by the clinicianId param AND followUp=false AND status='PENDING', POST-filtered in Java to keep ONLY rows whose patientBill.status is 'PAID' or 'COVERED' (PatientResource.java:807-827). An unpaid PENDING consultation is NOT shown to the doctor (second pay-before-service filter). Verify a PENDING+UNPAID consultation is hidden and a PENDING+PAID one is shown.

AC-WORKLIST-2: load_follow_up_list_by_clinician_id filters clinician + followUp=true + status IN {PENDING,IN-PROCESS} with NO bill filter (PatientResource.java:841-851). load_in_process_consultations_by_clinician_id filters clinician + followUp=false + status IN {IN-PROCESS,TRANSFERED} with no bill filter (859-877). Verify presence/absence of the bill filter per queue.

AC-WORKLIST-3: lab/radiology/pharmacy worklists are split outpatient/inpatient/outsider; each keys off context status=='IN-PROCESS' + patient.type (OUTPATIENT/INPATIENT/OUTSIDER) + patientBill.status. OUTPATIENT/OUTSIDER accept {PAID,COVERED}; INPATIENT additionally accepts 'VERIFIED' (PatientResource.java:3668-3768,4338-4388). Procedure worklist has ONLY outpatient+outsider endpoints. Verify the INPATIENT-VERIFIED allowance and that no inpatient procedure worklist endpoint exists. Self-URI copy-paste artefacts (procedure lists returning get_lab_*_list URIs at 3783/3800) are Location-header cosmetics — confirm no client dependency before changing.

AC-TRANSFER-1 (safety-critical): GIVEN a Consultation status=='IN-PROCESS', WHEN create_consultation_transfer to a DIFFERENT clinic, THEN source Consultation->'TRANSFERED' and a ConsultationTransfer status='PENDING' is created (PatientServiceImpl.java:2808-2819). Rejected if: source not IN-PROCESS ('Can not transfer. Not an active consultation'), patient already has a PENDING transfer, any PENDING LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription exists on the consultation, or destination clinic == source clinic (2756-2806). Verify each rejection independently.

AC-TRANSFER-2 (safety-critical): GIVEN a patient with a PENDING ConsultationTransfer, WHEN re-sent via do_consultation, THEN the destination clinic must equal transfer.clinic (else 'send the patient to the specified clinic', PatientServiceImpl.java:431-435); on match the transfer is set 'COMPLETED' and a NEW Consultation 'PENDING' is created for the destination clinician. PRECONDITION: the source TRANSFERED consultation must have been SIGNED-OUT first (via free_consultation) or the re-send is blocked by the {PENDING,TRANSFERED} guard (444-450). Verify the full transfer cycle succeeds only when the source is signed out.

AC-TRANSFER-3: GIVEN a Consultation status=='TRANSFERED' with a PENDING ConsultationTransfer, WHEN cancel_consultation_transfer, THEN the transfer->'CANCELED' and the source Consultation reverts to 'IN-PROCESS' (NOT PENDING) without deleting the transfer row (PatientResource.java:989-996). GIVEN status != 'TRANSFERED', THEN silent no-op returning the consultation unchanged. Verify the revert target is IN-PROCESS.

AC-FREE-1 (safety-critical, financial): GIVEN a Consultation status=='IN-PROCESS', WHEN free_consultation with the patient's registration number, THEN status->'SIGNED-OUT' (PatientResource.java:699) AND every child LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription PatientBill that is 'UNPAID' or null-status is set 'CANCELED' (701-754). Rejected if 'no' is empty, the number resolves to no patient, or the patient.id != consultation.patient.id (689-698). GIVEN status=='TRANSFERED', THEN status->'SIGNED-OUT' UNCONDITIONALLY with NO registration-number check and NO child-bill cancellation (687,763-764). Verify both branches distinctly.

AC-CANCEL-1 (safety-critical, financial): GIVEN a Consultation status=='PENDING', WHEN cancel_consultation, THEN status->'CANCELED', patientBill->'CANCELED' (PatientResource.java:618-627); if a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists it is set 'REFUNDED' and a PENDING PatientCreditNote (reference 'Canceled consultation', amount=bill.amount, no from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo()) is raised (636-654); any matching PatientInvoiceDetail is deleted (659-672). GIVEN status != 'PENDING', THEN 'Could not cancel, only a PENDING consultation can be canceled'. Verify refund only fires for RECEIVED payments.

AC-SWITCH-1 (financial): GIVEN a follow-up Consultation with a NONE bill, WHEN switch_to_consultation_by_consultation_id, THEN followUp->false and patientBill.status is set 'COVERED' if the bill has an insurancePlan else 'UNPAID' (PatientResource.java:938-948) — re-introducing a payment requirement. Verify the previously-free follow-up becomes payable and is no longer a follow-up.

AC-ORDER-LAB-1 (safety-critical): GIVEN exactly one of consultation(OUTPATIENT)/nonConsultation(OUTSIDER)/admission(IN-PROCESS), and no duplicate LabTestType for that context, WHEN save_lab_test, THEN a LabTest status='PENDING' and a PatientBill (qty1, billItem='Lab Test', amount=type.price, status='UNPAID'; or COVERED with PENDING invoice for covered insurance; or VERIFIED for admission without covered plan) are created (PatientServiceImpl.java:819-918). Transitions: accept (PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED, clears reject fields), reject (PENDING|ACCEPTED->REJECTED, clears accept fields), collect (ACCEPTED->COLLECTED), verify (COLLECTED->VERIFIED writing result/level/range/unit), hold (ACCEPTED->PENDING), delete (PENDING only, credit-note on RECEIVED payment). Verify each guard rejects out-of-state input.

AC-ORDER-RAD-1 (safety-critical): Radiology transitions: save->PENDING, accept (PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED), reject (PENDING|ACCEPTED->REJECTED), hold (ACCEPTED->PENDING), verify (ACCEPTED->VERIFIED writing result+attachment) — verify goes straight from ACCEPTED with NO COLLECTED step (PatientResource.java:4280-4292). Attachment allowed only when status==ACCEPTED, max 5 (PatientServiceImpl.java:2927-2933). Treat collect_radiology111 (4317-4333) as dead/renamed unless confirmed live. Verify the pipeline differs from LabTest and is NOT unified.

AC-ORDER-PROC-1 (safety-critical, financial): Procedure transitions: save->PENDING, accept (PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED), add_note->VERIFIED. add_note requires a non-empty note AND patientBill.status in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} else 'Could not add procedure note. Payment not verified' (PatientResource.java:3408-3414); update_procedure edits the note only when status==ACCEPTED without finalizing. delete is PENDING-only and issues a PatientCreditNote on a RECEIVED payment (3486-3512). There is NO APPROVED state and NO approve/reject/hold endpoint — do not implement them. Verify the settlement gate and the absence of an approve step.

AC-SETTLEMENT-1 (safety-critical, financial): lab/radiology accept/verify endpoints do NOT check the bill — they check only the order's own status. The settlement gate is enforced at (a) worklist queries (only PAID/COVERED, plus VERIFIED for inpatient, are surfaced) and (b) report/note-add endpoints requiring patientBill.status in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} (PatientResource.java:3389,3191,3408). Document this exactly; any new hard gate at accept/verify is a change request (see CR), not legacy reproduction.

AC-DIAG-1: GIVEN a consultation and a DiagnosisType that exist, WHEN save_working_diagnosis (or save_final_diagnosis), THEN a row is created with patient copied from consultation.getPatient(); a duplicate (consultation,diagnosisType) is rejected with 'Duplicate Diagnosis Types is not allowed' (PatientResource.java:1654-1678,1774-1797). Admission diagnosis paths have NO duplicate guard — reproduce the asymmetry (admission duplicates allowed). diagnosisType is updatable=false (edit = delete+re-add); delete is an unguarded hard delete. Verify duplicate handling differs between consultation and admission.

AC-NOTE-1 (PHI; data-classification: clinical free-text): ClinicalNote/GeneralExamination persist as UPSERT for consultation/nonConsultation (one row, overwrite all fields) and APPEND for admission (multiple rows). saveCG must reference exactly one encounter ('Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously' for any co-presence; 'No Admission or Consultation found' if none) (PatientResource.java:1456-1606). All 8 note fields and all 11 vitals are optional free-text Strings with no validation or BMI/BSA computation. Verify upsert vs append and the exactly-one-encounter rule.

AC-VITAL-1: PatientVital lifecycle EMPTY->SUBMITTED->ARCHIVED. load_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id auto-creates EMPTY if none exists (PatientResource.java:1298-1307); request_patient_vitals_by_consultation_id requires status=='SUBMITTED' else 'Vitals already requested or not submitted', copies fields into a transient (non-persisted) GeneralExamination, and flips the PatientVital to 'ARCHIVED' (1321-1346). Verify the state guard and that re-pulling an ARCHIVED vital fails.

AC-RX-1 (safety-critical): GIVEN exactly one of consultation(OUTPATIENT)/nonConsultation(OUTSIDER)/admission(IN-PROCESS), an existing medicine, and no duplicate medicine on the same consultation, WHEN save_prescription, THEN a Prescription status='NOT-GIVEN' (PatientServiceImpl.java:1532) with issued=0, balance=qty, and a PatientBill amount=medicine.price*qty status='UNPAID' (or COVERED via covered MedicineInsurancePlan with PENDING invoice; or VERIFIED for inpatient without covered plan) are created. The complete Prescription status set is {NOT-GIVEN, GIVEN} — do not add any other status. Verify a duplicate medicine is rejected with 'Duplicate drug is not allowed. Consider editing qty'.

AC-RX-2 (safety-critical): GIVEN a Prescription status=='NOT-GIVEN' with sufficient stock, WHEN issueMedicine with issued==full qty, THEN status->'GIVEN', issued=qty, balance=0, issuePharmacy set, approvedBy/On/At stamped (PatientResource.java:3217-3235). Rejected if: status != NOT-GIVEN ('not a pending prescription'); issued<=0 or below balance ('Invalid issue value', :3221); issued != qty ('You can only issue the prescribed qty', :3224-3225); stock < qty (:3243). GIVEN is terminal. Verify partial/zero/over-issue and insufficient-stock rejections.

AC-RX-3: GIVEN prior GIVEN prescriptions for the same patient+medicine, WHEN the same-medicine alert endpoint is called, THEN it appends ' Has Drugs this month.' iff days-since-last-GIVEN-approvedAt <= 30 (PatientResource.java:4480-4521); WHEN the unfinished-course alert is called, THEN it returns the advisory iff elapsed days < Integer.valueOf(prescription.days) (4528-4580). Both are advisory free-text only and NEVER block; both swallow exceptions to empty string. Verify boundary days (0,30,31), non-numeric days (alert disabled), and that no action is blocked.

AC-CHART-1 (PHI, safety-critical): GIVEN an admission status=='IN-PROCESS', a nurse present, and a linked Prescription status=='GIVEN', WHEN save_patient_prescription_chart, THEN a PatientPrescriptionChart row is created (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544-2577). Rejected if: prescription not GIVEN ('Prescription not picked from pharmacy'); nurse missing ('Nurse information not found'); patient is outpatient/outsider; admission not IN-PROCESS. Verify each guard.

AC-DEATH-1 (safety-critical, financial, PHI): GIVEN an OUTPATIENT consultation, WHEN save_deceased_note with non-empty patientSummary AND causeOfDeath AND exactly one of consultation/admission, THEN the consultation->'HELD' and a DeceasedNote status='PENDING' is created (PatientResource.java:5743-5771). WHEN get_deceased_summary AND the linked consultation status=='HELD' AND no related PatientInvoiceDetail bill is UNPAID or VERIFIED, THEN consultation->'SIGNED-OUT', Patient.type->'DECEASED' (the only death marker; there is no boolean), DeceasedNote->'APPROVED', and related PatientInvoices->'APPROVED' (5912-5928). For any non-HELD status it silently no-ops (200) re-saving the unchanged note. approvedBy/approvedOn are copied from createdBy/createdOn (creator, not approver). Verify the bill gate and the HELD precondition.
