AC-REFERRAL-1 (safety-critical, financial): GIVEN an OUTPATIENT consultation with NO UNPAID LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription bill (UNPAID only, not VERIFIED) AND no existing PENDING ReferralPlan AND an existing ExternalMedicalProvider, WHEN save_referral_plan, THEN consultation->'SIGNED-OUT', patient.paymentType->'CASH', patient.insurancePlan->null, and a ReferralPlan status='PENDING' is created (PatientResource.java:5460-5528). The patient remains a bookable OUTPATIENT (NOT deceased). WHEN get_referral_summary, THEN ReferralPlan->'APPROVED' and Patient.type->'OUTPATIENT' (5670-5677). Verify insurance is cleared and the patient is re-bookable.

AC-AUDIT-1 (net-new, safety-critical): Legacy has NO Envers audit trail (zero @Audited entities) and NO device-binding. The rebuild's tamper-evident audit trail is net-new (per engagement principle) and MUST cover every clinical state transition (open/transfer/cancel/free/accept/reject/collect/verify/hold/dispense/death/referral), capturing actor, timestamp, before/after status, and entity id. This is NOT a legacy behaviour to port; it is a net-new gate approved via change request (see CR). Verify each transition emits an audit event.

AC-NUMBERING-1: NO consultation, transfer, or clinical order (LabTest/Radiology/Procedure/Prescription) carries a human-facing document number in legacy — each is identified by its IDENTITY surrogate id only (no CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} / ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq}; those planning-doc schemes do not exist). The only generated numbers are billing artefacts (PatientCreditNote.no via requestPatientCreditNoteNo; PatientInvoice.no = id-as-string after a transient Math.random() seed). Any clinical document numbering in the rebuild is net-new and requires a change request. Verify no clinical entity sets a 'no' field.
changeRequests: CR-INC05-01 (net-new hardening) — Pay-before-service HARD gate at accept/verify. Legacy enforces settlement only via worklist filtering and report/note-add endpoints; lab/radiology accept/verify methods do NOT check the bill, so a direct API call bypassing the worklist can accept/verify an UNPAID order (PatientResource.java:3896-3909,4208-4221 have no bill check; only Procedure add_note checks the bill at 3408-3414). Rationale: in a modern API-first architecture the worklist filter is not a security boundary; reproducing the gap is a clinical-safety/financial risk. PROPOSAL: add an explicit patientBill-status gate at the lab/radiology accept/collect/verify transitions matching the worklist rule (PAID/COVERED, plus VERIFIED for inpatient). This is a NET-NEW gate, not legacy reproduction — requires engagement-lead approval before implementation.

CR-INC05-02 (net-new hardening) — Method-level RBAC on clinical endpoints. Legacy clinical-workflow endpoints carry NO active @PreAuthorize: open_consultation, open_follow_up_consultation, create/cancel_consultation_transfer, all accept/reject/collect/verify/hold endpoints, all worklists, save_working/final_diagnosis, save_lab_test/radiology/procedure/prescription, and the death/referral endpoints are authentication-only (annotations absent or commented out). The only real codes are PATIENT-ALL/PATIENT-CREATE/PATIENT-UPDATE on do_consultation/switch/cancel/free (509,548,606,681) and ADMIN-ACCESS on diagnosis_types/save (DiagnosisTypeResource.java:74). Planning-doc codes (CONSULTATION_START, ORDER_RAISE, PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE, PRESCRIPTION_CREATE) DO NOT EXIST. Rationale: leaving clinical state-mutating actions unauthenticated-beyond-login is a PHI/safety risk. PROPOSAL: introduce real per-action privileges (new codes) for the clinical lifecycle. This is NET-NEW behaviour requiring security-architect design and engagement-lead approval; do NOT silently invent privilege names.

CR-INC05-03 (net-new hardening) — Tamper-evident audit trail for clinical transitions. Legacy has zero @Audited entities (Envers dependency declared but unused) and relies on hand-rolled createdBy/createdOn/createdAt and *By/*On/*At triplets; several flows (e.g. DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan approval) copy approvedBy/approvedOn from the CREATOR not the approver (PatientResource.java:5905-5907,5628-5630), so the approver's identity is lost. Rationale: the engagement principle mandates a net-new tamper-evident audit trail for PHI/financial correctness. PROPOSAL: emit immutable audit events for every clinical/financial transition capturing the true actor, day, timestamp, and before/after state. NET-NEW; not a behaviour to port. Approver attribution must be corrected (record the real approver) — flag the legacy creator-as-approver as a defect to fix under this CR.

CR-INC05-04 (reproduce-legacy by default; candidate fix) — Long-id reference-equality (==) bugs. Same-clinic and clinic-match guards compare Long ids with '==' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2804 raise-time; :433 accept-time) and free_consultation compares patient ids with '!=' (PatientResource.java:696). For ids outside the Integer cache (-128..127) these are reference comparisons and may misbehave. Rationale: exact-process says preserve observable behaviour, but this is a latent correctness defect that produces WRONG clinical routing for clinic/patient ids >127. PROPOSAL: fix to value equality (.equals/.compareTo). Because the corrected behaviour differs from the (buggy) legacy at large ids, log as a CR; default is reproduce-legacy unless engagement-lead approves the fix. Recommend approval given safety impact.

CR-INC05-05 (reproduce-legacy by default; candidate fix) — OUTSIDER duplicate-drug NPE. save_prescription's OUTSIDER/nonConsultation branch calls existsByConsultationAndMedicine(consultation_.get(),...) on an empty Optional, causing a NPE rather than evaluating the duplicate (PatientResource.java:2116); no existsByNonConsultationAndMedicine method exists. Rationale: this is a latent crash, not a deliberate rule. PROPOSAL: either reproduce the NPE (faithful) or apply a proper nonConsultation duplicate check (corrected). Default reproduce-legacy pending decision; recommend the corrected nonConsultation guard.

CR-INC05-06 (reproduce-legacy by default; candidate fix) — Side-effecting GET loaders. load_clinical_note/general_examination loaders PERSIST a new empty row as a side effect of a GET (HTTP 201) (PatientResource.java:1230-1247 etc.); get_deceased_summary/get_referral_summary are GETs that mutate consultation/admission/patient state and approve invoices. Rationale: side-effecting GETs violate REST/idempotency and complicate the modern API; but changing the verb/contract alters observable behaviour for existing clients. PROPOSAL: decide per endpoint whether to preserve the side-effecting GET (faithful) or convert to POST/PUT with explicit create/approve semantics. Requires engagement-lead + frontend-engineer agreement; default reproduce-legacy.

CR-INC05-07 (reproduce-legacy by default) — Duplicate-diagnosis guard asymmetry. Consultation diagnosis paths forbid duplicate (consultation,diagnosisType) pairs; admission diagnosis paths have NO duplicate guard (PatientResource.java:1662-1664,1782-1784 vs 1685-1707,1804-1825). Rationale: a 'clean' unified guard would silently change admission behaviour. PROPOSAL: reproduce the asymmetry exactly by default; if the healthcare-domain-expert deems admission duplicates clinically wrong, raise a deliberate deviation CR. Do NOT auto-unify.

CR-INC05-08 (reproduce-legacy by default; candidate fix) — Empty-invoice deletion bug ('j = j++'). deletePrescription's cleanup loop uses 'j = j++' (no-op), so the parent PatientInvoice is ALWAYS deleted whenever any detail is removed, even with surviving details (PatientResource.java:3589-3592). Rationale: data-integrity hazard (orphaning surviving invoice details). PROPOSAL: fix the counter so the invoice is deleted only when truly empty. Differs from legacy -> CR; recommend approval. Flag to data-architect/data-migration-engineer for legacy data already affected.

CR-INC05-09 (reproduce-legacy by default) — Bill-gate inconsistency across closure flows. Death/inpatient closure gates reject UNPAID OR VERIFIED bills; OPD-referral save/summary gates reject ONLY UNPAID (PatientResource.java:5857/5876/5598 vs 5468/5637). Rationale: inconsistent revenue-leakage tolerance between closure types. PROPOSAL: preserve both gates exactly by default; if a single consistent rule is desired, raise a deliberate CR with finance sign-off. Do not silently harmonize.

CR-INC05-10 (net-new hardening) — Deceased-patient booking guard. Legacy has NO PATIENT_IS_DECEASED guard; a DECEASED patient is blocked only incidentally by the OUTPATIENT-type requirement, with a misleading message (PatientResource.java:535-537; doConsultation never reads type). Rationale: a clinically explicit deceased guard improves safety and message clarity. PROPOSAL: add an explicit deceased-patient block with a clear error. NET-NEW; requires engagement-lead approval. Until approved, reproduce the incidental block exactly.

CR-INC05-11 (reproduce-legacy by default) — Empty catch-all swallowing in day-rollover archival sweep. The 48h ARCHIVED sweep for DischargePlan/DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan is wrapped in try{...}catch(Exception e){} with an empty body (UpdatePatient.java:600), silently aborting the remainder of the sweep on any bad record. Rationale: silent failure of a batch that touches clinical closure records is an operational risk. PROPOSAL: at minimum log/observe the swallowed exception (the sweep itself likely belongs to a day-management bounded context, not inc-05) — confirm ownership with solution-architect. Behaviour change (logging/continue-on-error) is a CR.

CR-INC05-12 (reproduce-legacy by default) — Unordered 'last row' selection. The same-medicine alert reads the LAST element of an unordered findAllByPatientAndMedicineAndStatus list (PatientResource.java:4498-4500), and admission ClinicalNote/GeneralExamination loaders return the last row of an unordered list (1360-1364,1426-1430). Rationale: non-deterministic 'current' selection w.r.t. DB return order. PROPOSAL: define deterministic ordering (e.g. max(approvedAt) for the alert; ORDER BY id/createdAt desc for the loaders). Preserves the same business intent but is technically a behaviour change -> CR; recommend approval (the alert math and note-field set are unchanged).

CR-INC05-13 (data-type change, pre-approved pattern) — Vitals free-text Strings to typed values. All vitals incl. BMI/BSA are free-text String columns with no validation/computation (GeneralExamination.java:42-51). Per the pre-approved double->BigDecimal / data-model-may-change scope, the rebuild may store numeric vitals as typed/validated fields. Rationale: improves data quality without changing process. PROPOSAL: migrate to typed numeric fields with validation AND preserve verbatim any non-numeric legacy values during migration (reconciliation required). Confirm with data-architect/data-migration-engineer; the alert/exam MATH must remain exact. Distinct from inventing server-side BMI computation (which would be net-new and needs separate approval).

CR-INC05-14 (reproduce-legacy by default) — collect_radiology111 dead/renamed endpoint. A collectRadiology endpoint is mapped to '/patients/collect_radiology111' moving ACCEPTED->COLLECTED, but the active radiology verify path goes straight ACCEPTED->VERIFIED ignoring COLLECTED (PatientResource.java:4280-4292 vs 4317-4333). Rationale: ambiguous whether COLLECTED is a live radiology state. PROPOSAL: pending healthcare-domain-expert confirmation, treat radiology as ACCEPTED->VERIFIED (no COLLECTED) and drop the '111' endpoint as dead. Dropping an endpoint is a scope decision -> CR; do not reproduce the malformed URL.

CR-INC05-15 (reproduce-legacy by default) — Procedure REJECTED reachability and admission worklist gap. acceptProcedure allows from REJECTED but no reject_procedure endpoint exists (PatientResource.java:4034); get_procedures_by_patient_id omits admission-scoped procedures (4603) though admission procedures can be created. Rationale: a procedure can never reach REJECTED and admission procedures are invisible in the by-patient view. PROPOSAL: confirm with healthcare-domain-expert whether these are intended gaps (reproduce) or defects (add reject_procedure / include admissions in the query). Default reproduce-legacy; any addition is net-new scope.
notes: SOLID facts (high-confidence, verified with file:line in both extraction and verification): Consultation.status is a free-text String with values PENDING / IN-PROCESS / TRANSFERED / HELD / SIGNED-OUT / CANCELED (no enum; no BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED). The pay-before-service open gate (PAID|COVERED, +NONE for follow-up) is solid. The CR-20 NONE follow-up bill is solid. Three separate order entities (LabTest/Radiology/Procedure) + Prescription — NO polymorphic ClinicalOrder, NO OrderResult entity. Two separate diagnosis entities (WorkingDiagnosis/FinalDiagnosis) — NO discriminated ConsultationDiagnosis. Prescription status set is exactly {NOT-GIVEN, GIVEN} (the PENDING->...->SOLD lifecycle belongs to PharmacySaleOrderDetail). Death is recorded by Patient.type='DECEASED' String (no boolean). NO clinical document numbering anywhere. NO active @PreAuthorize on the clinical lifecycle; the only real codes are PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE and ADMIN-ACCESS. NO Envers audit, NO device-binding, NO ICD coding.

SOLID corrections from verification that the BA backlog MUST adopt: (1) HELD is a real sixth Consultation status (deceased-note flow) — the state machine MUST include it; (2) free_consultation DOES sign out a TRANSFERED consultation unconditionally (the extraction inverted the control flow) — this RESOLVES the transfer-accept reachability conflict; (3) Prescription initial status is NOT-GIVEN, not PENDING; (4) additional SIGNED-OUT entry points exist via referral (PatientResource.java:5501,5670) and admission force-close (PatientServiceImpl.java:1956); (5) approvedBy/approvedOn copy the creator not the approver on DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan.

OPEN questions (NOT yet resolved — do NOT author final acceptance criteria that assume an answer; raise to legacy-analyst / healthcare-domain-expert / engagement-lead): (a) terminal status of a source TRANSFERED consultation if it is never freed/admitted; (b) whether STOPPED is ever a Consultation status (only found on Admission) — the change_type guard queries it; (c) whether COLLECTED is a live Radiology state or collect_radiology111 is dead; (d) how a Procedure reaches REJECTED with no reject endpoint; (e) whether admission-scoped procedures should appear in get_procedures_by_patient_id; (f) whether the silent no-op of get_deceased_summary on non-HELD status is required; (g) whether PrescriptionBatch traceability is an active requirement or a dormant table to migrate as-is; (h) the canonical billing invoice-number generator that should replace the Math.random()->id pattern.

SCOPE boundary reminder: this is INSURANCE PRICING-per-service-type reproduction only (ConsultationInsurancePlan / LabTestInsurancePlan / RadiologyInsurancePlan / ProcedureInsurancePlan / MedicineInsurancePlan covered-flag repricing). There is NO claims-submission workflow in legacy (confirmed M22). Any Insurance/Claims story beyond reproducing this pricing logic is NEW SCOPE, must be logged in the change-request register, and its acceptance criteria must state it is net-new — never written as if reproducing legacy. None of the inc-05 clinical stories above introduce claims submission.

Data-classification: every story touching Consultation/ClinicalNote/GeneralExamination/PatientVital/WorkingDiagnosis/FinalDiagnosis/Prescription/DeceasedNote contains PHI/PII — anonymized examples only in artifacts; attach the relevant RBAC privilege note. Tag all booking/open/transfer/cancel/free/order/dispense/death/referral and all insurance-pricing stories 'safety-critical' (clinical and/or financial correctness).

Data-continuity flags for data-migration-engineer: preserve exact legacy status spellings (IN-PROCESS hyphen, TRANSFERED single-R, CANCELED single-L) for any external integration unless a mapping CR is approved; preserve the field-name casing divergence (LabTest acceptedBy camelCase vs Radiology/Procedure acceptedby lowercase) at the DB-column level (columns like ordered_by_user_id are shared); reconcile the 'j=j++' empty-invoice deletion bug's effect on historical invoices (CR-INC05-08); reconcile non-numeric legacy vitals when migrating to typed fields (CR-INC05-13).

Traceability anchors (legacy class/service -> bounded context Clinical/OPD): Consultation lifecycle = PatientServiceImpl.doConsultation + PatientResource open/free/cancel/transfer; orders = PatientServiceImpl.saveLabTest/saveRadiology/saveProcedure + PatientResource accept/reject/collect/verify/hold; prescriptions = PatientServiceImpl.savePrescription + PatientResource issueMedicine + alerts inline at 4480-4580; notes/exam/diagnosis = PatientResource saveCG/saveWorking/saveFinalDiagnosis + DiagnosisTypeServiceImpl; closure = PatientResource save_deceased_note/get_deceased_summary/save_referral_plan/get_referral_summary + UpdatePatient archival sweep. ClinicianPerformance is created as a side-effect of open (PatientResource.java:889-893,918-922) and must be linked in the traceability matrix to a performance-reporting story.

Inc-05 ownership/seams: the local clinical 'settled' flag is written by SettlementDispatcher.onBillPaid in the same tx (billing->encounter, no reverse edge); inc-05 should consume billing.api SettlementPolicy.requireSettled rather than re-deriving the PAID/COVERED gate. The registration.domain.Consultation stub (PENDING only, CR-21) is taken over by inc-05 and must be expanded to the full status set incl. HELD; reassignClinician() is the inc-05 extension point for clinician reassignment (Consultation.clinician is updatable=true). Flyway for inc-05 starts at V20.

synthesis.summary: RATIFIED inc-05 (Clinical/OPD) build spec — legacy-faithful, drift resolved in favour of the legacy. Inc-05 takes ownership of the registration Consultation stub (CR-21) and builds the full OPD clinical context: consultation lifecycle (6 statuses), non-consultation walk-in track, consultation transfer, clinical notes/general exam/patient-vital staging, two separate diagnosis aggregates (working/final), three separate order aggregates (lab/radiology/procedure) + prescription, prescription chart, deceased-note and referral-plan closure. KEY RATIFIED FACTS, all legacy-cited: (1) Consultation.status is a free-text-equivalent value set PENDING / IN-PROCESS / TRANSFERED / CANCELED / SIGNED-OUT / HELD (modelled as a String-backed enum preserving EXACT hyphenated/single-R/single-L spellings; planning-doc BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED REJECTED as inventions; STOPPED is query-only ghost, NOT a writable Consultation status, EXCLUDED). (2) THREE separate order tables (lab_tests/radiologies/procedures) + prescriptions — NO polymorphic ClinicalOrder; NO OrderResult entity (results are columns). (3) TWO separate diagnosis tables (working_diagnoses/final_diagnoses) — NO discriminated ConsultationDiagnosis. (4) Prescription status is EXACTLY {NOT-GIVEN, GIVEN}; the PENDING→…→SOLD lifecycle belongs to PharmacySaleOrderDetail and is OUT OF SCOPE. (5) NO clinical document numbering anywhere — IDENTITY id + ULID uid only; CONS-/ORD- schemes REJECTED. (6) Death = Patient.type='DECEASED' String, no boolean. (7) Pay-before-service is enforced ONLY at the open transition + worklist filter (parity); per-transition hardening is a CR. (8) Legacy RBAC on clinical endpoints is almost entirely authentication-only — only do_consultation/switch/cancel/free carry PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE and diagnosis_types/save carries ADMIN-ACCESS; planning-doc privilege names (CONSULTATION_START etc.) REJECTED. (9) No Envers/@Audited, no device-binding, no ICD — confirmed across two independent extractions. The settlement seam consumes billing.api SettlementPolicy.requireSettled against a LOCAL settled flag written only by SettlementDispatcher.onBillPaid (billing→encounter, same tx, no reverse edge); inc-05 must also drive the local flag for COVERED/NONE/inpatient at booking/charge time since onBillPaid fires only on the cash PAID transition. Flyway V20 widens the existing V19 consultations status CHECK and adds membership_no + insurance_plan_uid; V21-V28 create the remaining clinical tables; V29 (audit DDL) is DEFERRED pending security-architect PHI classification. Build is sequenced C1..C12, schema-first then per-aggregate, each independently mvn-clean-verify green. Six items genuinely require the human engagement owner (RBAC tightening, settlement hard-gate at accept/verify, deceased-rebooking guard, allergy-check gate, closure bill-gate harmonisation, side-effecting-GET contract) — surfaced as NEEDS_HUMAN, never auto-decided.
aggregates: Consultation -> table consultations (EXTEND existing V19 stub, CR-21 ownership transfer to clinical module); owns the OPD encounter lifecycle, status enum widened to 6 values (Consultation.java:47-110)
NonConsultation -> table non_consultations (NEW); walk-in/no-doctor track, no bill/clinic/clinician/followUp (NonConsultation.java:44-80)

PENDING|ACCEPTED->REJECTED via reject_lab_test [clears accept*]. ACCEPTED->COLLECTED via collect_lab_test. COLLECTED->VERIFIED via verify_lab_test [writes result/level/range/unit]. ACCEPTED->PENDING via hold_lab_test [sets held* then reverts]. PENDING->(deleted) via delete_lab_test [credit-note on RECEIVED payment]. ACCEPTED->COLLECTED via collect_radiology111 [DEAD/renamed endpoint, active verify ignores COLLECTED — keep COLLECTED in CHECK for fidelity but do NOT expose the '111' URL]. PENDING->(deleted) [credit-note on RECEIVED]. Attachment allowed ONLY when status==ACCEPTED (different gate than lab), max 5.

NO APPROVED state, NO approve/reject/hold endpoint (held* cols vestigial). REJECTED reachable in accept guard but no reject_procedure endpoint exists — open question.

synthesis.settlementWiring: Inc-05 consumes billing.api.SettlementPolicy.requireSettled(settled, paymentType, inpatient, emergency, billUid) at its OWN open transition (open_consultation / open_follow_up_consultation) evaluated against the clinical-LOCAL settled flag — it NEVER calls back into billing to read bill status (no reverse edge; ADR-0008 §6, confirmed SettlementDispatcher.java:18-20). The local flag is written ONLY in the billing->encounter direction by SettlementDispatcher.onBillPaid(bill, ctx) (SettlementDispatcher.java:31-35), invoked by PaymentService in the SAME tx as bill.markSettled — the documented inc-05/06 seam (SettlementDispatcher.java:33-34 'also set settled=true on the downstream encounter/order LOCAL projection'). PARITY MAPPING of the legacy open gate (PatientResource.java:884-896 PAID|COVERED; open_follow_up additionally NONE @914): SettlementPolicy.requiresPrepayment returns true ONLY for CASH+non-inpatient+non-emergency (SettlementPolicy.java:36-47), which maps the legacy 'CASH outpatient must be PAID' case to the only blocked outcome; INSURANCE(COVERED) auto-passes; inpatient passes (legacy worklists also accept VERIFIED for inpatient @3693); follow-up NONE passes because the local settled flag is set at booking for the followUp NONE-bill path (CR-20). CRITICAL: because onBillPaid fires ONLY on the cash PAID transition, inc-05 MUST drive the local settled flag at booking/charge time for the COVERED / NONE / inpatient-VERIFIED cases (they never produce a cash-PAID event) — this is wired in C2 (consultation booking) and the per-order charge paths (C7/C8/C9/C10) by setting settled=true when SettlementPolicy.requiresPrepayment(...) is false at create time. ENFORCEMENT PLACEMENT (legacy-faithful): requireSettled goes at the OPEN transition only, matching legacy which gated pay-before-service at the open gate + worklist UI filter and did NOT re-check at accept/verify (PatientResource.java:3896-3909 have no bill check). Adding a hard gate at every order accept/verify is net-new hardening (CR-INC05-01, NEEDS_HUMAN) — NOT in the default build. The Procedure add_note bill gate (PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED @3408-3414) IS legacy and IS reproduced as-is via a local-flag check at add_note. INVARIANT ENFORCEMENT: an ArchUnit rule + ApplicationModules.verify() must fail the build if any com.otapp.hmis.clinical.* type depends on com.otapp.hmis.billing.* except the published billing.api package (SettlementPolicy/PayBeforeServiceException/BillingCommands/ChargeRequest), and SettlementDispatcher must remain the sole writer of the downstream local settled flag (billing->encounter only). Clinical charge creation goes through BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest, ctx) with ChargeRequest.followUp carrying the CR-20 NONE-bill flag (ChargeRequest.java:23-36).
numbering: NO document numbers anywhere in the clinical module — every aggregate is IDENTITY surrogate id + ULID uid only. Confirmed: grep requestConsultationNo|requestLabTestNo|requestRadiologyNo|requestProcedureNo|requestPrescriptionNo across com.orbix.api = NO matches; no setNo() on any clinical entity (Consultation.java:48-50).
Planning-doc CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} and ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} schemes REJECTED as inventions — do NOT create seq_consultation_no or seq_order_no.

DiagnosisType.code (DiagnosisType.java:41-43), ExternalMedicalProvider.code (ExternalMedicalProvider.java:33-35), PrescriptionBatch.no (PrescriptionBatch.java:38-39) are user-supplied free-text UNIQUE strings — NO generator, NO format mask, NOT ICD.

Side-artefact numbers belong to BILLING, not inc-05: PatientInvoice.no = transient Math.random() then overwritten with id.toString() (a legacy defect — the canonical billing invoice-number generator must replace it, NEEDS_HUMAN to confirm generator); PatientCreditNote.no via patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo() (billing seq_pcn_no, V13/V16 — already exists). NO new sequence created in inc-05.
uid VARCHAR(26) is an application-layer ULID (not a DB sequence), consistent with V15/V19 house convention.
Any clinical document numbering in the rebuild is NET-NEW and requires a change request (AC-NUMBERING-1).
rbac: PATIENT-ALL — REAL (V2__seed_iam.sql:55) -> do_consultation/switch/cancel/free (PatientResource.java:509,548,606,681). Already wired PatientController.java:189.
PATIENT-CREATE — REAL (V2:56) -> same four consultation-lifecycle mutations.
PATIENT-UPDATE — REAL (V2:57) -> same four consultation-lifecycle mutations.

ADMIN-ACCESS — REAL (V2:27) -> diagnosis_types/save ONLY (DiagnosisTypeResource.java:74); already enforced DiagnosisTypeController.java:47,55. The only active clinical-area gate outside the consultation lifecycle.

AUTHENTICATED-ONLY (no privilege gate) — the verified baseline for EVERYTHING else: open_consultation, open_follow_up_consultation, create/cancel/get consultation transfers, all order save/accept/reject/collect/verify/hold, prescription save/issue, working/final diagnosis save/delete (incl. admission variants), all clinical-note/general-exam/patient-vital save+loaders, ALL worklists, save/get deceased-note and referral-plan. Legacy annotations are absent or commented out (PatientResource.java:572,595,879,905,933,983,1224-1929,3888-4388,5393-5838). Reproduce as authenticated-only PARITY (CR-04 reads-authenticated precedent, PatientController.java:65-66).

DRIFT — DO NOT CREATE: CONSULTATION_START, ORDER_RAISE, PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE, PRESCRIPTION_CREATE. Planning-doc 05-clinical-opd.md inventions; ZERO @PreAuthorize sites, ZERO seed rows. The '177 privilege codes' is the count of @PreAuthorize SITES, not distinct codes (real inventory = 35 codes in V2:26-61).

DRIFT — DO NOT CREATE per-action LAB-*/RADIOLOGY-*/PROCEDURE-*/PRESCRIPTION-* codes; none exist in the seed. PHARMACY_ORDER-* (V2:49-51) is procurement, NOT clinical dispense — mapping it onto clinical Prescription issue is an invented boundary.

DEAD-but-seeded (fidelity only, gate nothing): PATIENT-A/PATIENT-C/PATIENT-U (V2:58-60, only in commented-out transfer gates @572,595), PRODUCT-CREATE (V2:61, only commented on save_lab_test @1932). Do NOT promote to active gates without a CR.

Adding real per-action clinical privileges is NET-NEW (CR-INC05-02) requiring security-architect design + engagement-lead approval — surfaced as NEEDS_HUMAN, not auto-decided. Do NOT seed any new V20+ privilege for inc-05 unless that CR is approved.

PHI compensating control: because clinical reads are authenticated-only with no per-record boundary, a PHI read-access-log (who/when/which patient record) is required on worklists/loaders; field set handed to data-architect. No PHI in URLs (ULIDs only), JWT, or error bodies.

V26__clinical_procedures.sql — CREATE TABLE procedures (NO collected/approved cols; hours/minutes NUMERIC(19,6) not double; proc_time TIME, proc_date DATE) + status CHECK (4 values 'PENDING','ACCEPTED','REJECTED','VERIFIED' — NO APPROVED) + theatre_uid partial index + ck_one_encounter. Header cites Procedure.java:40-147, planning-doc M14 approve-step REJECTED as fabricated.

V27__clinical_prescriptions.sql — CREATE TABLE prescriptions (status DEFAULT 'NOT-GIVEN', CHECK IN ('NOT-GIVEN','GIVEN'); qty/issued/balance NUMERIC(19,6); ck_qty_nonneg; approved_* dispense audit) + prescription_batches + patient_prescription_charts + alert composite idx (patient_id,medicine_uid,status) + pharmacy worklist partial idx WHERE status='NOT-GIVEN'. Header cites Prescription.java:38-144, two-status-only finding, PharmacySaleOrderDetail disambiguation.

V28__clinical_closure_deceased_referral.sql — CREATE TABLE deceased_notes + referral_plans (status CHECK PENDING/APPROVED/ARCHIVED; ck_one_encounter; partial-unique per encounter for deceased; list partial indexes WHERE status IN (PENDING,APPROVED)). Header cites DeceasedNote.java:36-76, ReferralPlan.java:35-80, Patient.type='DECEASED' is a String not boolean, approved_by-copies-creator legacy quirk, external_medical_provider_uid loose (table not built).

V29__clinical_audit.sql — DEFERRED / NOT AUTHORED NOW. Append-only audit_log + per-PHI-entity audit tables MUST wait for security-architect's PHI field classification + audit-coverage requirements. Clinical module is PHI-dense; audit will be substantial but the covered set is NOT self-selected. Placeholder version reserved. Legacy has NO Envers/@Audited — audit is greenfield, nothing to migrate.

CONVENTION: each V20-V28 is a single transactional DDL file; parent tables before dependent FKs; constraints named pk_/uq_/fk_/ck_/idx_; header block with bounded-context + ADR/CR refs + legacy file:line citations matching V15/V19 house style. Additive-only after baseline; the only ALTER is V20's status-CHECK widening (constraint-widening, no data loss).

changeRequests.title: Pay-before-service HARD gate at order accept/verify transitions
changeRequests.rationale: Legacy enforces settlement only at the open transition + worklist filter; lab/radiology accept/verify do NOT re-check the bill (PatientResource.java:3896-3909,4208-4221), so a direct API call bypassing the worklist can accept/verify an UNPAID order. In an API-first architecture the worklist filter is not a security boundary, but adding a per-transition gate is NET-NEW patient-safety/financial hardening that changes observable behaviour. Default build places requireSettled at the open transition only (parity). Per-transition enforcement is a scope/safety decision for the engagement owner.

changeRequests.title: Method-level RBAC privileges on clinical endpoints
changeRequests.rationale: Legacy clinical-workflow endpoints (open/transfer/order-lifecycle/diagnosis/notes/worklists/deceased/referral) are authentication-only — annotations absent or commented out. Leaving state-mutating clinical actions and DEATH RECORDING unauthorized-beyond-login is a PHI/safety risk, but introducing real per-action privileges is NET-NEW and requires security-architect design + engagement-lead sign-off. Planning-doc names (CONSULTATION_START etc.) must NOT be invented. Default = reproduce authenticated-only parity until approved.

changeRequests.title: Tamper-evident audit trail for clinical/financial transitions
changeRequests.rationale: Legacy has ZERO @Audited entities (Envers declared-but-unused) and copies approvedBy/approvedOn from the CREATOR not the approver (DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan @5905-5907,5628-5630), losing approver identity. The engagement principle mandates a net-new tamper-evident audit. ACCEPTED in principle as a build requirement, BUT the audit DDL (V29) and covered-entity set are DEFERRED to security-architect's PHI classification — not self-selected here. Approver attribution must be captured from the real authenticated principal. The DDL authoring is gated; the requirement is ratified.

changeRequests.title: Long-id reference-equality (==) routing bugs
changeRequests.rationale: Same-clinic/clinic-match guards compare Long ids with '==' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2804,433) and free_consultation compares patient ids with '!=' (PatientResource.java:696) — reference comparison for ids >127 (outside Integer cache) produces WRONG clinical routing. In the new build clinic/clinician/patient cross-refs are ULID Strings compared with .equals(), so the modern model is correct by construction; the legacy '==' bug cannot be reproduced (no boxed-Long ids exist). ACCEPTED as resolved-by-design — value equality is inherent to the uid model; no boxed-id path to preserve.

changeRequests.title: OUTSIDER duplicate-drug NPE in save_prescription
changeRequests.rationale: Legacy nonConsultation branch calls existsByConsultationAndMedicine(consultation_.get(),...) on an empty Optional -> NPE for outsiders (PatientResource.java:2116); no existsByNonConsultationAndMedicine exists. This is a latent crash, not a deliberate rule. ACCEPTED the corrected behaviour: implement a proper nonConsultation duplicate check (existsByNonConsultationAndMedicine) so outsider prescriptions evaluate the duplicate rule rather than crash. The intended business rule (block duplicate drug per encounter) is preserved and extended to the encounter type that legacy crashed on; recommend, low risk.

changeRequests.title: Side-effecting GET loaders (auto-create / approve-on-GET)
changeRequests.rationale: load_clinical_note/general_examination loaders PERSIST an empty row on a GET (HTTP 201, PatientResource.java:1230-1277); get_deceased_summary/get_referral_summary are GETs that mutate state and approve invoices. Side-effecting GETs violate idempotency and pollute the clinical record with blank notes, but changing verbs/contract alters observable behaviour and the auto-create semantics are relied on by the legacy UI flow. Per-endpoint decision (preserve faithful vs convert to POST/PUT) needs engagement-lead + frontend-engineer agreement. Default = reproduce.

changeRequests.title: Duplicate-diagnosis-type guard consultation-vs-admission asymmetry
changeRequests.rationale: Consultation diagnosis paths forbid duplicate (consultation,diagnosisType) pairs; admission paths have NO guard (PatientResource.java:1662/1782 vs 1685/1804). ACCEPTED to reproduce the asymmetry EXACTLY: app-layer duplicate check on consultation paths only, NO DB unique on (admission_uid,diagnosis_type_uid). Do NOT auto-unify — that would silently change admission behaviour. If HDE later deems admission duplicates clinically wrong, a deliberate deviation CR is raised then.

changeRequests.title: Empty-invoice deletion bug (j = j++ no-op)
changeRequests.rationale: deletePrescription's cleanup uses 'j = j++' (no-op self-assignment) so the parent PatientInvoice is ALWAYS deleted whenever any detail is removed, orphaning surviving details (PatientResource.java:3589-3592). This is a data-integrity hazard, not a deliberate rule. ACCEPTED the corrected behaviour: delete the invoice ONLY when it has no remaining details. Flag to data-migration-engineer to reconcile historical invoices already affected. This cleanup lives in the billing context (invoice ownership); coordinate the fix placement with billing.

changeRequests.title: Closure bill-gate inconsistency (UNPAID vs UNPAID|VERIFIED)
changeRequests.rationale: Death/inpatient closure gates reject {UNPAID,VERIFIED} bills; OPD-referral gates reject {UNPAID} only (PatientResource.java:5857/5876/5598 vs 5468/5637). For NHIF patients, VERIFIED (insurer-pending) bills are legitimately unsettled — harmonising affects which insured patients can close an encounter. Default = preserve both gates exactly. Whether to harmonise to one canonical rule is a finance/NHIF-compliance decision for the engagement owner.

changeRequests.title: Deceased-patient booking guard
changeRequests.rationale: Legacy has NO PATIENT_IS_DECEASED guard; a DECEASED patient is blocked only incidentally by the OUTPATIENT-type requirement with a misleading message (PatientResource.java:535-537); no code reverts type away from DECEASED. HDE warns an irreversible deceased-lock is hazardous (mis-recorded deaths, MRN mix-ups would permanently lock a living patient out of care). Adding an explicit deceased guard requires an approved AUDITED reversal path. NET-NEW; engagement-lead + clinical-governance decision. Default = reproduce the incidental block.

changeRequests.title: Empty catch-all swallows day-rollover archival sweep errors
changeRequests.rationale: The 48h ARCHIVED sweep for DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan/DischargePlan is wrapped in try{...}catch(Exception e){} (UpdatePatient.java:600), silently aborting the rest of the sweep on any bad record. This sweep touches the day-management/business-day bounded context, NOT the inc-05 clinical write paths. DEFERRED to the owning context (business-day/day-rollover increment). For inc-05, document the legacy behaviour; do not reproduce or harden the sweep here.

changeRequests.title: Non-deterministic 'last row' selection (alerts + admission loaders)
changeRequests.rationale: Same-medicine alert reads the LAST element of an unordered list (PatientResource.java:4498-4500); admission note/exam loaders return the last row of an unordered findAllByAdmission (1360-1364,1426-1430). HDE flags the loader case as a patient-safety hazard (clinician may act on a stale note). ACCEPTED deterministic ordering: same-medicine alert = MAX(approvedAt); admission loaders = ORDER BY created_at/id DESC. The alert MATH and field set are unchanged — same business intent, deterministic result. Low risk, recommended.

changeRequests.title: Vitals free-text Strings to typed numeric
changeRequests.rationale: All vitals incl BMI/BSA are free-text Strings with no validation/computation (GeneralExamination.java:42-51). REJECTED for the default inc-05 build: preserve free-text VARCHAR for exact-process fidelity (HDE: the double->BigDecimal pre-approval does NOT extend to silently adding range validation or server-side BMI computation, which would be net-new). Numeric vitals + validation is a SEPARATE future CR with backfill/reconciliation if the engagement owner wants it; not done by default. This keeps round-trip of arbitrary strings (e.g. '120/80','high') verbatim.

changeRequests.title: collect_radiology111 dead/renamed endpoint
changeRequests.rationale: collectRadiology is mapped to '/patients/collect_radiology111' (ACCEPTED->COLLECTED) but the active verify path goes straight ACCEPTED->VERIFIED ignoring COLLECTED (PatientResource.java:4280-4292 vs 4317-4333). ACCEPTED: do NOT expose the malformed '111' endpoint; model radiology as ACCEPTED->VERIFIED. KEEP COLLECTED in the radiologies status CHECK for data fidelity (legacy rows/migration may carry it) but provide no live transition into it. The endpoint is dead — reproducing a malformed URL would be a defect. (Confirm-with-HDE noted but the active pipeline is unambiguous.)

changeRequests.title: Procedure REJECTED reachability + admission procedure worklist gap
changeRequests.rationale: acceptProcedure allows from REJECTED but no reject_procedure endpoint exists (PatientResource.java:4034) — a Procedure can never reach REJECTED; get_procedures_by_patient_id omits admission-scoped procedures (4603) though they can be created. DEFERRED pending healthcare-domain-expert confirmation whether these are intended gaps (reproduce) or defects (add reject_procedure / include admissions). Default = reproduce-legacy (no reject endpoint, admission procedures invisible in by-patient view). Adding either is net-new scope to be sequenced after HDE input.

buildChunks: C1 — SCHEMA FIRST (Flyway V20-V28, no Java yet beyond enum widening). Author V20 (extend consultations: widen status CHECK to 6 values, add membership_no + insurance_plan_uid, add clinician/status/follow_up + insurance indexes) and V21-V28 (all clinical tables). Widen the ConsultationStatus enum to the 6 String-mapped legacy values (PENDING/IN-PROCESS/TRANSFERED/CANCELED/SIGNED-OUT/HELD) with EnumType.STRING preserving exact spellings. Green criteria: mvn clean verify passes; ApplicationModules.verify() still green; Flyway migrate-then-validate on a Testcontainers Postgres 16 succeeds; existing V19 PENDING consultation rows remain valid against the widened CHECK. NO behaviour change yet.

C2 — Consultation lifecycle core (booking + open + free + cancel + switch). Take ownership of the Consultation aggregate from registration (CR-21): implement do_consultation booking (fee bill via BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge with followUp/CR-20 NONE flag, NEW Visit SUBSEQUENT/PENDING, INSURANCE two-plan guard + flip PENDING invoices APPROVED, ConsultationInsurancePlan repricing), open_consultation (pay-before-service via SettlementPolicy.requireSettled at OPEN against local settled flag; set local flag at booking for COVERED/NONE/inpatient), open_follow_up (NONE accepted, silent no-op on non-PENDING), cancel (PENDING-only, refund/credit-note), free (IN-PROCESS reg-no+child-bill-cancel / TRANSFERED unconditional), switch-to-normal/switch-to-consultation. Wire the four REAL PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE guards; everything else authenticated-only. Green: state-machine tests cover all transitions + guards; settlement parity tests (CASH-blocked / INSURANCE-pass / inpatient-pass / follow-up-NONE-pass); ArchUnit clinical->billing.api-only rule green.

C3 — ConsultationTransfer aggregate. create_consultation_transfer (IN-PROCESS->TRANSFERED + transfer PENDING, all four no-open-work guards, destination!=source via uid .equals, one-pending-per-patient partial-unique), cancel_consultation_transfer (TRANSFERED->IN-PROCESS), do_consultation destination pickup completion (transfer->COMPLETED, precondition source SIGNED-OUT), get_consultation_transfers (system-wide PENDING, unscoped). Green: full transfer cycle test asserts source must be freed (SIGNED-OUT) before destination re-send succeeds; one-pending invariant enforced.

C4 — NonConsultation walk-in track. Create non_consultations lazily inside the order save paths; IN-PROCESS/SIGNED-OUT lifecycle. Green: order creation against a nonConsultation context creates/reuses the walk-in row; OUTSIDER patient-type gate enforced.

C5 — ClinicalNote + GeneralExamination + PatientVital. saveCG (exactly-one-encounter guard, upsert for consultation/non-consultation, append for admission), auto-create-on-GET loaders (reproduce HTTP 201 side-effect per CR-INC05-06 default), PatientVital EMPTY/SUBMITTED/ARCHIVED staging + request copy-to-transient-exam. Vitals as free-text VARCHAR (CR-INC05-13 REJECT — no numeric). Admission loaders ORDER BY created_at DESC (CR-INC05-12 ACCEPT). Green: upsert-vs-append tests; exactly-one-encounter rejection; vital state guard; deterministic admission 'current' note.

C6 — WorkingDiagnosis + FinalDiagnosis (two separate aggregates). save_working/final_diagnosis (consultation duplicate guard via existsByConsultationAndDiagnosisType), admission variants (NO duplicate guard — CR-INC05-07 ACCEPT asymmetry), unguarded hard-delete GETs (reproduce default; flag as security finding). diagnosis_type ref is loose uid to masterdata. Green: consultation duplicate rejected; admission duplicate allowed; delete-by-id works; two physically separate tables.

C7 — LabTest aggregate + attachments. save_lab_test (PENDING + bill via recordClinicalCharge; set local settled flag for COVERED/inpatient-VERIFIED at create), accept/reject (toggle clears opposite audit group), collect (ACCEPTED->COLLECTED), verify (COLLECTED->VERIFIED writes result/level/range/unit), hold (ACCEPTED->PENDING), delete (PENDING-only + credit-note on RECEIVED), attachments (max 5, only when COLLECTED, delete-blocked-when-VERIFIED, ungated download). Worklists outpatient/inpatient/outsider (bill PAID|COVERED, +VERIFIED inpatient). Green: full lab pipeline + guards; settlement enforced at worklist not accept/verify (parity, CR-INC05-01 default).

C8 — Radiology aggregate + attachments. save_radiology, accept/reject (reject does NOT blank rejectComment — preserve asymmetry), hold, verify (ACCEPTED->VERIFIED, NO COLLECTED, writes result+BYTEA attachment), delete. collect_radiology111 NOT exposed (CR-INC05-14 ACCEPT, COLLECTED kept in CHECK only). Attachments max 5, only when ACCEPTED. Worklists mirror lab. Green: radiology pipeline asserts ACCEPTED->VERIFIED divergence from lab; attachment gate at ACCEPTED.

C9 — Procedure aggregate. save_procedure (PENDING + bill), accept (PENDING|REJECTED->ACCEPTED), add_note (->VERIFIED, bill gate PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED via local flag — legacy gate reproduced), update_procedure (edit note when ACCEPTED), delete (PENDING-only + credit-note). NO approve/reject/hold endpoints (CR-INC05-15 DEFER). hours/minutes NUMERIC. Worklists outpatient+outsider only (no inpatient list). Green: add_note settlement gate; absence of approve step verified; by-patient query omits admissions (reproduce default).

C10 — Prescription aggregate + batches + charts. save_prescription (NOT-GIVEN + bill, HARD duplicate-drug block, nonConsultation duplicate check per CR-INC05-05 ACCEPT, exactly-one-encounter, admission IN-PROCESS guard, dosage/frequency/route free-text), issueMedicine (NOT-GIVEN->GIVEN, two issue-value guards + all-or-nothing + stock, approved_* dispense audit), deletePrescription (NOT-GIVEN-only + credit-note; invoice-cleanup fixed per CR-INC05-08 ACCEPT, coordinate placement with billing), prescription_batches (inert), patient_prescription_charts (requires linked GIVEN, admission IN-PROCESS, nurse). Green: two-status-only enforced; all-or-nothing dispense; chart guards; duplicate block.

C11 — Prescribing alerts (advisory, non-blocking). same-medicine-30-day (MAX(approvedAt) deterministic per CR-INC05-12, append ' Has Drugs this month.' when days<=30) and unfinished-course (parse days String, fire when elapsed<days, swallow exceptions). Both free-text, never block. Green: boundary tests (0/30/31 days, non-numeric days disables), confirms no action is blocked.

C12 — Closure: DeceasedNote + ReferralPlan. save_deceased_note (->HELD, mandatory summary+causeOfDeath, exactly-one-of consultation/admission, ward-bed free on inpatient), get_deceased_summary (HELD->SIGNED-OUT, bill gate UNPAID|VERIFIED, Patient.type->DECEASED, approver attribution from REAL principal per CR-INC05-03 not creator-copy), save_referral_plan (OPD: SIGNED-OUT + wipe insurance to CASH/null, bill gate UNPAID-only; inpatient: admission STOPPED), get_referral_summary (->APPROVED, OPD type->OUTPATIENT). load_deceased_list/load_referral_list hide ARCHIVED. Green: death + referral state machines; bill-gate asymmetry preserved (CR-INC05-09 default); separate entities not a polymorphic closure. (V29 audit DDL DEFERRED — not in this chunk.)
driftCorrections: Consultation status names: planning doc BOOKED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED — REJECTED. Legacy reality: PENDING, IN-PROCESS (hyphen), TRANSFERED (single-R), CANCELED (single-L), SIGNED-OUT, HELD (Consultation.java:55-56; HELD@5753). No BOOKED, no IN_PROGRESS, no COMPLETED on Consultation. STOPPED is a query-only ghost (@328), NOT a writable Consultation status — EXCLUDED from the CHECK.

Polymorphic ClinicalOrder(kind=LAB_TEST|RADIOLOGY|PROCEDURE) — REJECTED. Legacy has THREE separate entities/tables LabTest/Radiology/Procedure + Prescription as a fourth (LabTest.java:43, Radiology.java:42, Procedure.java:40, Prescription.java:38), with divergent state machines.
OrderResult entity — REJECTED (invented). Results are columns on each order: LabTest.result/level/range/unit/report, Radiology.result/report/attachment, Procedure.note.

Single ConsultationDiagnosis(kind=WORKING|FINAL) — REJECTED. Legacy has two physically separate tables working_diagnoses + final_diagnoses (byte-for-byte twins; WorkingDiagnosis.java:28-65, FinalDiagnosis.java:31-68). No kind discriminator.
ProviderProfile entity — REJECTED (does not exist). The doctor is the Clinician personnel extension (loose clinician_user_uid ref), modelled in iam per inc-01.

Procedure APPROVED state + approve step (planning-doc M14) + PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE privilege — REJECTED as fabricated. Procedure has NO approved* columns and NO approve endpoint; only finalize is add_note->VERIFIED (Procedure.java:40-147; @3408-3414).

Prescription lifecycle PENDING->ACCEPTED->HELD->VERIFIED->APPROVED->SOLD|REJECTED|CANCELLED — REJECTED for Prescription. That is the PharmacySaleOrderDetail (walk-in sales) lifecycle. Legacy Prescription is EXACTLY {NOT-GIVEN, GIVEN} (Prescription.java:50). Initial status is NOT-GIVEN, NOT PENDING.
Prescription payStatus UNPAID|PAID — REJECTED. Prescription has NO payStatus field; payment is carried on its PatientBill. payStatus belongs to PharmacySaleOrderDetail.
Prescription FK links to Dosage/AdministrationRoute/DosingFrequency — REJECTED. dosage/frequency/route/days are plain free-text Strings (Prescription.java:42-45); the only mandatory FK is Medicine.

Privilege codes CONSULTATION_START / ORDER_RAISE / PROCEDURE_ORDER_APPROVE / PRESCRIPTION_CREATE — REJECTED (zero @PreAuthorize sites, zero seed rows). Real codes: PATIENT-ALL/CREATE/UPDATE (consultation lifecycle) + ADMIN-ACCESS (diagnosis_types/save).
'177 privilege codes' — REJECTED. Real inventory is 35 codes (V2:26-61); '177' is the count of @PreAuthorize SITES, most of which are commented out on clinical endpoints anyway.
CONS{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} / ORD{yyyyMMdd}-{seq} document numbering — REJECTED. No clinical entity has a human-facing number (IDENTITY id + ULID uid only; grep negative).
closure() method with kind=DECEASED|REFERRAL — REJECTED. Death and referral are two SEPARATE workflows/entities (DeceasedNote vs ReferralPlan); no closure() method, no kind discriminator.
Patient.deceased boolean — REJECTED. Death is recorded by the String Patient.type='DECEASED' (Patient.java:63-64; @5901,5920). No boolean field exists.
PATIENT_IS_DECEASED booking-block error — REJECTED (does not exist). A DECEASED patient is blocked only incidentally by the OUTPATIENT-type requirement with a generic message.

Settlement gate at order accept/verify — NOT legacy. Legacy gates pay-before-service at the OPEN transition + worklist filter only; accept/verify do not re-check the bill. Default build reproduces this; per-transition gate is CR-INC05-01.

Clinic-clinician affiliation check at transfer accept — REJECTED (NOT FOUND). doConsultation validates only cn.isActive(); no affiliation check. Affiliation is owned by iam (loose clinicUids @ElementCollection); enforcing it would be net-new.

Server-side BMI/BSA computation + numeric vitals + range validation — REJECTED for default build (vitals are free-text Strings, no computation; GeneralExamination.java:42-51). Numeric vitals is a separate future CR (CR-INC05-13 REJECT default).
ICD-10 diagnosis coding — REJECTED (zero ICD in legacy). DiagnosisType.code is a free-text unique String; an ICD dimension is a likely future MOH/NHIF requirement but is net-new, not exact-process.
Hibernate Envers / @Audited audit baseline — REJECTED as a thing-to-port (zero @Audited across com.orbix.api, two independent confirmations). Audit is greenfield (CR-INC05-03), nothing to migrate.
Device-fingerprint / device-binding — REJECTED (zero fingerprint/deviceId/X-Device across com.orbix.api). No device-binding requirement for inc-05.

ClinicianPerformance as a clinical aggregate — DRIFT-CAUTION: created as a side-effect of open (@889-893) but ownership (iam vs reporting vs clinical) is undecided; EXCLUDED from inc-05 clinical migration pending solution-architect ruling (open decision), not built in this increment.
openDecisionsForHuman: CR-INC05-02 — Whether the modernized build adds real method-level RBAC privileges to the clinical lifecycle endpoints (open/transfer/order-lifecycle/diagnosis/notes/worklists) and especially to DEATH RECORDING (save_deceased_note/get_deceased_summary), which legacy leaves authentication-only. HDE treats fully-open death/diagnosis endpoints as a BLOCKER pending this decision. Requires security-architect design + engagement-lead approval; planning-doc privilege names must not be invented. Default until decided = authenticated-only parity.

CR-INC05-01 — Whether to add a hard pay-before-service gate at order accept/verify transitions (net-new) or faithfully reproduce the legacy gap where accept/verify do not re-check the bill (settlement enforced only at open + worklist). Patient-safety/financial scope decision for the engagement owner.

CR-INC05-10 — Whether to introduce an explicit deceased-patient booking guard. HDE warns an irreversible deceased-lock is hazardous without an approved, audited reversal path (mis-recorded deaths/MRN mix-ups would permanently lock a living patient out of care). Requires clinical-governance + engagement-lead sign-off.

CR-INC05-09 — Whether to harmonise the closure bill-gate inconsistency (death/inpatient reject UNPAID|VERIFIED vs OPD-referral rejects UNPAID-only). Affects which NHIF/insured patients can close an encounter when insurer-pending (VERIFIED) bills are outstanding. Finance/NHIF-compliance decision; default preserves both gates exactly.

CR-INC05-06 — Per-endpoint contract decision for the side-effecting GET loaders (auto-create note/exam on GET; approve-on-GET for deceased/referral summaries). Preserve faithfully vs convert to POST/PUT with explicit semantics — needs engagement-lead + frontend-engineer agreement; changing verbs alters observable behaviour for existing clients.

Allergy-check gate at prescribing — HDE-identified safety gap: ClinicalNote.drugsAndAllergyHistory exists but nothing in the prescribing path reads it; there is no drug-allergy contraindication check anywhere. Adding one is NET-NEW beyond exact-process and must be a deliberate, approved change request to engagement-lead/clinical governance — not silently implemented. Flagged in the edge-case register as a known safety gap.

Canonical billing invoice-number generator — the legacy PatientInvoice.no = Math.random()-then-id pattern is broken and an NHIF-submission risk; the billing context must supply the real generator (the same context that owns requestPatientCreditNoteNo) and guarantee the transient random value never persists. Confirm the generator with billing/data-architect before any NHIF-submission path consumes invoice numbers.

ClinicianPerformance ownership — iam vs reporting vs clinical. Created as a side-effect of open_consultation/open_follow_up; EXCLUDED from inc-05 clinical migration pending solution-architect ruling on the owning module. Confirm ownership before it is built so the side-effect is wired into the correct context.

Registration+clinical physical-schema co-location — whether consultations.patient_id/visit_id remain REAL FKs (default, matching the V19 stub which already FKs consultations->patients/visits, same schema) or must become loose patient_uid/visit_uid if registration and clinical are separated into distinct physical schemas. Confirm the schema topology so the FK strategy is correct.

STOPPED / Procedure-REJECTED / admission-procedure-worklist (CR-INC05-15) ambiguities — whether STOPPED is ever a Consultation status (only found on Admission), how a Procedure reaches REJECTED with no reject endpoint, and whether admission-scoped procedures should appear in get_procedures_by_patient_id. Need healthcare-domain-expert confirmation before modelling/adding; default = reproduce-legacy (exclude STOPPED, no reject_procedure, admission procedures invisible).
deferred: V29 audit DDL (append-only audit_log + per-PHI-entity audit tables) — DEFERRED pending security-architect's PHI field classification + audit-coverage requirements. The covered-entity set is NOT self-selected; the requirement (CR-INC05-03) is ratified but the schema authoring is gated. Legacy has no Envers/@Audited, so nothing to migrate.

Inpatient / Nursing context — admission lifecycle, ward-bed management, admission-scoped notes/orders/diagnosis append semantics. The admissions table is NOT built in this codebase; all 12 clinical tables carry a LOOSE admission_uid (no FK) and stay loose under ADR-0008. Admission force-close of consultations (PatientServiceImpl.java:1956), admission diagnosis duplicate-asymmetry, and the inpatient discharge/referral-STOPPED paths are reproduced at the clinical edge but the Admission aggregate itself is a future increment.

Pharmacy dispense / store-side PharmacySaleOrderDetail walk-in sales lifecycle (PENDING->ACCEPTED->HELD->VERIFIED->APPROVED->SOLD) — a DIFFERENT entity from the clinician Prescription; out of scope for inc-05 (the clinical prescription is only NOT-GIVEN/GIVEN). issueMedicine consumes pharmacy stock but the Pharmacy/store inventory context is separate.
Lab-machine / analyzer interface — no automated result ingestion in legacy; results are entered manually at verify. No machine-interface in scope.

Insurance/Claims submission workflow — confirmed ABSENT in legacy (M22). Inc-05 reproduces only per-service insurance PRICING (ConsultationInsurancePlan / LabTestInsurancePlan / RadiologyInsurancePlan / ProcedureInsurancePlan / MedicineInsurancePlan covered-flag repricing). Any claims-submission story is NEW SCOPE requiring a separate change request, never written as legacy reproduction.

ExternalMedicalProvider master (referral.external_medical_providers) — referral target table not yet built; referral_plans.external_medical_provider_uid is a loose uid. The provider-master CRUD belongs to a referral/masterdata increment.
ClinicianPerformance aggregate — deferred pending ownership ruling (iam/reporting/clinical); created as a side-effect of open but not built in inc-05.

Day-rollover archival sweep (UpdatePatient.java:572-600, the 48h DeceasedNote/ReferralPlan->ARCHIVED batch with empty catch-all, CR-INC05-11) — belongs to the business-day/day-management bounded context, not the inc-05 clinical write paths. Deferred to that increment.

Numeric/validated vitals migration (CR-INC05-13, REJECTED for default build) — deferred as a separate future CR with backfill/reconciliation if the engagement owner wants typed vitals; default preserves free-text Strings.

Reporting/analytics over clinical data (fast/slow-moving drugs by prescriptions.approved_at, clinician performance reports) — the Reporting bounded context; inc-05 provides the source data and indexes but the report services are deferred.
